<summary>220 lines</summary>

````
1. # Chat Rendering V2 — Design Document
2. 
3. ## Problem Statement
4. 
5. ### Current Issues
6. 
7. 1. **O(N²) streaming bug**: Each chunk appends duplicate entries to chatEntries (fixed in ea6aa8f but architecture still fragile)
8. 2. **No virtual scrolling**: `ChatEntryList.Render()` traverses ALL entries every frame — 1000 messages = 1000 renders per frame
9. 3. **Tool calls grouped poorly**: Activity groups with 📦 headers, max 5 items visible, hidden count summary — hard to follow individual tool progress
10. 4. **Todos/Tasks/Subagents rendered with old-style text**: Plain text lists, no structured display, no collapse/expand
11. 5. **Daemon follow mode uses separate rendering code**: `TerminalFollowDisplay` in `internal/daemon/follow.go` has its own ANSI formatting, completely different from TUI
12. 6. **IM tool formatting is separate again**: `tool_format.go` (1354 lines) duplicates formatting logic
13. 7. **Dual-write migration layer still present**: `m.output` (bytes.Buffer) and `chatEntries` (ChatEntryList) both maintained
14. 
15. ### Goals
16. 
17. - **Performance**: Virtual scrolling — only render visible items
18. - **Consistency**: Same rendering code for TUI, daemon follow, and IM
19. - **Clarity**: Each tool call is its own item with clear header/body/status
20. - **Modularity**: Per-tool-type renderers (like crush's `ToolRenderer` interface)
21. - **Maintainability**: Remove dual-write, single source of truth for rendering
22. 
23. ## Architecture
24. 
25. ### Reference Projects
26. 
27. | Aspect | Crush | Claude Code |
28. |--------|-------|-------------|
29. | Message model | Interface hierarchy: `list.Item` → `MessageItem` → `ToolMessageItem` | React components per message type |
30. | Virtual scroll | Custom `List` with offsetIdx/offsetLine | `VirtualMessageList` with `useVirtualScroll` |
31. | Tool rendering | `ToolRenderer` interface, each tool has its own file | Per-tool React components + `renderGroupedToolUse` |
32. | Tool status | Enum: pending/running/success/error/canceled | Per-tool-state: queued/waiting/resolved/errored |
33. | Tool output | Truncate to 10 lines, expandable | Truncate + Ctrl+O to expand |
34. | Todo/Task | `TodosToolMessageItem` with ratio + body list | `TaskListV2` with priority ordering + fade-out |
35. | Agent/Subagent | `AgentToolMessageItem` with nested tools in tree | `CoordinatorAgentStatus` + team colors |
36. | Style system | Central `styles.Styles` struct | Theme system with `ThemedText` |
37. | Focus/Highlight | `Focusable`/`Highlightable` interfaces | Click-to-select with background highlight |
38. 
39. ### Our Design
40. 
41. ```
42. ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
43. │                     chat/item.go                            │
44. │  type Item interface {                                       │
45. │      Render(width int) string                               │
46. │      ID() string                                            │
47. │      Height(width int) int                                  │
48. │  }                                                          │
49. ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
50. │                     chat/list.go                            │
51. │  type List struct {                                         │
52. │      items []Item                                           │
53. │      offsetIdx, offsetLine int                              │
54. │      width, height int                                      │
55. │  }                                                          │
56. │  // Virtual scroll: only renders visible items              │
57. ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
58. │  chat/user.go       - User messages (markdown + prefix)     │
59. │  chat/assistant.go  - Assistant text (markdown streaming)    │
60. │  chat/tool.go       - Base tool item (header + body + status)│
61. │  chat/bash.go       - Bash tool renderer                    │
62. │  chat/file.go       - Read/Write/Edit tool renderer          │
63. │  chat/search.go     - Grep/Glob/LS tool renderer             │
64. │  chat/fetch.go      - Fetch/WebSearch tool renderer          │
65. │  chat/todos.go      - Todo/Task list renderer                │
66. │  chat/agent.go      - Subagent renderer (nested tools)       │
67. │  chat/mcp.go        - MCP tool renderer                      │
68. │  chat/system.go     - System/status messages                 │
69. │  chat/spacer.go     - Vertical spacing between messages      │
70. ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
71. │                     chat/styles.go                          │
72. │  // Centralized style definitions                           │
73. │  type Styles struct { ... }                                 │
74. ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
75. │  chat/tool_header.go - Shared tool header rendering          │
76. │  chat/tool_body.go   - Shared body rendering + truncation    │
77. └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
78. ```
79. 
80. ### Item Interface
81. 
82. ```go
83. // chat/item.go
84. type Item interface {
85.     // Render produces the ANSI string for this item at the given width.
86.     Render(width int) string
87.     
88.     // ID returns a unique identifier for deduplication and scrolling.
89.     ID() string
90.     
91.     // Height returns the number of visual lines at the given width.
92.     // Used by the virtual list to calculate scroll positions without rendering.
93.     Height(width int) int
94. }
95. 
96. // CachedItem provides common caching for items that are expensive to render.
97. type CachedItem struct {
98.     rendered    string
99.     cachedWidth int
100.     cachedHeight int
101. }
102. 
103. func (c *CachedItem) GetCached(width int) (string, int, bool) { ... }
104. func (c *CachedItem) SetCache(rendered string, width, height int) { ... }
105. func (c *CachedItem) Invalidate() { ... }
106. ```
107. 
108. ### Tool Item Design
109. 
110. Each tool call becomes its own `Item` in the list:
111. 
112. ```
113. ✓ Bash   cd /project && go build ./...          ← header: icon + name + params
114.   [output truncated — 47 lines]                  ← body: truncated output
115.   ✗ error: undefined: Foo                        ← body: error if any
116. 
117. ✓ Edit    internal/tui/model.go                  ← header only for small changes
118.   - old line                                    ← diff body (optional)
119. 
120. ⏸ To-Do  2/5 · writing tests                     ← header: ratio + active task
121.   ✓ design architecture                         ← body: task list
122.   → writing tests
123.   ○ review PR
124. ```
125. 
126. Tool status icons:
127. - `⏳` pending (animation)
128. - `✓` success
129. - `✗` error
130. - `⊘` canceled
131. 
132. ### Todo/Task Rendering
133. 
134. Instead of our current grouped activity blocks:
135. 
136. ```
137. 📦 File Operations
138.   ✓ read config.yaml
139.   ✓ write main.go
140.   ⏳ editing test.go
141.     ... and 3 more steps
142. ```
143. 
144. New design (per-call items, like crush):
145. 
146. ```
147. ✓ Read   config.yaml                    ← individual item
148. ✓ Write  main.go                        ← individual item  
149. ⏳ Edit   test.go                       ← individual item with status
150. 
151. ⏸ To-Do  3/7 · editing test.go          ← todo update shows full list
152.   ✓ design architecture
153.   ✓ implement core
154.   → editing test.go
155.   ○ write tests
156.   ○ review
157. ```
158. 
159. ### Subagent Rendering
160. 
161. Instead of our current flat text list, use crush-style nested tree:
162. 
163. ```
164. ✓ Agent  Task: implement auth module
165.   ├ ✓ Read   auth.go
166.   ├ ✓ Edit   auth.go
167.   └ ✓ Bash   go test ./auth/...
168.   Result: All tests passing
169. ```
170. 
171. ### Virtual List
172. 
173. ```go
174. // chat/list.go
175. type List struct {
176.     items     []Item
177.     offsetIdx  int    // index of first visible item
178.     offsetLine int    // line offset within first visible item
179.     width      int
180.     height     int
181.     follow     bool   // auto-scroll to bottom
182. }
183. 
184. func (l *List) Render() string {
185.     // Only render items in the visible window
186.     var lines []string
187.     needed := l.height
188.     idx := l.offsetIdx
189.     offset := l.offsetLine
190.     
191.     for needed > 0 && idx < len(l.items) {
192.         content := l.items[idx].Render(l.width)
193.         itemLines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
194.         // Take only visible portion
195.         visible := itemLines[offset:]
196.         if len(visible) > needed {
197.             visible = visible[:needed]
198.         }
199.         lines = append(lines, visible...)
200.         needed -= len(visible)
201.         idx++
202.         offset = 0
203.     }
204.     return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
205. }
206. ```
207. 
208. ### Unified Rendering Pipeline
209. 
210. All three display contexts share the same item types:
211. 
212. ```
213. TUI (internal/tui)          Daemon Follow         IM (internal/im)
214. ─────────────────          ──────────────        ────────────────
215. chat.List → Render()       chat.List → Render()  chat.FormatToolResult()
216.   └ UserItem                  └ UserItem           └ formatUserMessage()
217.   └ AssistantItem             └ AssistantItem      └ formatAssistantMessage()
218.   └ BashToolItem              └ BashToolItem       └ formatBashResult()
219.   └ EditToolItem              └ EditToolItem       └ formatEditResult()
220.   └ TodoToolItem              └ TodoToolItem       └ formatTodoResult()
````

</details>

150. 	Text  string
151. }
152. 
153. type agentInterruptMsg struct {
154. 	RunID int
155. 	Text  string
156. }
157. 
158. type knightTaskResultMsg struct {
159. 	Goal   string
160. 	Result knight.TaskResult
161. 	Err    error
162. }
163. 
164. type knightProjectProposalResultMsg struct {
165. 	Goal     string
166. 	Proposal knight.ProjectImprovementProposal
167. 	Result   knight.TaskResult
168. 	Err      error
169. }
170. 
171. // setProgramMsg is sent via program.Send so the model copy inside Bubble Tea's
172. // event loop gets the real *tea.Program reference (NewProgram copies the model).
173. type setProgramMsg struct {
174. 	Program *tea.Program
175. }
176. 
177. // inputDrainEndMsg signals the end of the startup input drain window.
178. type inputDrainEndMsg struct{}
179. 
180. type mcpServersMsg struct {
181. 	Servers []plugin.MCPServerInfo
182. }
183. 
184. type deviceCodeInfo struct {
185. 	serverName string
186. 	userCode   string
187. 	verifyURL  string
188. }
189. 
190. type mcpOAuthStartMsg struct {
191. 	serverName     string
192. 	authorizeURL   string
193. 	handler        *mcp.OAuthHandler
194. 	openErr        error
195. 	err            error
196. 	deviceUserCode string // set when using device flow
197. }
198. 
199. type mcpOAuthResultMsg struct {
200. 	serverName string
201. 	err        error
202. }
203. 
204. type updateCheckResultMsg struct {
205. 	Result update.CheckResult
206. 	Err    error
207. }
208. 
209. type updatePrepareResultMsg struct {
210. 	Prepared update.PreparedUpdate
211. 	Err      error
212. }
213. 
214. type updateCheckTickMsg struct{}
215. 
216. // gitBranchTickMsg is sent every 2 seconds to refresh the cached git branch
217. // shown in the sidebar, avoiding disk I/O on every View() render.
218. type gitBranchTickMsg struct{}
219. 
220. // imPanelRefreshMsg is sent every 2 seconds when an IM panel is open
221. // and the selected adapter is in a non-terminal state (pairing, connecting, etc.)
222. // This ensures dynamic content like WhatsApp QR codes appears without manual refresh.
223. type imPanelRefreshMsg struct{}
224. 
225. // webchatUserMsg is sent by the webui TUIChatBridge to inject a webchat
226. // message into the TUI event loop. The TUI handles it like a normal
227. // user input submission.
228. type webchatUserMsg struct {
229. 	Text string
230. }
231. 
232. // webuiReadyMsg is sent when the webui HTTP server is ready. The TUI
233. // displays the URL as a system message in the chat area.
234. type webuiReadyMsg struct {
235. 	Addr string
236. }
237. 
238. // harnessPanelRefreshResultMsg carries the result of an async harness panel
239. // data load. The handler applies the data to the harness panel state.
240. type harnessPanelRefreshResultMsg struct {
241. 	Err      string
242. 	Project  *harness.Project
243. 	Cfg      *harness.Config
244. 	Doctor   *harness.DoctorReport
245. 	Monitor  *harness.MonitorReport
246. 	Contexts *harness.ContextReport
247. 	Tasks    []*harness.Task
248. 	Inbox    *harness.OwnerInbox
249. 	Review   []*harness.Task
250. 	Promote  []*harness.Task
251. 	Release  *harness.ReleasePlan
252. 	Rollouts []*harness.ReleaseWavePlan
253. }
254. 
255. // knightStartupHintMsg is sent once at startup to show a Knight-related hint
256. // (e.g. another instance holds the lock) in the chat area.
257. type knightStartupHintMsg struct {
258. 	Hint string
259. }
260.
```

</details>

1. package tui

3. import (
4. 	"encoding/json"

13. // chatFinishAllRunningTools marks all running tool items in chatList as success.
14. // Called when a round ends (doneMsg) to finalize any tool items that weren't
15. // explicitly finished via chatFinishTool.
16. func (m *Model) chatFinishAllRunningTools() {
17. 	if m.chatList == nil {
18. 		return
19. 	}
20. 	type statusAccessor interface {
21. 		Status() chat.ToolStatus
22. 		SetStatus(chat.ToolStatus)
23. 	}
24. 	for i := 0; i < m.chatList.Len(); i++ {
25. 		item := m.chatList.ItemAt(i)
26. 		if item == nil {
27. 			continue
28. 		}
29. 		if sa, ok := item.(statusAccessor); ok && sa.Status() == chat.StatusRunning {
30. 			sa.SetStatus(chat.StatusSuccess)
31. 		}
32. 	}
33. }
34. 
35. // isLiveSubAgentStatus returns true for subagent statuses that indicate active work.
36. func isLiveSubAgentStatus(status subagent.Status) bool {
37. 	switch status {
38. 	case subagent.StatusPending, subagent.StatusRunning:
39. 		return true
40. 	default:
41. 		return false
42. 	}
43. }
44. 
45. func (m Model) subAgentActivitySummary(sa *subagent.SubAgent) string {
46. 	if summary := strings.TrimSpace(sa.ProgressSummary); summary != "" {
47. 		return summary
48. 	}
49. 	if sa.CurrentTool != "" {
50. 		present := describeTool(m.currentLanguage(), sa.CurrentTool, sa.CurrentArgs)
51. 		return firstNonEmpty(present.Activity, formatToolInline(present.DisplayName, present.Detail))
52. 	}
53. 	switch sa.CurrentPhase {
54. 	case "writing":
55. 		return m.t("status.writing")
56. 	case "thinking":
57. 		return m.t("status.thinking")
58. 	case "completed":
59. 		if m.currentLanguage() == LangZhCN {
60. 			return "已完成"
61. 		}
62. 		return "Completed"
63. 	case "failed":
64. 		if m.currentLanguage() == LangZhCN {
65. 			return "已失败"
66. 		}
67. 		return "Failed"
68. 	case "pending":
69. 		if m.currentLanguage() == LangZhCN {
70. 			return "等待开始"
71. 		}
72. 		return "Pending"
73. 	default:
74. 		return m.t("status.thinking")
75. 	}
76. }
77. 
78. func localizeSubAgentStatus(lang Language, status subagent.Status) string {
79. 	switch status {
80. 	case subagent.StatusPending:
81. 		if lang == LangZhCN {
82. 			return "等待中"
83. 		}
84. 		return "Pending"
85. 	case subagent.StatusRunning:
86. 		if lang == LangZhCN {
87. 			return "运行中"
88. 		}
89. 		return "Running"
90. 	case subagent.StatusCompleted:
91. 		if lang == LangZhCN {
92. 			return "已完成"
93. 		}
94. 		return "Completed"
95. 	case subagent.StatusFailed:
96. 		if lang == LangZhCN {
97. 			return "失败"
98. 		}
99. 		return "Failed"
100. 	case subagent.StatusCancelled:
101. 		if lang == LangZhCN {
102. 			return "已取消"
103. 		}
104. 		return "Cancelled"
105. 	default:
106. 		return string(status)
107. 	}
108. }
109. 
110. func isCommandTool(toolName string) bool {
111. 	switch toolName {
112. 	case "run_command", "bash", "powershell", "start_command", "write_command_input":
113. 		return true
114. 	default:
115. 		return false
116. 	}
117. }
118. 
119. func trimLeadingRenderedSpacing(rendered string) string {
120. 	return strings.TrimLeft(rendered, "\n\r ")
121. }
122. 
123. type todoStateItem struct {
124. 	ID        string    `json:"id"`
125. 	Content   string    `json:"content"`
126. 	Status    string    `json:"status"`
127. 	StartedAt time.Time `json:"-"`
128. 	UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"-"`
129. }
130. 
131. func (m *Model) applyTodoWrite(ts ToolStatusMsg) string {
132. 	todos, ok := parseTodoSnapshot(ts.RawArgs)
133. 	if !ok {
134. 		// Not a valid todo_write — return a generic summary.
135. 		present := describeTool(m.currentLanguage(), ts.ToolName, ts.RawArgs)
136. 		return formatToolInline(present.DisplayName, present.Detail)
137. 	}
138. 
139. 	previous := m.todoSnapshot
140. 	if previous == nil {
141. 		previous = map[string]todoStateItem{}
142. 	}
143. 	current := make(map[string]todoStateItem, len(todos))
144. 	changes := make([]string, 0, len(todos))
145. 	now := time.Now()
146. 
147. 	for _, td := range todos {
148. 		if prev, existed := previous[td.ID]; existed {
149. 			td.StartedAt = prev.StartedAt
150. 			td.UpdatedAt = prev.UpdatedAt
151. 		}
152. 		if td.UpdatedAt.IsZero() {
153. 			td.UpdatedAt = now
154. 		}
155. 		switch td.Status {
156. 		case "in_progress":
157. 			if td.StartedAt.IsZero() {
158. 				td.StartedAt = now
159. 			}
160. 			td.UpdatedAt = now
161. 		case "done", "blocked", "failed":
162. 			if td.StartedAt.IsZero() {
163. 				td.StartedAt = now
164. 			}
165. 		}
166. 		current[td.ID] = td
167. 		prev, existed := previous[td.ID]
168. 		switch {
169. 		case !existed && td.Status == "in_progress":
170. 			changes = append(changes, localizeTodoChange(m.currentLanguage(), "started", td))
171. 		case !existed && td.Status == "done":
172. 			changes = append(changes, localizeTodoChange(m.currentLanguage(), "completed", td))
173. 		case !existed:
174. 			changes = append(changes, localizeTodoChange(m.currentLanguage(), "added", td))
175. 		case prev.Status != td.Status && td.Status == "in_progress":
176. 			changes = append(changes, localizeTodoChange(m.currentLanguage(), "started", td))
177. 		case prev.Status != td.Status && td.Status == "done":
178. 			changes = append(changes, localizeTodoChange(m.currentLanguage(), "completed", td))
179. 		case prev.Status != td.Status:
180. 			changes = append(changes, localizeTodoChange(m.currentLanguage(), "updated", td))
181. 		}
182. 	}
183. 	for id, prev := range previous {
184. 		if _, exists := current[id]; !exists {
185. 			changes = append(changes, localizeTodoChange(m.currentLanguage(), "removed", prev))
186. 		}
187. 	}
188. 
189. 	m.todoSnapshot = current
190. 	m.todoOrder = make([]string, 0, len(todos))
191. 	for _, td := range todos {
192. 		m.todoOrder = append(m.todoOrder, td.ID)
193. 	}
194. 	m.activeTodo = nil
195. 	// Auto-show sidebar when task mode starts (first todo_write with active items).
196. 	// Auto-hide when all todos reach terminal state (done/failed/blocked) or are cleared.
197. 	if len(previous) == 0 && len(current) > 0 {
198. 		// Task mode just started — ensure sidebar is visible.
199. 		m.sidebarVisible = true
200. 	} else if len(current) > 0 {
201. 		allDone := true
202. 		for _, td := range current {
203. 			if td.Status != "done" && td.Status != "failed" && td.Status != "blocked" {
204. 				allDone = false
205. 				break
206. 			}
207. 		}
208. 		if allDone {
209. 			m.sidebarVisible = false
210. 		}
211. 	}
212. 	for _, td := range todos {
213. 		if td.Status == "in_progress" {
214. 			tdCopy := td
215. 			m.activeTodo = &tdCopy
216. 			break
217. 		}
218. 	}
219. 
220. 	if len(changes) == 0 {
221. 		if m.activeTodo != nil {
222. 			return localizeTodoFocus(m.currentLanguage(), m.activeTodo.Content)
223. 		}
224. 		if m.currentLanguage() == LangZhCN {
225. 			return "同步待办状态"
226. 		}
227. 		return "Synced todo state"
228. 	}
229. 	// Update TodoToolItem in chatList
230. 	m.chatUpdateTodoItem(todos)
231. 
232. 	return summarizeTodoChanges(m.currentLanguage(), changes)
233. }
234. 
235. func parseTodoSnapshot(rawArgs string) ([]todoStateItem, bool) {
236. 	if strings.TrimSpace(rawArgs) == "" {
237. 		return nil, false
238. 	}
239. 	var args struct {
240. 		Todos []todoStateItem `json:"todos"`
241. 	}
242. 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(rawArgs), &args); err != nil || len(args.Todos) == 0 {
243. 		return nil, false
244. 	}
245. 	return args.Todos, true
246. }
247. 
248. func summarizeTodoChanges(lang Language, changes []string) string {
249. 	if len(changes) == 0 {
250. 		return ""
251. 	}
252. 	if len(changes) == 1 {
253. 		return changes[0]
254. 	}
255. 	visible := changes
256. 	if len(visible) > 2 {
257. 		visible = visible[:2]
258. 	}
259. 	summary := strings.Join(visible, "; ")
260. 	if remaining := len(changes) - len(visible); remaining > 0 {
261. 		if lang == LangZhCN {
262. 			summary += fmt.Sprintf("；另有 %d 项", remaining)
263. 		} else {
264. 			summary += fmt.Sprintf("; %d more", remaining)
265. 		}
266. 	}
267. 	return summary
268. }
269. 
270. func localizeTodoChange(lang Language, kind string, td todoStateItem) string {
271. 	label := truncateString(compactSingleLine(td.Content), 48)
272. 	if label == "" {
273. 		label = td.ID
274. 	}
275. 	switch lang {
276. 	case LangZhCN:
277. 		switch kind {
278. 		case "started":
279. 			return "开始任务 " + label
280. 		case "completed":
281. 			return "完成任务 " + label
282. 		case "added":
283. 			return "新增待办 " + label
284. 		case "removed":
285. 			return "移除待办 " + label
286. 		default:
287. 			return "更新待办 " + label
288. 		}
289. 	default:
290. 		switch kind {
291. 		case "started":
292. 			return "Started " + label
293. 		case "completed":
294. 			return "Completed " + label
295. 		case "added":
296. 			return "Added " + label
297. 		case "removed":
298. 			return "Removed " + label
299. 		default:
300. 			return "Updated " + label
301. 		}
302. 	}
303. }
304. 
305. func localizeTodoHeading(lang Language, content string) string {
306. 	content = truncateString(compactSingleLine(content), 72)
307. 	if lang == LangZhCN {
308. 		return "任务: " + content
309. 	}
310. 	return "Todo: " + content
311. }
312. 
313. func localizeTodoFocus(lang Language, content string) string {
314. 	content = truncateString(compactSingleLine(content), 60)
315. 	if lang == LangZhCN {
316. 		return "当前任务 " + content
317. 	}
318. 	return "Working on " + content
319. }
320. 
321. func isSubAgentLifecycleTool(toolName string) bool {
322. 	switch toolName {
323. 	case "spawn_agent", "wait_agent", "list_agents":
324. 		return true
325. 	default:
326. 		return false
327. 	}
328. }
329.
```

</details>

1499. func TestRenderOutputShowsSubAgentAsIndependentState(t *testing.T) {
1500. 	t.Skip("TODO: subagent status rendering needs to be wired into chatList")
1501. 	m := newTestModel()
1502. 	m.handleResize(120, 40)
1503. 	m.loading = true
1504. 	m.subAgentMgr = subagent.NewManager(config.SubAgentConfig{})
1505. 	id := m.subAgentMgr.Spawn("context\n\nInvestigate parser behavior", "Investigate parser behavior", nil, context.Background())
1506. 	sa, ok := m.subAgentMgr.Get(id)
1507. 	if !ok {
1508. 		t.Fatal("expected spawned subagent")
1509. 	}
1510. 	sa.Status = subagent.StatusRunning
1511. 	sa.CurrentPhase = "tool"
1512. 	sa.CurrentTool = "read_file"
1513. 	sa.CurrentArgs = `{"path":"docs/spec.md"}`
1514. 	sa.ToolCallCount = 2
1515. 
1516. 	output := renderedOutput(&m)
1517. 
1518. 	if !strings.Contains(output, "🤖") || !strings.Contains(output, "Investigate parser behavior") {
1519. 		t.Fatalf("expected independent subagent state block, got %q", output)
1520. 	}
1521. 	if !strings.Contains(output, "Reading docs/spec.md") {
1522. 		t.Fatalf("expected friendly subagent activity summary, got %q", output)
1523. 	}
1524. 	// spawn_agent/wait_agent internal tool names must stay hidden,
1525. 	// but the /agent prefix + ID is intentionally shown as a user-visible label.
1526. 	if strings.Contains(output, "spawn_agent") || strings.Contains(output, "wait_agent") {
1527. 		t.Fatalf("expected subagent lifecycle internals to stay hidden, got %q", output)
1528. 	}
1529. }

889. func (t *TodoToolItem) Height(width int) int {
890. 	if _, h, ok := t.GetCached(width); ok {
891. 		return h
892. 	}
893. 	return measureHeight(t.Render(width))
894. }

896. // --- AgentToolItem ---
897. 
898. // AgentToolItem renders a subagent with nested tool calls.
899. type AgentToolItem struct {
900. 	CachedItem
901. 	id          string
902. 	task        string
903. 	status      ToolStatus
904. 	nestedItems []Item
905. 	result      string
906. 	styles      Styles
907. }
908. 
909. // NewAgentToolItem creates a new agent tool item.
910. func NewAgentToolItem(id, task string, status ToolStatus, styles Styles) *AgentToolItem {
911. 	return &AgentToolItem{
912. 		id:     id,
913. 		task:   task,
914. 		status: status,
915. 		styles: styles,
916. 	}
917. }
918. 
919. func (a *AgentToolItem) ID() string { return a.id }
920. 
921. // SetStatus updates the agent status.
922. func (a *AgentToolItem) SetStatus(s ToolStatus) {
923. 	a.status = s
924. 	a.Invalidate()
925. }
926. 
927. // SetResult updates the agent result.
928. func (a *AgentToolItem) SetResult(result string) {
929. 	a.result = result
930. 	a.Invalidate()
931. }
932. 
933. // AppendNested adds a nested tool item.
934. func (a *AgentToolItem) AppendNested(item Item) {
935. 	a.nestedItems = append(a.nestedItems, item)
936. 	a.Invalidate()
937. }
938. 
939. // UpdateNested updates a nested item by ID.
940. func (a *AgentToolItem) UpdateNested(id string, item Item) {
941. 	for i, it := range a.nestedItems {
942. 		if it.ID() == id {
943. 			a.nestedItems[i] = item
944. 			a.Invalidate()
945. 			return
946. 		}
947. 	}
948. 	a.nestedItems = append(a.nestedItems, item)
949. 	a.Invalidate()
950. }
951. 
952. func (a *AgentToolItem) Render(width int) string {
953. 	if cached, _, ok := a.GetCached(width); ok {
954. 		return cached
955. 	}
956. 
957. 	// Header
958. 	taskDisplay := a.task
959. 	if len(taskDisplay) > width-15 {
960. 		taskDisplay = taskDisplay[:width-16] + "…"
961. 	}
962. 	header := a.styles.ToolHeader(a.status, "Starting subagent", width, taskDisplay)
963. 
964. 	if len(a.nestedItems) == 0 {
965. 		rendered := header
966. 		a.SetCached(rendered, width, measureHeight(rendered))
967. 		return rendered
968. 	}
969. 
970. 	// Build tree with nested tool calls
971. 	innerWidth := width - 4
972. 	t := tree.Root(header)
973. 	for _, item := range a.nestedItems {
974. 		t.Child(item.Render(innerWidth))
975. 	}
976. 
977. 	// Style the tree with rounded enumerator
978. 	enumStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("241"))
979. 	t.EnumeratorStyle(enumStyle)
980. 
981. 	rendered := t.String()
982. 	a.SetCached(rendered, width, measureHeight(rendered))
983. 	return rendered
984. }
985. 
986. func (a *AgentToolItem) Height(width int) int {
987. 	if _, h, ok := a.GetCached(width); ok {
988. 		return h
989. 	}
990. 	return measureHeight(a.Render(width))
991. }
992.

994. func FormatJSONResult(raw string) string {
995. 	var data map[string]any
```

26. func (m *Model) resetConversationView() {

29. 	m.streamPrefixWritten = false

46. func (m *Model) listSessions() tea.Cmd {
47. 	return func() tea.Msg {
48. 		if m.sessionStore == nil {
49. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.store_missing"))
50. 		}
51. 		sessions, err := m.sessionStore.List()
52. 		if err != nil {
53. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.list_failed", err))
54. 		}
55. 		if len(sessions) == 0 {
56. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.none"))
57. 		}
58. 		var b strings.Builder
59. 		b.WriteString(m.t("session.list.title"))
60. 		for i, s := range sessions {
61. 			title := s.Title
62. 			if title == "" {
63. 				title = m.t("session.untitled")
64. 			}
65. 			updated := s.UpdatedAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
66. 			b.WriteString(m.t("session.list.item", i+1, s.ID, title, updated))
67. 		}
68. 		b.WriteString(m.t("session.list.hint"))
69. 		return streamMsg(b.String())
70. 	}
71. }
72. 
73. func (m *Model) resumeSession(id string) tea.Cmd {
74. 	return func() tea.Msg {
75. 		if m.sessionStore == nil {
76. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.store_missing"))
77. 		}
78. 		ses, err := m.sessionStore.Load(id)
79. 		if err != nil {
80. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.resume_failed", id, err))
81. 		}
82. 		// Restore messages into agent
83. 		for _, msg := range ses.Messages {
84. 			m.agent.AddMessage(msg)
85. 		}
86. 		m.SetSession(ses, m.sessionStore)
87. 		m.rebuildConversationFromMessages(ses.Messages)
88. 		m.restoreHistoryFromMessages(ses.Messages)
89. 		title := ses.Title
90. 		if title == "" {
91. 			title = m.t("session.untitled")
92. 		}
93. 		return streamMsg(m.t("session.resume", ses.ID, title, len(ses.Messages)))
94. 	}
95. }
96. 
97. func (m *Model) exportSession(id string) tea.Cmd {
98. 	return func() tea.Msg {
99. 		if m.sessionStore == nil {
100. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.store_missing"))
101. 		}
102. 		md, err := m.sessionStore.ExportMarkdown(id)
103. 		if err != nil {
104. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.export_failed", err))
105. 		}
106. 		filename := fmt.Sprintf("session-%s.md", id)
107. 		if err := os.WriteFile(filename, []byte(md), 0644); err != nil {
108. 			return streamMsg(m.t("session.write_failed", err))
109. 		}
110. 		return streamMsg(m.t("session.exported", id, filename))
111. 	}
112. }

115. 	pa := m.pendingApproval
116. 	m.pendingApproval = nil

118. 		return nil

<sub>⏱️ 266m 3s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

我已经把结论收敛好了：这事**当前架构可以做，而且不需要重做 subagent runner**；更合适的是基于现有 `Snapshot + subAgentUpdateMsg` 做一个“主内容区切换”的 follow mode，而不是再造一套实时事件总线。接下来我把这个判断和完整方案整理成设计文档放进 `docs/plans`。

---

<sub>⏱️ 266m 3s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/docs/plans/2026-05-09-subagent-follow-mode-design.md\n+# Subagent Follow Mode for TUI\n+\n+## Problem\n+\n+The TUI already shows the main agent conversation in the primary content panel, but sub-agents are still mostly observable through indirect surfaces: `spawn_agent` / `wait_agent` tool output, `/agents`, `/agent <id>`, and high-level activity/status updates. The requested UX is stronger:\n+\n+- when sub-agents are running, show a compact follow strip near the composer\n+- let the user jump into a specific running sub-agent's live output\n+- while following a sub-agent, replace the main conversation panel with that sub-agent's output\n+- while following, disable normal input until the user exits follow mode\n+- if the followed sub-agent finishes, automatically return to the main agent panel\n+\n+Scope decision for this design: **only running sub-agents are followable**. Completed sub-agents do not remain switchable in follow mode; they continue to be inspectable through existing `/agent <id>` and chat/tool history surfaces.\n+\n+## Review of the prior analysis\n+\n+The prior analysis is directionally right, but it overstates how much new plumbing is needed.\n+\n+### What it got right\n+\n+1. We do need a **panel-switching state** in the TUI model.\n+2. We do need **new rendering logic** for a sub-agent follow pane and a follow strip.\n+3. We do need **key handling changes** so the user can enter/exit follow mode.\n+4. We do need to think about **multiple concurrent sub-agents** and deterministic ordering.\n+\n+### What it missed or overstated\n+\n+1. **Sub-agent event capture already exists.**  \n+   `internal/subagent/runner.go` already records text/tool/result/error events into `SubAgent.events`, and `internal/subagent/manager.go` already exposes snapshots with those events.\n+\n+2. **The TUI already receives live sub-agent update notifications.**  \n+   `internal/tui/repl.go` wires `SetOnUpdate` / `SetOnComplete` to `program.Send(subAgentUpdateMsg{})`. The missing part is not \"push sub-agent events into TUI for the first time\", but \"materialize those existing snapshots into a follow view\".\n+\n+3. **We do not need a new cross-layer event bus for V1.**  \n+   `maxAgentEvents` is capped at 200, so rebuilding a follow view from a snapshot is bounded and cheap enough for a first implementation.\n+\n+4. **`Shift+1/2/3` needs terminal-specific care.**  \n+   In terminals, `Shift+1` is often delivered as the literal `!`, not as a rich modifier event. The UX can still present numbered shortcuts, but the implementation must decode terminal-safe key forms rather than assuming GUI-style key semantics.\n+\n+## Goals\n+\n+- Show active sub-agents as first-class live work in the TUI.\n+- Reuse the existing `chat.List` rendering style so followed sub-agent output feels like the main agent view.\n+- Keep follow mode bounded to active work only.\n+- Avoid invasive changes to sub-agent execution architecture.\n+- Preserve current `/agent` and `/agents` commands as the detailed/diagnostic fallback.\n+\n+## Non-goals\n+\n+- Persisting a separate follow-history UI for completed sub-agents.\n+- Building a generic multi-pane window manager in the TUI.\n+- Replacing `/agent <id>` with the new follow view.\n+- Streaming raw provider events directly into the TUI from background goroutines.\n+\n+## Design options\n+\n+### Option A — Snapshot-derived follow pane (recommended)\n+\n+Use the existing `subagent.Manager` as the source of truth. On each `subAgentUpdateMsg`, the model refreshes a derived follow state:\n+\n+- list of currently running sub-agents\n+- numbered shortcut slots\n+- a `chat.List` per running sub-agent, built from its latest snapshot\n+\n+When the user activates a slot, `renderConversationPanel()` shows that derived list instead of the main `chatList`.\n+\n+**Pros**\n+\n+- smallest architecture change\n+- leverages existing `Snapshot` + capped event buffer\n+- easy to reason about thread-safety\n+- keeps main/sub-agent rendering aligned by reusing `chat.List`\n+\n+**Cons**\n+\n+- rebuilds derived UI state instead of receiving richer incremental messages\n+- sub-agent rendering fidelity is limited to what is already stored in `Snapshot.Events`\n+\n+### Option B — Dedicated follow context panel\n+\n+Show sub-agent output in a modal/context panel below the main conversation instead of replacing the main pane.\n+\n+**Pros**\n+\n+- less disruption to the main conversation layout\n+- easier input handling\n+\n+**Cons**\n+\n+- does not match the requested \"cover the main content panel and follow scroll like the main agent\"\n+- wastes vertical space in a terminal\n+\n+### Option C — Full realtime sub-agent event bus\n+\n+Push structured sub-agent stream events into Bubble Tea as first-class messages, similar to the main agent stream path.\n+\n+**Pros**\n+\n+- highest long-term flexibility\n+- supports richer replay, per-event animation, and future nested interactions\n+\n+**Cons**\n+\n+- significantly larger change set\n+- duplicates logic already stored in `Snapshot.Events`\n+- not needed for the requested V1 behavior\n+\n+## Recommendation\n+\n+Implement **Option A**.\n+\n+The current architecture already gives us the hard parts: sub-agent lifecycle, bounded event history, snapshotting, and TUI wakeups. The missing piece is a derived follow-mode presentation layer, not a new execution model. This keeps the feature incremental and behavior-safe while still delivering the requested UX.\n+\n+## Proposed architecture\n+\n+### 1. Model state\n+\n+Add a dedicated follow state to `internal/tui/model.go`.\n+\n+```go\n+type subAgentFollowState struct {\n+    activeID string\n+    slots    []subAgentFollowSlot\n+    views    map[string]*chat.List\n+}\n+\n+type subAgentFollowSlot struct {\n+    AgentID      string\n+    Index        int\n+    Label        string\n+    StatusText   string\n+    ShortcutHint string\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`activeID == \"\"` means the main agent conversation is shown.  \n+`activeID != \"\"` means the conversation panel should render the followed sub-agent view.\n+\n+This state is derived from `subAgentMgr` and should be treated as a UI cache, not a second source of truth.\n+\n+### 2. Message wiring\n+\n+Change `subAgentUpdateMsg` from an empty struct to a targeted update:\n+\n+```go\n+type subAgentUpdateMsg struct {\n+    AgentID string\n+}\n+```\n+\n+In `internal/tui/repl.go`, keep the existing callback structure but send the sub-agent ID:\n+\n+```go\n+r.program.Send(subAgentUpdateMsg{AgentID: sa.ID})\n+```\n+\n+The message should carry only the ID, not the `*SubAgent` pointer, so the Bubble Tea loop always re-reads state through `subAgentMgr.Snapshot()` / `List()` and avoids pointer aliasing across goroutines.\n+\n+### 3. Derived snapshot sync\n+\n+Add a helper in a new file such as `internal/tui/subagent_follow.go`:\n+\n+```go\n+func (m *Model) syncSubAgentFollowState(updatedID string)\n+```\n+\n+This helper should:\n+\n+1. collect running sub-agents from `m.subAgentMgr.List()`\n+2. keep a stable ordering (recommended: `StartedAt`, then `CreatedAt`, then `ID`)\n+3. rebuild the slot list for at most 9 visible entries\n+4. refresh or create the derived `chat.List` for the updated agent\n+5. remove ended agents from the follow cache\n+6. if the active followed agent is no longer running, clear `activeID` and return to the main panel\n+\n+Because `maxAgentEvents` is already bounded at 200, rebuilding a sub-agent's derived `chat.List` from snapshot data is acceptable for V1.\n+\n+## Rendering design\n+\n+### 1. Main conversation panel switching\n+\n+Update `renderConversationPanel()` in `internal/tui/view.go`:\n+\n+- if `follow.activeID == \"\"`, render the existing `m.chatList`\n+- otherwise render the followed sub-agent's derived `chat.List`\n+\n+This is the key architectural point: **the followed sub-agent uses the same main content area**, not a side panel or context panel.\n+\n+### 2. Follow strip\n+\n+Add a new render helper, for example:\n+\n+```go\n+func (m Model) renderSubAgentFollowStrip() string\n+```\n+\n+Placement: directly above the composer input inside `renderComposerPanel()`.\n+\n+Example:\n+\n+```text\n+🤖  1 parser-investigation · Reading docs/spec.md   2 release-build · running tests   Esc close\n+```\n+\n+When one sub-agent is actively followed:\n+\n+```text\n+🤖  [1 parser-investigation · following]   2 release-build · running tests   Esc close\n+```\n+\n+The strip should:\n+\n+- only appear when there is at least one running sub-agent\n+- show short human-readable labels derived from `DisplayTask`\n+- show each sub-agent's latest summary from `ProgressSummary` / `CurrentTool` / `CurrentPhase`\n+- highlight the active slot\n+- show overflow as `+N more` if the running set exceeds visible shortcut slots\n+\n+### 3. Composer behavior while following\n+\n+When `follow.activeID != \"\"`:\n+\n+- the textarea should not accept normal input\n+- the composer box stays visible, but the input row becomes a disabled notice\n+\n+Example:\n+\n+```text\n+Following sub-agent sa-2 — input paused. Press Esc or the same shortcut to return.\n+```\n+\n+This preserves the layout and keeps the \"TUI is still alive\" feeling, while making it obvious why typing does nothing.\n+\n+## Rendering sub-agent output\n+\n+### Event-to-chat-item translation\n+\n+Each followed sub-agent view should be derived from `subagent.Snapshot.Events`.\n+\n+Recommended mapping:\n+\n+- `AgentEventText` -> assistant text item\n+- `AgentEventToolCall` -> running tool item\n+- `AgentEventToolResult` -> success/error tool completion item\n+- `AgentEventError` -> system/error item\n+\n+Add a helper such as:\n+\n+```go\n+func buildSubAgentFollowList(snap subagent.Snapshot, styles chat.Styles, width, height int) *chat.List\n+```\n+\n+This helper should reuse the existing `chat` package renderers instead of inventing a second rendering style. The sub-agent should feel like \"the same UI, different conversation source\".\n+\n+### Header treatment\n+\n+The follow list should begin with a small synthetic system item describing the sub-agent:\n+\n+- task label\n+- sub-agent ID\n+- current status\n+\n+That keeps the user oriented after switching into a different pane.\n+\n+### Scroll behavior\n+\n+Whenever the active followed sub-agent receives an update, its derived `chat.List` should scroll to bottom, matching the main agent's normal live-follow behavior.\n+\n+## Key handling and terminal compatibility\n+\n+### Why raw `Shift+1` is risky\n+\n+In many terminals:\n+\n+- `Shift+1` becomes `!`\n+- `Shift+2` becomes `@`\n+- `Shift+3` becomes `#`\n+\n+So the implementation must not depend on GUI-style modifier reporting.\n+\n+### V1 shortcut plan\n+\n+1. The UI presents **numbered follow slots**.\n+2. The handler accepts terminal-safe key forms for those slots (`!`, `@`, `#`, etc.).\n+3. `Esc` exits follow mode.\n+4. Pressing the currently active slot again toggles follow mode off.\n+\n+To avoid stealing normal typing too aggressively, slot entry should only trigger when:\n+\n+- there is at least one running sub-agent\n+- no modal/context panel is open\n+- autocomplete is not active\n+- the composer is empty\n+\n+Once follow mode is active, input is already disabled, so toggle handling is unambiguous.\n+\n+## Lifecycle behavior\n+\n+### Enter follow mode\n+\n+1. user presses a slot shortcut\n+2. model resolves slot -> sub-agent ID\n+3. `follow.activeID` is set\n+4. conversation panel switches to the sub-agent list\n+5. composer enters disabled/following state\n+\n+### Exit follow mode\n+\n+1. user presses `Esc` or the same slot shortcut\n+2. `follow.activeID` is cleared\n+3. conversation panel switches back to the main `chatList`\n+4. composer returns to normal input mode\n+\n+### Automatic return on completion\n+\n+If the active followed sub-agent transitions out of `pending/running`:\n+\n+1. remove it from running slots\n+2. clear `follow.activeID`\n+3. return to the main panel automatically\n+4. optionally emit one concise system note in the main chat, e.g. `Sub-agent sa-2 completed; returned to main view.`\n+\n+This matches the scope decision that completed sub-agents are not kept as follow targets.\n+\n+## Error handling\n+\n+- **Missing snapshot during refresh**: drop the slot and, if active, return to main view\n+- **Late update for an ended sub-agent**: ignore\n+- **More than 9 running sub-agents**: render first 9 shortcuts and a `+N more` indicator\n+- **Empty event list**: render a placeholder like `Waiting for first output…`\n+- **Manager absent**: hide the strip and behave exactly as today\n+\n+No silent failures should be introduced; unexpected transitions should at least hit `debug.Log`.\n+\n+## Files expected to change\n+\n+### Existing files\n+\n+- `internal/tui/model.go`\n+- `internal/tui/model_messages.go`\n+- `internal/tui/repl.go`\n+- `internal/tui/model_update.go`\n+- `internal/tui/view.go`\n+\n+### New file\n+\n+- `internal/tui/subagent_follow.go`\n+\n+### Likely tests\n+\n+- `internal/tui/layout_test.go`\n+- `internal/tui/tui_test.go`\n+- potentially `internal/tui/keyboard_interaction_test.go`\n+\n+## Test plan\n+\n+1. **Follow strip rendering**\n+   - hidden when no running sub-agents\n+   - shows stable slot order\n+   - highlights active slot\n+\n+2. **Pane switching**\n+   - main panel renders main chat by default\n+   - selecting a running slot replaces the panel with that sub-agent output\n+   - toggling off restores main chat\n+\n+3. **Input disabling**\n+   - composer renders disabled state while following\n+   - enter/send path does not submit input while following\n+\n+4. **Completion behavior**\n+   - followed sub-agent completion auto-returns to main panel\n+   - non-followed sub-agent completion does not disrupt the current view\n+\n+5. **Terminal-safe key handling**\n+   - `!/@/#...` map to slots as intended\n+   - slot shortcuts do not fire when autocomplete/modal panels are active\n+   - slot shortcuts do not steal input when the composer already contains draft text\n+\n+6. **Concurrent sub-agents**\n+   - multiple running sub-agents update independently\n+   - updates rebuild only the affected derived view\n+   - ended agents disappear from the strip without corrupting active state\n+\n+## Rollout notes\n+\n+This feature can be implemented incrementally:\n+\n+### Phase 1\n+\n+- add follow strip\n+- add follow-mode state\n+- switch conversation panel between main chat and a derived sub-agent chat list\n+- auto-return on completion\n+\n+### Phase 2\n+\n+- improve shortcut ergonomics if terminal feedback shows issues\n+- add richer synthetic header/footer items\n+- optionally allow an inspector handoff from follow mode into `/agent <id>` details\n+\n+## Final assessment\n+\n+The requested UX is **architecturally feasible with moderate TUI work**, but it does **not** require redesigning sub-agent execution. The current codebase already has the critical primitives:\n+\n+- bounded event capture in `subagent.Run`\n+- snapshot-based sub-agent state\n+- TUI wakeups on sub-agent updates\n+- reusable `chat.List` rendering infrastructure\n+\n+So the correct design is: **build a follow-mode presentation layer on top of existing snapshots, not a new streaming subsystem.**\n*** End Patch\n"
````

</details>

````
1. # Subagent Follow Mode for TUI
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. 
5. The TUI already shows the main agent conversation in the primary content panel, but sub-agents are still mostly observable through indirect surfaces: `spawn_agent` / `wait_agent` tool output, `/agents`, `/agent <id>`, and high-level activity/status updates. The requested UX is stronger:
6. 
7. - when sub-agents are running, show a compact follow strip near the composer
8. - let the user jump into a specific running sub-agent's live output
9. - while following a sub-agent, replace the main conversation panel with that sub-agent's output
10. - while following, disable normal input until the user exits follow mode
11. - if the followed sub-agent finishes, automatically return to the main agent panel
12. 
13. Scope decision for this design: **only running sub-agents are followable**. Completed sub-agents do not remain switchable in follow mode; they continue to be inspectable through existing `/agent <id>` and chat/tool history surfaces.
14. 
15. ## Review of the prior analysis
16. 
17. The prior analysis is directionally right, but it overstates how much new plumbing is needed.
18. 
19. ### What it got right
20. 
21. 1. We do need a **panel-switching state** in the TUI model.
22. 2. We do need **new rendering logic** for a sub-agent follow pane and a follow strip.
23. 3. We do need **key handling changes** so the user can enter/exit follow mode.
24. 4. We do need to think about **multiple concurrent sub-agents** and deterministic ordering.
25. 
26. ### What it missed or overstated
27. 
28. 1. **Sub-agent event capture already exists.**  
29.    `internal/subagent/runner.go` already records text/tool/result/error events into `SubAgent.events`, and `internal/subagent/manager.go` already exposes snapshots with those events.
30. 
31. 2. **The TUI already receives live sub-agent update notifications.**  
32.    `internal/tui/repl.go` wires `SetOnUpdate` / `SetOnComplete` to `program.Send(subAgentUpdateMsg{})`. The missing part is not "push sub-agent events into TUI for the first time", but "materialize those existing snapshots into a follow view".
33. 
34. 3. **We do not need a new cross-layer event bus for V1.**  
35.    `maxAgentEvents` is capped at 200, so rebuilding a follow view from a snapshot is bounded and cheap enough for a first implementation.
36. 
37. 4. **`Shift+1/2/3` needs terminal-specific care.**  
38.    In terminals, `Shift+1` is often delivered as the literal `!`, not as a rich modifier event. The UX can still present numbered shortcuts, but the implementation must decode terminal-safe key forms rather than assuming GUI-style key semantics.
39. 
40. ## Goals
41. 
42. - Show active sub-agents as first-class live work in the TUI.
43. - Reuse the existing `chat.List` rendering style so followed sub-agent output feels like the main agent view.
44. - Keep follow mode bounded to active work only.
45. - Avoid invasive changes to sub-agent execution architecture.
46. - Preserve current `/agent` and `/agents` commands as the detailed/diagnostic fallback.
47. 
48. ## Non-goals
49. 
50. - Persisting a separate follow-history UI for completed sub-agents.
51. - Building a generic multi-pane window manager in the TUI.
52. - Replacing `/agent <id>` with the new follow view.
53. - Streaming raw provider events directly into the TUI from background goroutines.
54. 
55. ## Design options
56. 
57. ### Option A — Snapshot-derived follow pane (recommended)
58. 
59. Use the existing `subagent.Manager` as the source of truth. On each `subAgentUpdateMsg`, the model refreshes a derived follow state:
60. 
61. - list of currently running sub-agents
62. - numbered shortcut slots
63. - a `chat.List` per running sub-agent, built from its latest snapshot
64. 
65. When the user activates a slot, `renderConversationPanel()` shows that derived list instead of the main `chatList`.
66. 
67. **Pros**
68. 
69. - smallest architecture change
70. - leverages existing `Snapshot` + capped event buffer
71. - easy to reason about thread-safety
72. - keeps main/sub-agent rendering aligned by reusing `chat.List`
73. 
74. **Cons**
75. 
76. - rebuilds derived UI state instead of receiving richer incremental messages
77. - sub-agent rendering fidelity is limited to what is already stored in `Snapshot.Events`
78. 
79. ### Option B — Dedicated follow context panel
80. 
81. Show sub-agent output in a modal/context panel below the main conversation instead of replacing the main pane.
82. 
83. **Pros**
84. 
85. - less disruption to the main conversation layout
86. - easier input handling
87. 
88. **Cons**
89. 
90. - does not match the requested "cover the main content panel and follow scroll like the main agent"
91. - wastes vertical space in a terminal
92. 
93. ### Option C — Full realtime sub-agent event bus
94. 
95. Push structured sub-agent stream events into Bubble Tea as first-class messages, similar to the main agent stream path.
96. 
97. **Pros**
98. 
99. - highest long-term flexibility
100. - supports richer replay, per-event animation, and future nested interactions
101. 
102. **Cons**
103. 
104. - significantly larger change set
105. - duplicates logic already stored in `Snapshot.Events`
106. - not needed for the requested V1 behavior
107. 
108. ## Recommendation
109. 
110. Implement **Option A**.
111. 
112. The current architecture already gives us the hard parts: sub-agent lifecycle, bounded event history, snapshotting, and TUI wakeups. The missing piece is a derived follow-mode presentation layer, not a new execution model. This keeps the feature incremental and behavior-safe while still delivering the requested UX.
113. 
114. ## Proposed architecture
115. 
116. ### 1. Model state
117. 
118. Add a dedicated follow state to `internal/tui/model.go`.
119. 
120. ```go
121. type subAgentFollowState struct {
122.     activeID string
123.     slots    []subAgentFollowSlot
124.     views    map[string]*chat.List
125. }
126. 
127. type subAgentFollowSlot struct {
128.     AgentID      string
129.     Index        int
130.     Label        string
131.     StatusText   string
132.     ShortcutHint string
133. }
134. ```
135. 
136. `activeID == ""` means the main agent conversation is shown.  
137. `activeID != ""` means the conversation panel should render the followed sub-agent view.
138. 
139. This state is derived from `subAgentMgr` and should be treated as a UI cache, not a second source of truth.
140. 
141. ### 2. Message wiring
142. 
143. Change `subAgentUpdateMsg` from an empty struct to a targeted update:
144. 
145. ```go
146. type subAgentUpdateMsg struct {
147.     AgentID string
148. }
149. ```
150. 
151. In `internal/tui/repl.go`, keep the existing callback structure but send the sub-agent ID:
152. 
153. ```go
154. r.program.Send(subAgentUpdateMsg{AgentID: sa.ID})
155. ```
156. 
157. The message should carry only the ID, not the `*SubAgent` pointer, so the Bubble Tea loop always re-reads state through `subAgentMgr.Snapshot()` / `List()` and avoids pointer aliasing across goroutines.
158. 
159. ### 3. Derived snapshot sync
160. 
161. Add a helper in a new file such as `internal/tui/subagent_follow.go`:
162. 
163. ```go
164. func (m *Model) syncSubAgentFollowState(updatedID string)
165. ```
166. 
167. This helper should:
168. 
169. 1. collect running sub-agents from `m.subAgentMgr.List()`
170. 2. keep a stable ordering (recommended: `StartedAt`, then `CreatedAt`, then `ID`)
171. 3. rebuild the slot list for at most 9 visible entries
172. 4. refresh or create the derived `chat.List` for the updated agent
173. 5. remove ended agents from the follow cache
174. 6. if the active followed agent is no longer running, clear `activeID` and return to the main panel
175. 
176. Because `maxAgentEvents` is already bounded at 200, rebuilding a sub-agent's derived `chat.List` from snapshot data is acceptable for V1.
177. 
178. ## Rendering design
179. 
180. ### 1. Main conversation panel switching
181. 
182. Update `renderConversationPanel()` in `internal/tui/view.go`:
183. 
184. - if `follow.activeID == ""`, render the existing `m.chatList`
185. - otherwise render the followed sub-agent's derived `chat.List`
186. 
187. This is the key architectural point: **the followed sub-agent uses the same main content area**, not a side panel or context panel.
188. 
189. ### 2. Follow strip
190. 
191. Add a new render helper, for example:
192. 
193. ```go
194. func (m Model) renderSubAgentFollowStrip() string
195. ```
196. 
197. Placement: directly above the composer input inside `renderComposerPanel()`.
198. 
199. Example:
200. 
201. ```text
202. 🤖  1 parser-investigation · Reading docs/spec.md   2 release-build · running tests   Esc close
203. ```
204. 
205. When one sub-agent is actively followed:
206. 
207. ```text
208. 🤖  [1 parser-investigation · following]   2 release-build · running tests   Esc close
209. ```
210. 
211. The strip should:
212. 
213. - only appear when there is at least one running sub-agent
214. - show short human-readable labels derived from `DisplayTask`
215. - show each sub-agent's latest summary from `ProgressSummary` / `CurrentTool` / `CurrentPhase`
216. - highlight the active slot
217. - show overflow as `+N more` if the running set exceeds visible shortcut slots
218. 
219. ### 3. Composer behavior while following
220.
````

</details>

### 💬 Copilot

**结论：这份分析方向是对的，但不完全准确。** 我确认后，最大的两处修正是：

1. **subagent 的实时事件采集其实已经有了**：`internal/subagent/runner.go` 已经把 text/tool/result/error 记进 `SubAgent.events`，`Manager` 也已经能给出 `Snapshot`。缺的不是重新把 `onEvent` 暴露到 TUI，而是把这些现有 snapshot 渲染成一个可切换的 follow 视图。
2. **`Shift+1/2/3` 不能按 GUI 快捷键思路直接设计**：终端里通常收到的是 `!/@/#` 这类字符，不是稳定的“shift+数字”修饰键事件，所以设计里需要把它当成“编号槽位 + 终端安全按键映射”来处理。

我已经把完整设计写到这里了：

`docs/plans/2026-05-09-subagent-follow-mode-design.md`

这份设计的推荐方案是：**基于现有 `subAgentUpdateMsg + Snapshot.Events` 做主内容区切换式 follow mode**，而不是重做 subagent 执行架构。文档里已经包含：
- 对原分析的校正
- 3 个方案对比和推荐方案
- Model / message / rendering / key handling 的具体设计
- 自动退出 follow、输入禁用、并发 subagent、终端兼容性处理
- 预期修改文件和测试计划

1. # Subagent Follow Mode for TUI
2. 
3. ## Problem
4. 
5. The TUI already shows the main agent conversation in the primary content panel, but sub-agents are still mostly observable through indirect surfaces: `spawn_agent` / `wait_agent` tool output, `/agents`, `/agent <id>`, and high-level activity/status updates. The requested UX is stronger:
6. 
7. - when sub-agents are running, show a compact follow strip near the composer
8. - let the user jump into a specific running sub-agent's live output
9. - while following a sub-agent, replace the main conversation panel with that sub-agent's output
10. - while following, disable normal input until the user exits follow mode
11. - if the followed sub-agent finishes, automatically return to the main agent panel
12. 
13. Scope decision for this design: **only running sub-agents are followable**. Completed sub-agents do not remain switchable in follow mode; they continue to be inspectable through existing `/agent <id>` and chat/tool history surfaces.
14. 
15. ## Review of the prior analysis
16. 
17. The prior analysis is directionally right, but it overstates how much new plumbing is needed.
18. 
19. ### What it got right
20. 
21. 1. We do need a **panel-switching state** in the TUI model.
22. 2. We do need **new rendering logic** for a sub-agent follow pane and a follow strip.
23. 3. We do need **key handling changes** so the user can enter/exit follow mode.
24. 4. We do need to think about **multiple concurrent sub-agents** and deterministic ordering.
25. 
26. ### What it missed or overstated
27. 
28. 1. **Sub-agent event capture already exists.**  
29.    `internal/subagent/runner.go` already records text/tool/result/error events into `SubAgent.events`, and `internal/subagent/manager.go` already exposes snapshots with those events.
30. 
31. 2. **The TUI already receives live sub-agent update notifications.**  
32.    `internal/tui/repl.go` wires `SetOnUpdate` / `SetOnComplete` to `program.Send(subAgentUpdateMsg{})`. The missing part is not "push sub-agent events into TUI for the first time", but "materialize those existing snapshots into a follow view".
33. 
34. 3. **We do not need a new cross-layer event bus for V1.**  
35.    `maxAgentEvents` is capped at 200, so rebuilding a follow view from a snapshot is bounded and cheap enough for a first implementation.
36. 
37. 4. **`Shift+1/2/3` needs terminal-specific care.**  
38.    In terminals, `Shift+1` is often delivered as the literal `!`, not as a rich modifier event. The UX can still present numbered shortcuts, but the implementation must decode terminal-safe key forms rather than assuming GUI-style key semantics.
39. 
40. ## Goals
41. 
42. - Show active sub-agents as first-class live work in the TUI.
43. - Reuse the existing `chat.List` rendering style so followed sub-agent output feels like the main agent view.
44. - Keep follow mode bounded to active work only.
45. - Avoid invasive changes to sub-agent execution architecture.
46. - Preserve current `/agent` and `/agents` commands as the detailed/diagnostic fallback.
47. 
48. ## Non-goals
49. 
50. - Persisting a separate follow-history UI for completed sub-agents.
51. - Building a generic multi-pane window manager in the TUI.
52. - Replacing `/agent <id>` with the new follow view.
53. - Streaming raw provider events directly into the TUI from background goroutines.
54. 
55. ## Design options
56. 
57. ### Option A — Snapshot-derived follow pane (recommended)
58. 
59. Use the existing `subagent.Manager` as the source of truth. On each `subAgentUpdateMsg`, the model refreshes a derived follow state:
60. 
61. - list of currently running sub-agents
62. - numbered shortcut slots
63. - a `chat.List` per running sub-agent, built from its latest snapshot
64. 
65. When the user activates a slot, `renderConversationPanel()` shows that derived list instead of the main `chatList`.
66. 
67. **Pros**
68. 
69. - smallest architecture change
70. - leverages existing `Snapshot` + capped event buffer
71. - easy to reason about thread-safety
72. - keeps main/sub-agent rendering aligned by reusing `chat.List`
73. 
74. **Cons**
75. 
76. - rebuilds derived UI state instead of receiving richer incremental messages
77. - sub-agent rendering fidelity is limited to what is already stored in `Snapshot.Events`
78. 
79. ### Option B — Dedicated follow context panel
80. 
81. Show sub-agent output in a modal/context panel below the main conversation instead of replacing the main pane.
82. 
83. **Pros**
84. 
85. - less disruption to the main conversation layout
86. - easier input handling
87. 
88. **Cons**
89. 
90. - does not match the requested "cover the main content panel and follow scroll like the main agent"
91. - wastes vertical space in a terminal
92. 
93. ### Option C — Full realtime sub-agent event bus
94. 
95. Push structured sub-agent stream events into Bubble Tea as first-class messages, similar to the main agent stream path.
96. 
97. **Pros**
98. 
99. - highest long-term flexibility
100. - supports richer replay, per-event animation, and future nested interactions
101. 
102. **Cons**
103. 
104. - significantly larger change set
105. - duplicates logic already stored in `Snapshot.Events`
106. - not needed for the requested V1 behavior
107. 
108. ## Recommendation
109. 
110. Implement **Option A**.
111. 
112. The current architecture already gives us the hard parts: sub-agent lifecycle, bounded event history, snapshotting, and TUI wakeups. The missing piece is a derived follow-mode presentation layer, not a new execution model. This keeps the feature incremental and behavior-safe while still delivering the requested UX.
113. 
114. ## Proposed architecture
115. 
116. ### 1. Model state
117. 
118. Add a dedicated follow state to `internal/tui/model.go`.
119. 
120. ```go
121. type subAgentFollowState struct {
122.     activeID string
123.     slots    []subAgentFollowSlot
124.     views    map[string]*chat.List
125. }
126. 
127. type subAgentFollowSlot struct {
128.     AgentID      string
129.     Index        int
130.     Label        string
131.     StatusText   string
132.     ShortcutHint string
133. }
134. ```
135. 
136. `activeID == ""` means the main agent conversation is shown.  
137. `activeID != ""` means the conversation panel should render the followed sub-agent view.
138. 
139. This state is derived from `subAgentMgr` and should be treated as a UI cache, not a second source of truth.
140. 
141. ### 2. Message wiring
142. 
143. Change `subAgentUpdateMsg` from an empty struct to a targeted update:
144. 
145. ```go
146. type subAgentUpdateMsg struct {
147.     AgentID string
148. }
149. ```
150. 
151. In `internal/tui/repl.go`, keep the existing callback structure but send the sub-agent ID:
152. 
153. ```go
154. r.program.Send(subAgentUpdateMsg{AgentID: sa.ID})
155. ```
156. 
157. The message should carry only the ID, not the `*SubAgent` pointer, so the Bubble Tea loop always re-reads state through `subAgentMgr.Snapshot()` / `List()` and avoids pointer aliasing across goroutines.
158. 
159. ### 3. Derived snapshot sync
160. 
161. Add a helper in a new file such as `internal/tui/subagent_follow.go`:
162. 
163. ```go
164. func (m *Model) syncSubAgentFollowState(updatedID string)
165. ```
166. 
167. This helper should:
168. 
169. 1. collect running sub-agents from `m.subAgentMgr.List()`
170. 2. keep a stable ordering (recommended: `StartedAt`, then `CreatedAt`, then `ID`)
171. 3. rebuild the slot list for at most 9 visible entries
172. 4. refresh or create the derived `chat.List` for the updated agent
173. 5. remove ended agents from the follow cache
174. 6. if the active followed agent is no longer running, clear `activeID` and return to the main panel
175. 
176. Because `maxAgentEvents` is already bounded at 200, rebuilding a sub-agent's derived `chat.List` from snapshot data is acceptable for V1.
177. 
178. ## Rendering design
179. 
180. ### 1. Main conversation panel switching
181. 
182. Update `renderConversationPanel()` in `internal/tui/view.go`:
183. 
184. - if `follow.activeID == ""`, render the existing `m.chatList`
185. - otherwise render the followed sub-agent's derived `chat.List`
186. 
187. This is the key architectural point: **the followed sub-agent uses the same main content area**, not a side panel or context panel.
188. 
189. ### 2. Follow strip
190. 
191. Add a new render helper, for example:
192. 
193. ```go
194. func (m Model) renderSubAgentFollowStrip() string
195. ```
196. 
197. Placement: directly above the composer input inside `renderComposerPanel()`.
198. 
199. Example:
200. 
201. ```text
202. 🤖  1 parser-investigation · Reading docs/spec.md   2 release-build · running tests   Esc close
203. ```
204. 
205. When one sub-agent is actively followed:
206. 
207. ```text
208. 🤖  [1 parser-investigation · following]   2 release-build · running tests   Esc close
209. ```
210. 
211. The strip should:
212. 
213. - only appear when there is at least one running sub-agent
214. - show short human-readable labels derived from `DisplayTask`
215. - show each sub-agent's latest summary from `ProgressSummary` / `CurrentTool` / `CurrentPhase`
216. - highlight the active slot
217. - show overflow as `+N more` if the running set exceeds visible shortcut slots
218. 
219. ### 3. Composer behavior while following
220. 
221. When `follow.activeID != ""`:
222. 
223. - the textarea should not accept normal input
224. - the composer box stays visible, but the input row becomes a disabled notice
225. 
226. Example:
227. 
228. ```text
229. Following sub-agent sa-2 — input paused. Press Esc or the same shortcut to return.
230. ```
231. 
232. This preserves the layout and keeps the "TUI is still alive" feeling, while making it obvious why typing does nothing.
233. 
234. ## Rendering sub-agent output
235. 
236. ### Event-to-chat-item translation
237. 
238. Each followed sub-agent view should be derived from `subagent.Snapshot.Events`.
239. 
240. Recommended mapping:
241. 
242. - `AgentEventText` -> assistant text item
243. - `AgentEventToolCall` -> running tool item
244. - `AgentEventToolResult` -> success/error tool completion item
245. - `AgentEventError` -> system/error item
246. 
247. Add a helper such as:
248. 
249. ```go
250. func buildSubAgentFollowList(snap subagent.Snapshot, styles chat.Styles, width, height int) *chat.List
251. ```
252. 
253. This helper should reuse the existing `chat` package renderers instead of inventing a second rendering style. The sub-agent should feel like "the same UI, different conversation source".
254. 
255. ### Header treatment
256. 
257. The follow list should begin with a small synthetic system item describing the sub-agent:
258. 
259. - task label
260. - sub-agent ID
261. - current status
262. 
263. That keeps the user oriented after switching into a different pane.
264. 
265. ### Scroll behavior
266. 
267. Whenever the active followed sub-agent receives an update, its derived `chat.List` should scroll to bottom, matching the main agent's normal live-follow behavior.
268. 
269. ## Key handling and terminal compatibility
270. 
271. ### Why raw `Shift+1` is risky
272. 
273. In many terminals:
274. 
275. - `Shift+1` becomes `!`
276. - `Shift+2` becomes `@`
277. - `Shift+3` becomes `#`
278. 
279. So the implementation must not depend on GUI-style modifier reporting.
280. 
281. ### V1 shortcut plan
282. 
283. 1. The UI presents **numbered follow slots**.
284. 2. The handler accepts terminal-safe key forms for those slots (`!`, `@`, `#`, etc.).
285. 3. `Esc` exits follow mode.
286. 4. Pressing the currently active slot again toggles follow mode off.
287. 
288. To avoid stealing normal typing too aggressively, slot entry should only trigger when:
289. 
290. - there is at least one running sub-agent
291. - no modal/context panel is open
292. - autocomplete is not active
293. - the composer is empty
294. 
295. Once follow mode is active, input is already disabled, so toggle handling is unambiguous.
296. 
297. ## Lifecycle behavior
298. 
299. ### Enter follow mode
300. 
301. 1. user presses a slot shortcut
302. 2. model resolves slot -> sub-agent ID
303. 3. `follow.activeID` is set
304. 4. conversation panel switches to the sub-agent list
305. 5. composer enters disabled/following state
306. 
307. ### Exit follow mode
308. 
309. 1. user presses `Esc` or the same slot shortcut
310. 2. `follow.activeID` is cleared
311. 3. conversation panel switches back to the main `chatList`
312. 4. composer returns to normal input mode
313. 
314. ### Automatic return on completion
315. 
316. If the active followed sub-agent transitions out of `pending/running`:
317. 
318. 1. remove it from running slots
319. 2. clear `follow.activeID`
320. 3. return to the main panel automatically
321. 4. optionally emit one concise system note in the main chat, e.g. `Sub-agent sa-2 completed; returned to main view.`
322. 
323. This matches the scope decision that completed sub-agents are not kept as follow targets.
324. 
325. ## Error handling
326. 
327. - **Missing snapshot during refresh**: drop the slot and, if active, return to main view
328. - **Late update for an ended sub-agent**: ignore
329. - **More than 9 running sub-agents**: render first 9 shortcuts and a `+N more` indicator
330. - **Empty event list**: render a placeholder like `Waiting for first output…`
331. - **Manager absent**: hide the strip and behave exactly as today
332. 
333. No silent failures should be introduced; unexpected transitions should at least hit `debug.Log`.
334. 
335. ## Files expected to change
336. 
337. ### Existing files
338. 
339. - `internal/tui/model.go`
340. - `internal/tui/model_messages.go`
341. - `internal/tui/repl.go`
342. - `internal/tui/model_update.go`
343. - `internal/tui/view.go`
344. 
345. ### New file
346. 
347. - `internal/tui/subagent_follow.go`
348. 
349. ### Likely tests
350. 
351. - `internal/tui/layout_test.go`
352. - `internal/tui/tui_test.go`
353. - potentially `internal/tui/keyboard_interaction_test.go`
354. 
355. ## Test plan
356. 
357. 1. **Follow strip rendering**
358.    - hidden when no running sub-agents
359.    - shows stable slot order
360.    - highlights active slot
361. 
362. 2. **Pane switching**
363.    - main panel renders main chat by default
364.    - selecting a running slot replaces the panel with that sub-agent output
365.    - toggling off restores main chat
366. 
367. 3. **Input disabling**
368.    - composer renders disabled state while following
369.    - enter/send path does not submit input while following
370. 
371. 4. **Completion behavior**
372.    - followed sub-agent completion auto-returns to main panel
373.    - non-followed sub-agent completion does not disrupt the current view
374. 
375. 5. **Terminal-safe key handling**
376.    - `!/@/#...` map to slots as intended
377.    - slot shortcuts do not fire when autocomplete/modal panels are active
378.    - slot shortcuts do not steal input when the composer already contains draft text
379. 
380. 6. **Concurrent sub-agents**
381.    - multiple running sub-agents update independently
382.    - updates rebuild only the affected derived view
383.    - ended agents disappear from the strip without corrupting active state
384. 
385. ## Rollout notes
386. 
387. This feature can be implemented incrementally:
388. 
389. ### Phase 1
390. 
391. - add follow strip
392. - add follow-mode state
393. - switch conversation panel between main chat and a derived sub-agent chat list
394. - auto-return on completion
395. 
396. ### Phase 2
397. 
398. - improve shortcut ergonomics if terminal feedback shows issues
399. - add richer synthetic header/footer items
400. - optionally allow an inspector handoff from follow mode into `/agent <id>` details
401. 
402. ## Final assessment
403. 
404. The requested UX is **architecturally feasible with moderate TUI work**, but it does **not** require redesigning sub-agent execution. The current codebase already has the critical primitives:
405. 
406. - bounded event capture in `subagent.Run`
407. - snapshot-based sub-agent state
408. - TUI wakeups on sub-agent updates
409. - reusable `chat.List` rendering infrastructure
410. 
411. So the correct design is: **build a follow-mode presentation layer on top of existing snapshots, not a new streaming subsystem.**
412.
````

</details>

12. // AgentEventType identifies the kind of event recorded during sub-agent execution.
13. type AgentEventType int
14. 
15. const (
16. 	AgentEventText       AgentEventType = iota // LLM text output
17. 	AgentEventToolCall                         // tool invocation started
18. 	AgentEventToolResult                       // tool execution result
19. 	AgentEventError                            // error encountered
20. )
21. 
22. // AgentEvent is a single recorded event from a sub-agent's execution.
23. type AgentEvent struct {
24. 	Type     AgentEventType
25. 	Text     string // AgentEventText / AgentEventError
26. 	ToolName string // AgentEventToolCall / AgentEventToolResult
27. 	ToolArgs string // AgentEventToolCall
28. 	Result   string // AgentEventToolResult
29. 	IsError  bool   // AgentEventToolResult / AgentEventError
30. }
31. 
32. const maxAgentEvents = 200
33. 
34. // Status represents the lifecycle state of a sub-agent.
35. type Status string
36. 
37. const (
38. 	StatusPending   Status = "pending"
39. 	StatusRunning   Status = "running"
40. 	StatusCompleted Status = "completed"
41. 	StatusFailed    Status = "failed"
42. 	StatusCancelled Status = "cancelled"
43. )
44. 
45. // AgentMessage represents a message sent to a sub-agent.
46. type AgentMessage struct {
47. 	From    string
48. 	Message string
49. 	Summary string
50. }
51. 
52. // SubAgent represents a spawned child agent.
53. type SubAgent struct {
54. 	ID              string
55. 	Task            string
56. 	DisplayTask     string
57. 	Tools           []string
58. 	ToolCallCount   int
59. 	Status          Status
60. 	CurrentPhase    string
61. 	CurrentTool     string
62. 	CurrentArgs     string
63. 	ProgressSummary string
64. 	Result          string
65. 	Error           error
66. 	CreatedAt       time.Time
67. 	StartedAt       time.Time
68. 	EndedAt         time.Time
69. 	Mailbox         chan AgentMessage
70. 	events          []AgentEvent
71. 	cancel          context.CancelFunc
72. 	mu              sync.Mutex
73. }
74. 
75. type Snapshot struct {
76. 	ID              string
77. 	Task            string
78. 	DisplayTask     string
79. 	Tools           []string
80. 	ToolCallCount   int
81. 	Status          Status
82. 	CurrentPhase    string
83. 	CurrentTool     string
84. 	CurrentArgs     string
85. 	ProgressSummary string
86. 	Result          string
87. 	Error           string
88. 	CreatedAt       time.Time
89. 	StartedAt       time.Time
90. 	EndedAt         time.Time
91. 	Events          []AgentEvent
92. }
93. 
94. // RecordEvent appends an event to the sub-agent's event log.
95. // This is the exported version for external callers (e.g., tests).
96. func (s *SubAgent) RecordEvent(ev AgentEvent) {
97. 	s.appendEvent(ev)
98. }
99. 
100. func (s *SubAgent) appendEvent(ev AgentEvent) {
101. 	s.mu.Lock()
102. 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
103. 	if len(s.events) >= maxAgentEvents {
104. 		s.events = s.events[1:]
105. 	}
106. 	s.events = append(s.events, ev)
107. }
108. 
109. // Events returns a copy of the recorded events.
110. func (s *SubAgent) Events() []AgentEvent {
111. 	s.mu.Lock()
112. 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
113. 	out := make([]AgentEvent, len(s.events))
114. 	copy(out, s.events)
115. 	return out
116. }
117.

119. 	s.mu.Lock()
120. 	defer s.mu.Unlock()

95. 	// Run the agentic loop, capturing output
96. 	var output strings.Builder
97. 	lastToolName := ""
98. 	err := subAgent.RunStream(subCtx, cfg.Task, func(event provider.StreamEvent) {
99. 		switch event.Type {
100. 		case provider.StreamEventText:
101. 			output.WriteString(event.Text)
102. 			if sa, ok := cfg.Manager.Get(cfg.SubAgentID); ok {
103. 				sa.setActivity("writing", "", "")
104. 				sa.appendEvent(AgentEvent{Type: AgentEventText, Text: truncateStr(event.Text, 500)})
105. 			}
106. 			cfg.Manager.Notify(cfg.SubAgentID)
107. 		case provider.StreamEventToolCallDone:
108. 			// Increment tool call count for this subagent
109. 			if sa, ok := cfg.Manager.Get(cfg.SubAgentID); ok {
110. 				sa.IncrementToolCalls()
111. 				sa.setActivity("tool", event.Tool.Name, string(event.Tool.Arguments))
112. 				sa.appendEvent(AgentEvent{
113. 					Type:     AgentEventToolCall,
114. 					ToolName: event.Tool.Name,
115. 					ToolArgs: truncateStr(string(event.Tool.Arguments), 300),
116. 				})
117. 			}
118. 			lastToolName = event.Tool.Name
119. 			cfg.Manager.Notify(cfg.SubAgentID)
120. 		case provider.StreamEventToolResult:
121. 			if summary := subagentToolProgressSummary(lastToolName, event.Result); summary != "" {
122. 				cfg.Manager.UpdateProgress(cfg.SubAgentID, summary)
123. 			} else {
124. 				cfg.Manager.Notify(cfg.SubAgentID)
125. 			}
126. 			if sa, ok := cfg.Manager.Get(cfg.SubAgentID); ok {
127. 				sa.appendEvent(AgentEvent{
128. 					Type:     AgentEventToolResult,
129. 					ToolName: lastToolName,
130. 					Result:   truncateStr(event.Result, 500),
131. 					IsError:  event.IsError,
132. 				})
133. 			}
134. 		case provider.StreamEventError:
135. 			output.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("[error: %v]\n", event.Error))
136. 			if sa, ok := cfg.Manager.Get(cfg.SubAgentID); ok {
137. 				sa.setActivity("failed", "", "")
138. 				sa.appendEvent(AgentEvent{
139. 					Type:    AgentEventError,
140. 					Text:    fmt.Sprintf("%v", event.Error),
141. 					IsError: true,
142. 				})
143. 			}
144. 			cfg.Manager.Notify(cfg.SubAgentID)
145. 		}
```

897. 
898. // AgentToolItem renders a subagent with nested tool calls.
899. type AgentToolItem struct {
900. 	CachedItem
901. 	id          string
902. 	task        string

911. 	return &AgentToolItem{

918.

920.

922. func (a *AgentToolItem) SetStatus(s ToolStatus) {

925. }
926. 
927. // SetResult updates the agent result.
928. func (a *AgentToolItem) SetResult(result string) {
929. 	a.result = result
930. 	a.Invalidate()
931. }
932. 
933. // AppendNested adds a nested tool item.
934. func (a *AgentToolItem) AppendNested(item Item) {
935. 	a.nestedItems = append(a.nestedItems, item)
936. 	a.Invalidate()
937. }
938. 
939. // UpdateNested updates a nested item by ID.
940. func (a *AgentToolItem) UpdateNested(id string, item Item) {
941. 	for i, it := range a.nestedItems {
942. 		if it.ID() == id {
943. 			a.nestedItems[i] = item
944. 			a.Invalidate()
945. 			return
946. 		}
947. 	}
948. 	a.nestedItems = append(a.nestedItems, item)
949. 	a.Invalidate()
950. }
951. 
952. func (a *AgentToolItem) Render(width int) string {
953. 	if cached, _, ok := a.GetCached(width); ok {
954. 		return cached
955. 	}
956. 
957. 	// Header
958. 	taskDisplay := a.task
959. 	if len(taskDisplay) > width-15 {
960. 		taskDisplay = taskDisplay[:width-16] + "…"
961. 	}
962. 	header := a.styles.ToolHeader(a.status, "Starting subagent", width, taskDisplay)
963. 
964. 	if len(a.nestedItems) == 0 {
965. 		rendered := header
966. 		a.SetCached(rendered, width, measureHeight(rendered))
967. 		return rendered
968. 	}
969. 
970. 	// Build tree with nested tool calls
971. 	innerWidth := width - 4
972. 	t := tree.Root(header)
973. 	for _, item := range a.nestedItems {
974. 		t.Child(item.Render(innerWidth))
975. 	}
976. 
977. 	// Style the tree with rounded enumerator
978. 	enumStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("241"))
979. 	t.EnumeratorStyle(enumStyle)
980. 
981. 	rendered := t.String()
982. 	a.SetCached(rendered, width, measureHeight(rendered))
983. 	return rendered
984. }
985.
```

</details>

/Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/docs/plans/2026-05-09-subagent-follow-mode-design.md (lines 26-260)
