37. 		},
38. 		"replace_all": {
39. 			"type": "boolean",
40. 			"description": "Replace all occurrences of old_text (default false)"
41. 		},
42. 		"description": {
43. 			"type": "string",
44. 			"description": "REQUIRED. Brief activity label shown in the UI. Write in the user's language (e.g. 'Searching for TODO patterns', '检查构建配置'). You MUST always provide this field."
45. 		}
46. 	},
47. 	"required": [
48. 		"file_path",
49. 		"old_text",
50. 		"new_text",
51. 		"description"
52. 	]
53. }`)
54. }
55. 
56. func (t EditFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
57. 	var args struct {
58. 		FilePath   string `json:"file_path"`
59. 		OldText    string `json:"old_text"`
60. 		NewText    string `json:"new_text"`
61. 		ReplaceAll bool   `json:"replace_all"`
62. 	}
63. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
64. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
65. 	}
66. 
67. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.FilePath) {
68. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
69. 	}
70. 
71. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
72. 	if err != nil {
73. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
74. 	}
75. 
76. 	content := string(data)
77. 
78. 	// Try exact match first.
79. 	oldText := args.OldText
80. 	if !strings.Contains(content, oldText) {
81. 		// Exact match failed — try whitespace normalization.
82. 		// This handles the common case where the LLM uses spaces for indentation
83. 		// but the file uses tabs (or vice versa), so it succeeds on the first
84. 		// attempt without wasting an LLM round-trip.
85. 		normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, oldText)
86. 		if normalized != oldText && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
87. 			oldText = normalized
88. 		} else {
89. 			hint := diagnoseMatchFailure(content, args.OldText)
90. 			msg := "old_text not found in file"
91. 			if hint != "" {
92. 				msg += ". " + hint
93. 			}
94. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
95. 		}
96. 	}
97. 
98. 	count := strings.Count(content, oldText)
99. 	if !args.ReplaceAll && count > 1 {
100. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("old_text found %d times in file — must be unique (use replace_all to replace all occurrences)", count)}, nil
101. 	}
102. 
103. 	// When we used a normalized match, also normalize new_text indentation
104. 	// so the replacement preserves the file's indentation style.
105. 	newText := args.NewText
106. 	if oldText != args.OldText {
107. 		newText = normalizeIndentation(content, args.NewText)
108. 	}
109. 
110. 	var newContent string
111. 	if args.ReplaceAll {
112. 		newContent = strings.ReplaceAll(content, oldText, newText)
113. 	} else {
114. 		newContent = strings.Replace(content, oldText, newText, 1)
115. 	}
116. 
117. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(newContent), 0644); err != nil {
118. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
119. 	}
120. 
121. 	// Build a summary
122. 	oldLines := strings.Count(args.OldText, "\n") + 1
123. 	newLines := strings.Count(args.NewText, "\n") + 1
124. 	if args.ReplaceAll {
125. 		return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Replaced %d occurrence(s) in %s: %d lines -> %d lines", count, args.FilePath, oldLines, newLines)}, nil
126. 	}
127. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Replaced 1 occurrence in %s: %d lines -> %d lines", args.FilePath, oldLines, newLines)}, nil
128. }
129. 
130. // normalizeIndentation converts the indentation of text to match the file's style.
131. // If the file uses tabs and text uses spaces, leading spaces are converted to tabs.
132. // If the file uses spaces and text uses tabs, leading tabs are converted to spaces.
133. // The width parameter is inferred from the file content.
134. func normalizeIndentation(fileContent, text string) string {
135. 	fileUsesTabs := false
136. 	fileTabWidth := 0
137. 	{
138. 		tabLines, spaceLines := 0, 0
139. 		spaceWidths := map[int]int{}
140. 		lines := strings.Split(fileContent, "\n")
141. 		limit := len(lines)
142. 		if limit > 200 {
143. 			limit = 200
144. 		}
145. 		for _, line := range lines[:limit] {
146. 			if len(line) == 0 {
147. 				continue
148. 			}
149. 			if line[0] == '\t' {
150. 				tabLines++
151. 			} else if line[0] == ' ' {
152. 				spaceLines++
153. 				n := 0
154. 				for n < len(line) && line[n] == ' ' {
155. 					n++
156. 				}
157. 				if n >= 2 {
158. 					spaceWidths[n]++
159. 				}
160. 			}
161. 		}
162. 		fileUsesTabs = tabLines > spaceLines
163. 		if len(spaceWidths) > 0 {
164. 			allW := make([]int, 0, len(spaceWidths))
165. 			for w := range spaceWidths {
166. 				allW = append(allW, w)
167. 			}
168. 			g := allW[0]
169. 			for _, w := range allW[1:] {
170. 				g = gcd(g, w)
171. 			}
172. 			if g < 2 {
173. 				g = 2
174. 			}
175. 			fileTabWidth = g
176. 		}
177. 		if fileTabWidth == 0 {
178. 			fileTabWidth = 4
179. 		}
180. 	}
181. 
182. 	// Check what the text uses
183. 	textHasTabs := false
184. 	textHasLeadingSpaces := false
185. 	for _, line := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
186. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == '\t' {
187. 			textHasTabs = true
188. 		}
189. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == ' ' {
190. 			textHasLeadingSpaces = true
191. 		}
192. 	}
193. 
194. 	// File uses tabs, text uses spaces → convert leading spaces to tabs
195. 	if fileUsesTabs && !textHasTabs && textHasLeadingSpaces {
196. 		return convertSpacesToTabs(text, fileTabWidth)
197. 	}
198. 
199. 	// File uses spaces, text uses tabs → convert leading tabs to spaces
200. 	if !fileUsesTabs && textHasTabs {
201. 		return convertTabsToSpaces(text, fileTabWidth)
202. 	}
203. 
204. 	return text
205. }
206. 
207. // convertSpacesToTabs converts leading spaces to tabs based on tabWidth.
208. // For example, with tabWidth=4:
209. //
210. //	"    foo" → "\tfoo"
211. //	"        bar" → "\t\tbar"
212. //	"  baz" → "\tbaz"  (non-multiple: consume one tab for any leading spaces)
213. func convertSpacesToTabs(text string, tabWidth int) string {
214. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
215. 	for i, line := range lines {
216. 		if len(line) == 0 {
217. 			continue
218. 		}
219. 		// Count leading spaces
220. 		spaces := 0
221. 		for spaces < len(line) && line[spaces] == ' ' {
222. 			spaces++
223. 		}
224. 		if spaces == 0 {
225. 			continue
226. 		}
227. 		tabs := spaces / tabWidth
228. 		remainder := spaces % tabWidth
229. 		// Use at least 1 tab if there are any leading spaces
230. 		if tabs == 0 && spaces > 0 {
231. 			tabs = 1
232. 			remainder = 0
233. 		}
234. 		lines[i] = strings.Repeat("\t", tabs) + strings.Repeat(" ", remainder) + line[spaces:]
235. 	}
236. 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
237. }
238. 
239. // convertTabsToSpaces converts leading tabs to spaces based on tabWidth.
240. func convertTabsToSpaces(text string, tabWidth int) string {
241. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
242. 	for i, line := range lines {
243. 		if len(line) == 0 {
244. 			continue
245. 		}
246. 		tabs := 0
247. 		for tabs < len(line) && line[tabs] == '\t' {
248. 			tabs++
249. 		}
250. 		if tabs == 0 {
251. 			continue
252. 		}
253. 		rest := line[tabs:]
254. 		lines[i] = strings.Repeat(" ", tabs*tabWidth) + rest
255. 	}
256. 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
257. }
258. 
259. // diagnoseMatchFailure provides actionable hints when old_text is not found.
260. // It checks for common causes: tab vs space, trailing whitespace, line ending differences.
261. func diagnoseMatchFailure(content, oldText string) string {
262. 	oldHasTabs := strings.Contains(oldText, "\t")
263. 	oldHasLeadingSpaces := false
264. 	for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") {
265. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == ' ' {
266. 			oldHasLeadingSpaces = true
267. 			break
268. 		}
269. 	}
270. 
271. 	fileHasTabs := false
272. 	fileIndentSpaces := 0
273. 	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
274. 	limit := len(lines)
275. 	if limit > 100 {
276. 		limit = 100
277. 	}
278. 	for _, line := range lines[:limit] {
279. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == '\t' {
280. 			fileHasTabs = true
281. 		}
282. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == ' ' {
283. 			fileIndentSpaces++
284. 		}
285. 	}
286. 
287. 	var hints []string
288. 
289. 	if fileHasTabs && !oldHasTabs && oldHasLeadingSpaces {
290. 		hints = append(hints, "file uses tab indentation — use \\t in old_text")
291. 		firstOldLine := strings.Split(oldText, "\n")[0]
292. 		trimmedOld := strings.TrimLeft(firstOldLine, " \t")
293. 		if trimmedOld != "" {
294. 			for _, line := range lines {
295. 				trimmed := strings.TrimLeft(line, " \t")
296. 				if trimmed == trimmedOld {
297. 					visual := visualizeWhitespace(line)
298. 					hints = append(hints, fmt.Sprintf("expected line: %s", visual))
299. 					break
300. 				}
301. 			}
302. 		}
303. 	} else if !fileHasTabs && oldHasTabs && fileIndentSpaces > 0 {
304. 		hints = append(hints, "file uses space indentation — remove \\t from old_text")
305. 	}
306. 
307. 	// Check for CRLF vs LF
308. 	if strings.Contains(content, "\r\n") && !strings.Contains(oldText, "\r\n") {
309. 		hints = append(hints, "file uses CRLF line endings — re-read the file to get exact content")
310. 	}
311. 
312. 	// Check if old_text is close to something in the file (first line matches partially)
313. 	if len(hints) == 0 {
314. 		firstOldLine := strings.Split(oldText, "\n")[0]
315. 		firstOldLine = strings.TrimSpace(firstOldLine)
316. 		if firstOldLine != "" {
317. 			for _, line := range lines {
318. 				trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
319. 				if trimmed == firstOldLine {
320. 					visual := visualizeWhitespace(line)
321. 					hints = append(hints, fmt.Sprintf(
322. 						"first line matches but whitespace differs. Expected line: %s — re-read the file with read_file and copy exact content",
323. 						visual,
324. 					))
325. 					break
326. 				}
327. 			}
328. 		}
329. 	}
330. 
331. 	if len(hints) == 0 {
332. 		hints = append(hints, "re-read the file with read_file and use exact content for old_text")
333. 	}
334. 
335. 	return strings.Join(hints, "; ")
336. }
337. 
338. // visualizeWhitespace replaces tab characters with the literal string "<TAB>"
339. // so the LLM can see the exact whitespace pattern in diagnostic messages.
340. func visualizeWhitespace(line string) string {
341. 	return strings.ReplaceAll(line, "\t", "<TAB>")
342. }
343.
```

</details>

/Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/multi_edit.go

<details>
<summary>146 lines</summary>

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"os"
8. 	"sort"
9. 	"strings"
10. )
11. 
12. // MultiEditFile applies multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call.
13. type MultiEditFile struct {
14. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
15. }
16. 
17. func (t MultiEditFile) Name() string { return "multi_edit_file" }
18. 
19. func (t MultiEditFile) Description() string {
20. 	return "Apply multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call. " +
21. 		"Each old_text must uniquely match in the file. All edits are applied atomically."
22. }
23. 
24. func (t MultiEditFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
25. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
26. 	"type": "object",
27. 	"properties": {
28. 		"file_path": {
29. 			"type": "string",
30. 			"description": "Path to the file to edit"
31. 		},
32. 		"edits": {
33. 			"type": "array",
34. 			"items": {
35. 				"type": "object",
36. 				"properties": {
37. 					"old_text": {
38. 						"type": "string",
39. 						"description": "Exact text to find"
40. 					},
41. 					"new_text": {
42. 						"type": "string",
43. 						"description": "Replacement text"
44. 					}
45. 				},
46. 				"required": [
47. 					"old_text",
48. 					"new_text"
49. 				]
50. 			},
51. 			"description": "Array of edit operations to apply"
52. 		},
53. 		"description": {
54. 			"type": "string",
55. 			"description": "REQUIRED. Brief activity label shown in the UI. Write in the user's language (e.g. 'Searching for TODO patterns', '检查构建配置'). You MUST always provide this field."
56. 		}
57. 	},
58. 	"required": [
59. 		"file_path",
60. 		"edits",
61. 		"description"
62. 	]
63. }`)
64. }
65. 
66. func (t MultiEditFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
67. 	var args struct {
68. 		FilePath string `json:"file_path"`
69. 		Edits    []struct {
70. 			OldText string `json:"old_text"`
71. 			NewText string `json:"new_text"`
72. 		} `json:"edits"`
73. 	}
74. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
75. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
76. 	}
77. 
78. 	if len(args.Edits) == 0 {
79. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "edits array must not be empty"}, nil
80. 	}
81. 
82. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.FilePath) {
83. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
84. 	}
85. 
86. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
87. 	if err != nil {
88. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
89. 	}
90. 
91. 	content := string(data)
92. 
93. 	// Find all match positions first, validate uniqueness.
94. 	type editPos struct {
95. 		start int
96. 		end   int
97. 		idx   int
98. 		old   string
99. 		new_  string
100. 	}
101. 	var positions []editPos
102. 
103. 	for i, edit := range args.Edits {
104. 		if edit.OldText == "" {
105. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("edits[%d]: old_text must not be empty", i)}, nil
106. 		}
107. 		count := strings.Count(content, edit.OldText)
108. 		if count == 0 {
109. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("edits[%d]: old_text not found in file", i)}, nil
110. 		}
111. 		if count > 1 {
112. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("edits[%d]: old_text found %d times — must be unique", i, count)}, nil
113. 		}
114. 		idx := strings.Index(content, edit.OldText)
115. 		positions = append(positions, editPos{
116. 			start: idx,
117. 			end:   idx + len(edit.OldText),
118. 			idx:   i,
119. 			old:   edit.OldText,
120. 			new_:  edit.NewText,
121. 		})
122. 	}
123. 
124. 	// Check for overlapping edits.
125. 	sort.Slice(positions, func(i, j int) bool { return positions[i].start < positions[j].start })
126. 	for i := 1; i < len(positions); i++ {
127. 		if positions[i].start < positions[i-1].end {
128. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(
129. 				"edits[%d] and edits[%d]: overlapping matches — each old_text must not overlap",
130. 				positions[i-1].idx, positions[i].idx)}, nil
131. 		}
132. 	}
133. 
134. 	// Apply edits from back to front to preserve earlier positions.
135. 	for i := len(positions) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
136. 		p := positions[i]
137. 		content = content[:p.start] + p.new_ + content[p.end:]
138. 	}
139. 
140. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
141. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
142. 	}
143. 
144. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Applied %d edits to %s", len(args.Edits), args.FilePath)}, nil
145. }
146.
```

</details>

1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"

9. // WriteFile implements the write_file tool.

12. }

14. func (t WriteFile) Name() string { return "write_file" }
15. 
16. func (t WriteFile) Description() string {
17. 	return "Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does."
18. }
19. 
20. func (t WriteFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
21. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
22. 	"type": "object",
23. 	"properties": {
24. 		"path": {
25. 			"type": "string",
26. 			"description": "Path to the file to write"
27. 		},
28. 		"content": {
29. 			"type": "string",
30. 			"description": "Content to write to the file"
31. 		},
32. 		"description": {
33. 			"type": "string",
34. 			"description": "REQUIRED. Brief activity label shown in the UI. Write in the user's language (e.g. 'Searching for TODO patterns', '检查构建配置'). You MUST always provide this field."
35. 		}
36. 	},
37. 	"required": [
38. 		"path",
39. 		"content",
40. 		"description"
41. 	]
42. }`)
43. }
44. 
45. func (t WriteFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
46. 	var args struct {
47. 		Path    string `json:"path"`
48. 		Content string `json:"content"`
49. 	}
50. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
51. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
52. 	}
53. 
54. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.Path) {
55. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
56. 	}
57. 
58. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.Path, []byte(args.Content), 0644); err != nil {
59. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
60. 	}
61. 
62. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Successfully wrote %d bytes to %s", len(args.Content), args.Path)}, nil
63. }

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"os"
8. 	"path/filepath"
9. 	"strings"
10. 
11. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/extract"
12. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/image"
13. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/provider"
14. )
15. 
16. // AllowedPathChecker is a function that checks if a path is allowed by sandbox policy.
17. type AllowedPathChecker func(path string) bool
18. 
19. // ReadFile implements the read_file tool.
20. type ReadFile struct {
21. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
22. }
23. 
24. func (t ReadFile) Name() string { return "read_file" }
25. 
26. func (t ReadFile) Description() string {
27. 	return "Read file contents. Supports text files, images (png/jpg/gif/webp), PDF, Office (docx/xlsx/pptx), OpenDocument (odt/ods/odp), EPUB, RTF, archives (zip/tar/tar.gz/tar.bz2), iWork (pages/numbers/key), and SVG. Archives are recursively extracted showing file listings and text content. Use offset and limit for range reading large files."
28. }
29. 
30. func (t ReadFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
31. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
32. 	"type": "object",
33. 	"properties": {
34. 		"path": {
35. 			"type": "string",
36. 			"description": "Absolute path to the file to read"
37. 		},
38. 		"offset": {
39. 			"type": "integer",
40. 			"description": "Line number to start reading from (1-based). Only applies to text files and extracted documents."
41. 		},
42. 		"limit": {
43. 			"type": "integer",
44. 			"description": "Maximum number of lines to read."
45. 		},
46. 		"description": {
47. 			"type": "string",
48. 			"description": "REQUIRED. Brief activity label shown in the UI. Write in the user's language (e.g. 'Searching for TODO patterns', '检查构建配置'). You MUST always provide this field."
49. 		}
50. 	},
51. 	"required": [
52. 		"path",
53. 		"description"
54. 	]
55. }`)
56. }
57. 
58. const maxFileSize = 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10MB pre-check limit
59. 
60. func (t ReadFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
61. 	var args struct {
62. 		Path   string `json:"path"`
63. 		Offset int    `json:"offset"`
64. 		Limit  int    `json:"limit"`
65. 	}
66. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
67. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
68. 	}
69. 
70. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.Path) {
71. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
72. 	}
73. 
74. 	// Pre-check file size
75. 	info, err := os.Stat(args.Path)
76. 	if err != nil {
77. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error accessing file: %v", err)}, nil
78. 	}
79. 	if info.Size() > maxFileSize {
80. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("file too large (%d MB). Use read_file with offset/limit for range reading.", info.Size()/(1024*1024))}, nil
81. 	}
82. 
83. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.Path)
84. 	if err != nil {
85. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
86. 	}
87. 
88. 	// Image file handling: decode and return as multimodal result (ignores offset/limit).
89. 	if image.IsImageFile(args.Path) {
90. 		img, err := image.Decode(data)
91. 		if err != nil {
92. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error decoding image: %v", err)}, nil
93. 		}
94. 		b64 := image.EncodeBase64(img)
95. 		return Result{
96. 			Content: image.Placeholder(filepath.Base(args.Path), img),
97. 			Images: []ResultImage{{
98. 				MIME:       img.MIME,
99. 				Base64:     b64,
100. 				Width:      img.Width,
101. 				Height:     img.Height,
102. 				SourcePath: args.Path,
103. 			}},
104. 		}, nil
105. 	}
106. 
107. 	// Document file handling: extract text, then apply range reading.
108. 	if extract.IsDocumentFile(args.Path) {
109. 		result, err := extract.Extract(args.Path, data)
110. 		if err != nil {
111. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error extracting document text: %v", err)}, nil
112. 		}
113. 
114. 		var header string
115. 		if result.Pages > 0 {
116. 			header = fmt.Sprintf("[Extracted from %s, %d pages]\n", result.Format, result.Pages)
117. 		} else {
118. 			header = fmt.Sprintf("[Extracted from %s]\n", result.Format)
119. 		}
120. 
121. 		text := readFileRange(result.Text, args.Offset, args.Limit, 0)
122. 		return Result{Content: header + text}, nil
123. 	}
124. 
125. 	// Plain text file: apply range reading.
126. 	content := string(data)
127. 	text := readFileRange(content, args.Offset, args.Limit, 0)
128. 
129. 	// Detect indentation style and encoding, prepend as metadata hint.
130. 	// This helps the LLM produce correct old_text matches for edit_file.
131. 	var meta string
132. 	indentHint := detectIndentStyle(content)
133. 	if indentHint != "" {
134. 		meta = indentHint
135. 	}
136. 	encHint := detectEncoding(data)
137. 	if encHint != "" {
138. 		if meta != "" {
139. 			meta += ", "
140. 		}
141. 		meta += encHint
142. 	}
143. 	if meta != "" {
144. 		text = "[" + meta + "]\n" + text
145. 	}
146. 
147. 	return Result{Content: text}, nil
148. }
149. 
150. // detectIndentStyle analyzes the first ~200 lines to determine tab vs space indentation.
151. func detectIndentStyle(content string) string {
152. 	tabs, spaces := 0, 0
153. 	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
154. 	limit := len(lines)
155. 	if limit > 200 {
156. 		limit = 200
157. 	}
158. 	for _, line := range lines[:limit] {
159. 		if len(line) == 0 || (line[0] != ' ' && line[0] != '\t') {
160. 			continue
161. 		}
162. 		if line[0] == '\t' {
163. 			tabs++
164. 		} else {
165. 			i := 0
166. 			for i < len(line) && line[i] == ' ' {
167. 				i++
168. 			}
169. 			if i >= 2 {
170. 				spaces++
171. 			}
172. 		}
173. 	}
174. 	total := tabs + spaces
175. 	if total == 0 {
176. 		return ""
177. 	}
178. 	if tabs > spaces {
179. 		return "indent: tab"
180. 	}
181. 	// Detect base indent unit by finding the greatest common divisor
182. 	// of all leading-space counts. This handles 2-space YAML vs 4-space Python/etc.
183. 	widths := map[int]int{}
184. 	for _, line := range lines[:limit] {
185. 		if len(line) == 0 || line[0] != ' ' {
186. 			continue
187. 		}
188. 		i := 0
189. 		for i < len(line) && line[i] == ' ' {
190. 			i++
191. 		}
192. 		if i >= 2 {
193. 			widths[i]++
194. 		}
195. 	}
196. 	if len(widths) == 0 {
197. 		return "indent: spaces"
198. 	}
199. 	// Find GCD of all observed widths
200. 	allWidths := make([]int, 0, len(widths))
201. 	for w := range widths {
202. 		allWidths = append(allWidths, w)
203. 	}
204. 	g := allWidths[0]
205. 	for _, w := range allWidths[1:] {
206. 		g = gcd(g, w)
207. 	}
208. 	if g < 2 {
209. 		g = 2
210. 	}
211. 	return fmt.Sprintf("indent: %d spaces", g)
212. }
213. 
214. func gcd(a, b int) int {
215. 	for b != 0 {
216. 		a, b = b, a%b
217. 	}
218. 	return a
219. }
220. 
221. // detectEncoding checks for BOM or non-UTF-8 content.
222. func detectEncoding(data []byte) string {
223. 	if len(data) >= 3 && data[0] == 0xEF && data[1] == 0xBB && data[2] == 0xBF {
224. 		return "encoding: UTF-8 BOM"
225. 	}
226. 	for _, b := range data[:min(len(data), 512)] {
227. 		if b == 0 {
228. 			return "encoding: binary/UTF-16"
229. 		}
230. 	}
231. 	return ""
232. }
233. 
234. // --- provider.ToolDefinition helper ---
235. 
236. // ToolDef creates a provider.ToolDefinition from a Tool.
237. func ToolDef(t Tool) provider.ToolDefinition {
238. 	return provider.ToolDefinition{
239. 		Name:        t.Name(),
240. 		Description: t.Description(),
241. 		Parameters:  t.Parameters(),
242. 	}
243. }
244.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 8937m 12s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/read_file_range.go.**

/Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/read_file_range.go

<details>
<summary>59 lines</summary>

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"strings"
6. )
7. 
8. const maxOutputLines = 2000
9. 
10. // readFileRange formats content with cat -n style line numbers, returning
11. // lines [offset, offset+limit) from the full content.
12. // offset is 1-based; 0 or 1 means start from the beginning.
13. // limit <= 0 means read to end (capped at maxOutputLines).
14. func readFileRange(content string, offset, limit int, totalLines int) string {
15. 	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
16. 
17. 	// Handle trailing newline: strings.Split on "abc\n" gives ["abc", ""]
18. 	if len(lines) > 0 && lines[len(lines)-1] == "" {
19. 		lines = lines[:len(lines)-1]
20. 	}
21. 	totalLines = len(lines)
22. 
23. 	// Convert 1-based offset to 0-based index
24. 	startIdx := offset - 1
25. 	if startIdx < 0 {
26. 		startIdx = 0
27. 	}
28. 	if startIdx >= totalLines {
29. 		return fmt.Sprintf("[File has %d lines. Offset %d is beyond end.]", totalLines, offset)
30. 	}
31. 
32. 	// Determine end index
33. 	endIdx := totalLines
34. 	if limit > 0 {
35. 		endIdx = startIdx + limit
36. 		if endIdx > totalLines {
37. 			endIdx = totalLines
38. 		}
39. 	} else if startIdx == 0 {
40. 		// No limit specified and starting from beginning: cap at maxOutputLines
41. 		if endIdx > maxOutputLines {
42. 			endIdx = maxOutputLines
43. 		}
44. 	}
45. 
46. 	var buf strings.Builder
47. 	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
48. 		fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "%6d\t%s\n", i+1, lines[i])
49. 	}
50. 
51. 	// Truncation notice
52. 	if endIdx < totalLines {
53. 		fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "[File truncated: showing lines %d-%d of %d. Use read_file with offset/limit for more.]\n",
54. 			startIdx+1, endIdx, totalLines)
55. 	}
56. 
57. 	return buf.String()
58. }
59.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 8938m 11s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

我看完了 `read_file` / `edit_file` / `multi_edit_file` / `write_file` 的实现，对现状和改进方向给你一个简明分析。

## 现状评估（已经做对的部分）

`read_file`：
- 已加 `cat -n` 行号、`[indent: tab/N spaces]`、`[encoding: UTF-8 BOM/binary]` 元数据头 — 给后续编辑提供了正确的缩进参考。

`edit_file`：
- exact 匹配失败时已经有 **whitespace 归一化 fallback**（tabs↔spaces 自动转换）。
- 失败时通过 `diagnoseMatchFailure` 给出 hints：tab/space 提示、CRLF 提示、首行匹配但缩进不同（含 `\<TAB\>` 可视化）。
- 多次匹配会要求 `replace_all`，并报告匹配次数。

## 真正的弱点（即弱模型经常踩的坑）

按"出现频率 × 修复 ROI"排序：

1. **`multi_edit_file` 没有任何 fallback 和诊断** — 任何一个 edit 失败就只回 `"old_text not found in file"`，没有 indent 归一化、没有 hints。这是最大的差距，弱模型用 multi_edit 几乎肯定卡死。

2. **`old_text` 误带了 `read_file` 输出的行号前缀** — `   42\t...` 这种粘贴回来的内容直接 fail，且 `diagnoseMatchFailure` 不识别这种模式（没有提示"看起来你把行号也粘进去了"）。

3. **行尾空白 / 前后空行差异** — exact 匹配失败时没有 trim 的二次尝试。弱模型经常少了或多了一个空格 / 末行换行。

4. **"非唯一"错误信息不够 actionable** — 只说"找到 N 次"，没告诉模型在哪几行（如果给出 line numbers，模型立刻就能加上下文行让它唯一）。

5. **`description` 字段是 REQUIRED schema 字段** — 弱模型经常漏，导致 JSON schema 校验失败而完全失败。所有 4 个工具都这样。属于 UI label，应该可选 + 默认回退到工具名。

6. **工具 Description 太短，缺少"how to use it well"指导** — 比如 `edit_file` 的 description 没告诉模型：先 `read_file`、保留缩进、避免行号前缀、加 1-2 行上下文确保唯一。模型靠猜。

7. **没有"按行号替换"的备用工具** — 对最弱的模型，line-range 替换比 search/replace 显著更可靠（Aider 的经验）。但这是一个新工具，需要权衡（增加选择困难 vs. 提升成功率）。

## 我建议的修复方向（YAGNI 优先）

**Phase 1（小改动、高 ROI、不引入新概念）：**
- a. 把 `edit_file` 的 normalize / diagnose 抽出来给 `multi_edit_file` 复用。
- b. 在 `diagnoseMatchFailure` 增加 **行号前缀检测**：发现 `^\s*\d+\t` 模式直接给出"removed line numbers and retry"提示；fallback 路径里直接尝试 strip 后重匹配（而不是只报错）。
- c. 增加 **trim trailing whitespace** 的二次匹配尝试（按行）。
- d. **CRLF fallback**：当文件是 CRLF 而 old_text 是 LF 时，自动把 old_text 的 `\n` 替换为 `\r\n` 再试一次（不只是报错）。
- e. 非唯一时，**列出每个匹配所在的行号**（"matches at lines 42, 87, 153 — add a line of surrounding context to disambiguate"）。
- f. 把所有 4 个工具的 `description` 字段改为 **optional**，UI label 缺失时回退到默认。
- g. 重写 4 个工具的 `Description()`，加上 weak-LLM 友好的使用规则（read first / preserve indent / 不要粘行号 / 加上下文确保唯一 / 失败后看 hint 不要瞎猜）。

**Phase 2（较大改动）：**
- h. 引入 `edit_file_lines` 或在 `edit_file` 增加 `start_line`/`end_line` 模式作为 search/replace 的可选替代 — 但只有在 Phase 1 改完后还测出弱模型扛不住才做，避免增加选择面。

## 几个想跟你确认的点

最关键的是：你是希望我**直接把 Phase 1 a–g 全做掉**，还是想先讨论某个具体点？

我会先问一个：

$ cd /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6 && head -60 docs/tool-schema-strict-mode.md

<summary>39 lines</summary>

```
# Tool Schema Strict Mode Analysis

> Status: **Deferred** — documented for future implementation.

## Background

We investigated adding `strict: true` to tool definitions to guarantee LLMs always fill required fields (especially the `description` parameter used for TUI activity labels).

### Problem Statement

Without `strict: true`, LLMs may omit `required` fields in tool calls. Session data confirmed that `read_file`/`edit_file`/`write_file` never included the `description` parameter despite it being marked `required` in the JSON Schema, while `run_command` consistently included it.

Root cause: `required` in JSON Schema is a **semantic hint**, not a runtime enforcement. LLMs can freely ignore it.

## What `strict: true` Does

Both OpenAI and Anthropic support `strict: true` at the **tool definition level** (not per-field). It uses **grammar-constrained sampling (CFG)** at inference time — compiling the JSON Schema into a context-free grammar that constrains token probabilities at each step, guaranteeing the generated JSON strictly matches the schema.

### Guarantees

- All `required` fields are always present
- Types match exactly (no `"2"` instead of `2`)
- No extra properties beyond the schema
- Tool name is always valid

## SDK Support

| Provider | SDK | Strict Field | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| OpenAI | `sashabaranov/go-openai` v1.41.2 | `FunctionDefinition.Strict bool` (`json:"strict,omitempty"`) | Works for GPT-4o+ |
| Anthropic | `anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go` v1.29.0 | `ToolParam.Strict param.Opt[bool]` (`json:"strict,omitzero"`) | Needs beta header `anthropic-beta: structured-outputs-2025-11-13` |
| Gemini | `google.golang.org/genai` v1.52.1 | No strict concept | N/A |
| OpenAI-compatible (DeepSeek, ZAI, etc.) | Same as OpenAI | Field exists in SDK | **Unknown behavior** — may ignore or return 400 |

### Additional Requirements

When `strict: true` is set, the JSON Schema **must** include `"additionalProperties": false` at the top level (and on all nested objects). This is enforced by both OpenAI and Anthropic APIs.

Current tool schemas do **not** include `"additionalProperties": false`.

## Implementation Plan (When Ready)

### Files to Modify

1. **`internal/provider/provider.go`** — Add `Strict bool` to `ToolDefinition`
2. **`internal/provider/openai.go`** — Set `Strict: t.Strict` in `convertTools()`
3. **`internal/provider/anthropic.go`** — Set `Strict` + inject `additionalProperties: false` into schema
4. **`internal/provider/gemini.go`** — No change (Gemini doesn't support strict)
5. **All tool `Parameters()` JSON schemas** — Add `"additionalProperties": false`

### Safety Analysis

- **OpenAI SDK**: `Strict bool` with `json:"strict,omitempty"` — `false` (zero value) is not serialized. Safe by default.
- **Anthropic SDK**: `Strict param.Opt[bool]` with `json:"strict,omitzero"` — zero value not serialized. Safe by default.
- **Gemini**: No strict field in SDK or API. Completely unaffected.
- **Third-party OpenAI-compatible APIs**: **RISK** — some may return HTTP 400 when receiving `strict: true`. Needs per-provider testing.

### Recommended Approach

1. Add a `StrictMode` config option (per-vendor or global) so it can be toggled per-endpoint
<exited with exit code 0>
```

</details>

21. 	}`)

24. 		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
25. 	}
26. 	if result.IsError {
27. 		t.Fatalf("unexpected tool error: %s", result.Content)
28. 	}
29. 
30. 	data, _ := os.ReadFile(fp)
31. 	if string(data) != "package main\n\nfunc main() {\n\tfmt.Println(\"world\")\n}\n" {
32. 		t.Errorf("unexpected file content: %q", string(data))
33. 	}
34. }

7. 	"path/filepath"
8. 	"testing"
9. )
10. 
11. func TestMultiEdit_Basic(t *testing.T) {
12. 	dir := t.TempDir()
13. 	fp := filepath.Join(dir, "test.go")
14. 	os.WriteFile(fp, []byte("package main\n\nfunc a() { }\nfunc b() { }\n"), 0644)
15. 
16. 	me := MultiEditFile{}
17. 	input, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]interface{}{
18. 		"file_path": fp,
19. 		"edits": []map[string]string{
20. 			{"old_text": "func a() { }", "new_text": "func a() int { return 1 }"},
21. 			{"old_text": "func b() { }", "new_text": "func b() int { return 2 }"},
22. 		},
23. 	})
24. 	result, err := me.Execute(context.Background(), input)
25. 	if err != nil {
26. 		t.Fatal(err)
27. 	}
28. 	if result.IsError {
29. 		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %s", result.Content)
30. 	}
31. 
32. 	data, _ := os.ReadFile(fp)
33. 	if !containsAny(string(data), "return 1") || !containsAny(string(data), "return 2") {
34. 		t.Errorf("unexpected content: %s", string(data))
35. 	}
36. }
37. 
38. func TestMultiEdit_Overlapping(t *testing.T) {
39. 	dir := t.TempDir()
40. 	fp := filepath.Join(dir, "test.txt")
```

<details>
<summary>47 lines</summary>

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"os"
6. 
7. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/checkpoint"
8. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/util"
9. )
10. 
11. // preWriteHook is called before a file write to save a checkpoint of the
12. // old content. Set via SetPreWriteHook during agent initialization.
13. // The hook may return an error to abort the write.
14. var preWriteHook func(filePath, oldContent, newContent, toolCall string) error
15. 
16. // SetPreWriteHook sets a global hook called before file writes.
17. // The hook receives (filePath, oldContent, newContent, toolName) and is
18. // used to save undo checkpoints. If the hook returns an error, the write is aborted.
19. func SetPreWriteHook(fn func(filePath, oldContent, newContent, toolCall string) error) {
20. 	preWriteHook = fn
21. }
22. 
23. // CheckpointSaver returns a pre-write hook that saves checkpoints to the
24. // given manager. This is the standard hook used by TUI/daemon/ACP modes.
25. // Checkpoint saving is in-memory only; errors are logged but do not abort writes.
26. func CheckpointSaver(mgr *checkpoint.Manager) func(filePath, oldContent, newContent, toolCall string) error {
27. 	return func(filePath, oldContent, newContent, toolCall string) error {
28. 		mgr.Save(filePath, oldContent, newContent, toolCall)
29. 		return nil
30. 	}
31. }
32. 
33. // atomicWriteFile writes data to a file atomically. Before writing, if a
34. // pre-write hook is set and the file already exists, the old content is
35. // captured and passed to the hook for checkpoint/undo support.
36. // If the hook returns an error, the write is aborted.
37. func atomicWriteFile(path string, data []byte, defaultMode os.FileMode) error {
38. 	if preWriteHook != nil {
39. 		if oldData, err := os.ReadFile(path); err == nil {
40. 			if err := preWriteHook(path, string(oldData), string(data), ""); err != nil {
41. 				return fmt.Errorf("pre-write hook aborted write to %s: %w", path, err)
42. 			}
43. 		}
44. 	}
45. 	return util.AtomicWriteFile(path, data, defaultMode)
46. }
47.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>39 lines</summary>

```
20. func AtomicWriteFile(path string, data []byte, defaultMode os.FileMode) error {
21. 	mode := defaultMode
22. 	if info, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
23. 		mode = info.Mode().Perm()
24. 	}
25. 
26. 	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
27. 	tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".ggcode-tmp-*")
28. 	if err != nil {
29. 		return fmt.Errorf("creating temp file in %s: %w", dir, err)
30. 	}
31. 	tmpName := tmp.Name()
32. 	cleanup := func() { os.Remove(tmpName) }
33. 
34. 	if _, err := tmp.Write(data); err != nil {
35. 		tmp.Close()
36. 		cleanup()
37. 		return fmt.Errorf("writing temp file: %w", err)
38. 	}
39. 	if err := tmp.Sync(); err != nil {
40. 		tmp.Close()
41. 		cleanup()
42. 		return fmt.Errorf("syncing temp file: %w", err)
43. 	}
44. 	if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
45. 		cleanup()
46. 		return fmt.Errorf("closing temp file: %w", err)
47. 	}
48. 	if err := os.Chmod(tmpName, mode); err != nil {
49. 		cleanup()
50. 		return fmt.Errorf("chmod temp file: %w", err)
51. 	}
52. 	if err := os.Rename(tmpName, path); err != nil {
53. 		cleanup()
54. 		return fmt.Errorf("renaming temp file into place: %w", err)
55. 	}
56. 	return nil
57. }
58.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 8946m 41s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Confirmed — the `internal/config` failures are pre-existing (unrelated to my changes). All `internal/tool` tests pass cleanly.

Summary:

## Changes (Phase 1 a–g)

**New: `internal/tool/edit_match.go`** — shared fallback chain for `edit_file` and `multi_edit_file`:
- `resolveOldText()` tries 4 fallbacks in order: indentation normalization → strip `cat -n` line-number prefix → CRLF↔LF conversion → per-line trailing-whitespace tolerance
- `findMatchLineNumbers()` → for actionable non-unique errors

**`edit_file.go`** — uses `resolveOldText`; non-unique error now lists matching line numbers and recommends adding context or `replace_all`; `description` no longer required; rewrote `Description()` and parameter docs with weak-LLM-friendly rules (read first, no line-number prefix, byte-for-byte match, etc.).

**`multi_edit.go`** — same fallback chain (previously had zero fallback); diagnose hints; non-unique error includes line numbers; `description` no longer required; new Description.

**`read_file.go`** — `description` no longer required; rewrote Description to explicitly tell the LLM about the line-number-prefix output format and that it MUST be stripped before use in `edit_file`.

**`write_file.go`** — auto-creates parent dirs (one less `mkdir` round-trip); `description` no longer required; new Description warns about overwrite and recommends `edit_file` for existing files.

**Tests:**
- `edit_file_fallback_test.go` (5 cases): line-number prefix, CRLF, trailing whitespace, non-unique-with-line-numbers, optional description
- `edit_match_test.go` (5 cases): multi_edit fallback chain (mixed indent + CRLF in one call), multi_edit hint, write_file parent-dir creation, exact-match identity, line-number false-positive guard

**Validation:** `go build -tags goolm ./...`, `go vet -tags goolm ./...`, `gofmt -l .`, all `internal/tool` tests pass. The 3 `internal/config` failures are pre-existing (vendor catalog drift on `requesty`/`nvidia`/`perplexity`/`getgoapi` — confirmed identical without my changes).

**Commit the file-edit tool improvements**

$ cd /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6 && git add internal/tool/edit_file.go internal/tool/multi_edit.go internal/tool/read_file.go internal/tool/write_file.go internal/tool/edit_match.go internal/tool/edit_file_fallback_test.go internal/tool/edit_match_test.go && git commit -m "feat(tool): make file edit tools more forgiving for weaker LLMs

Refactor edit_file/multi_edit_file/read_file/write_file to reduce common
failure modes that hurt smaller models:

- New internal/tool/edit_match.go shared by edit_file and multi_edit_file:
  resolveOldText() tries indent normalization, cat -n line-number prefix
  stripping, CRLF<->LF conversion, and per-line trailing-whitespace
  tolerance before failing.
- multi_edit_file previously had no fallback at all; it now uses the
  same chain plus diagnoseMatchFailure hints.
- Non-unique-match errors now list the line numbers where old_text
  occurs and explicitly suggest adding context or replace_all=true.
- write_file auto-creates parent directories.
- The 'description' field is now optional on all four file tools (UI
  label only; TUI falls back to describeTool()). Weak models that omit
  it no longer hit JSON schema validation errors.
- Tool Description() texts rewritten with explicit, weak-LLM-friendly
  rules: read first, preserve indentation, do not include line-number
  prefix from read_file output, add 1-3 lines of context for uniqueness.

Tests: 10 new cases covering each fallback, line-number-stripping false
positive guard, parent-dir creation, and optional description field.

<sub>⏱️ 8967m 56s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

看了下这个 trace —— 失败的根因是：**LLM 提供的 `old_text` 没有任何 leading whitespace，但文件该行以 `\t\t` 开头**。我现有的 `normalizeIndentation` 只做 tab↔space 转换，不处理"完全没缩进 vs 文件有缩进"这种"整块缩进偏移"。

这是弱模型的高频失败模式（懒得抄前导缩进），需要补一个 **leading-indent-shift** 容错。

### ✅ `view`

<details>
<summary>110 lines</summary>

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"regexp"
6. 	"strings"
7. )
8. 
9. // matchResult describes a successful old_text resolution.
10. type matchResult struct {
11. 	canonical string // the actual bytes in content that will be replaced
12. 	transform string // diagnostic tag for which fallback fired ("" = exact)
13. 	shift     string // for leading-indent-shift: prefix to prepend to new_text lines
14. }
15. 
16. // resolveOldText tries multiple strategies to locate oldText in content,
17. // helping weaker LLMs whose `old_text` argument frequently differs from the
18. // file in trivial ways (indentation style, line-number prefix copied from
19. // read_file output, CRLF vs LF, trailing whitespace, missing leading
20. // indentation).
21. //
22. // On success, canonical is the exact substring of content that will be
23. // substituted (so a strings.Replace targets the right bytes). On failure
24. // canonical is "".
25. func resolveOldText(content, oldText string) matchResult {
26. 	if oldText == "" {
27. 		return matchResult{}
28. 	}
29. 	if strings.Contains(content, oldText) {
30. 		return matchResult{canonical: oldText}
31. 	}
32. 
33. 	// 1. Indentation normalization (tabs <-> spaces).
34. 	if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, oldText); normalized != oldText && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
35. 		return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "indent-normalized"}
36. 	}
37. 
38. 	// 2. Line-number prefix from read_file output: "   42\t<line>".
39. 	if stripped := stripLineNumberPrefix(oldText); stripped != oldText {
40. 		if strings.Contains(content, stripped) {
41. 			return matchResult{canonical: stripped, transform: "line-numbers-stripped"}
42. 		}
43. 		if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, stripped); normalized != stripped && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
44. 			return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "line-numbers-stripped+indent-normalized"}
45. 		}
46. 	}
47. 
48. 	// 3. CRLF / LF mismatch.
49. 	if crlf := tryCRLFMatch(content, oldText); crlf != "" {
50. 		return matchResult{canonical: crlf, transform: "crlf-converted"}
51. 	}
52. 
53. 	// 4. Leading-indent shift: LLM provided the right text but with less
54. 	// (or no) leading indentation. Match by structural equality after
55. 	// stripping both leading and trailing whitespace, then return the
56. 	// file's actual block plus the shift to prepend to new_text lines.
57. 	if canonical, shift := tryLeadingIndentShift(content, oldText); canonical != "" {
58. 		return matchResult{canonical: canonical, transform: "leading-indent-shift", shift: shift}
59. 	}
60. 
61. 	// 5. Trailing-whitespace tolerance: leading whitespace already matches
62. 	// (otherwise step 4 would have caught the mismatch first); only the
63. 	// per-line trailing differs.
64. 	if trimmed := tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch(content, oldText); trimmed != "" {
65. 		return matchResult{canonical: trimmed, transform: "trailing-whitespace-tolerant"}
66. 	}
67. 
68. 	return matchResult{}
69. }
70. 
71. // adjustNewText applies the same transform that was used to find old_text
72. // to new_text, so the replacement remains consistent with the file.
73. func adjustNewText(content, newText string, mr matchResult) string {
74. 	out := newText
75. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "line-numbers-stripped") {
76. 		out = stripLineNumberPrefix(out)
77. 	}
78. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "indent-normalized") {
79. 		out = normalizeIndentation(content, out)
80. 	}
81. 	if mr.transform == "crlf-converted" && !strings.Contains(out, "\r\n") {
82. 		out = strings.ReplaceAll(out, "\n", "\r\n")
83. 	}
84. 	if mr.transform == "leading-indent-shift" && mr.shift != "" {
85. 		out = applyLeadingIndentShift(out, mr.shift)
86. 	}
87. 	return out
88. }
89. 
90. // lineNumberPrefixRE matches prefixes produced by readFileRange:
91. // up to 6 leading spaces, then 1+ digits, then a tab.
92. // We allow more leading spaces in case the LLM reformatted slightly.
93. var lineNumberPrefixRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^\s{0,12}\d+\t`)
94. 
95. // stripLineNumberPrefix removes "  42\t" style prefixes if a clear majority
96. // of non-empty lines have them. This catches the common failure where an
97. // LLM pastes back read_file output (which is line-numbered) verbatim as
98. // old_text.
99. func stripLineNumberPrefix(text string) string {
100. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
101. 	matched, nonEmpty := 0, 0
102. 	for _, l := range lines {
103. 		if strings.TrimSpace(l) == "" {
104. 			continue
105. 		}
106. 		nonEmpty++
107. 		if lineNumberPrefixRE.MatchString(l) {
108. 			matched++
109. 		}
110. 	}
```

</details>

99. func TestResolveOldText_PreservesUnchangedOnExactMatch(t *testing.T) {
100. 	got, transform := resolveOldText("hello world", "hello")
101. 	if got != "hello" || transform != "" {
102. 		t.Errorf("exact match should return unchanged; got %q, transform=%q", got, transform)
103. 	}
104. }
105. 
106. func TestStripLineNumberPrefix_NoFalsePositive(t *testing.T) {
107. 	// Single line that happens to have digits + tab prefix should not be
108. 	// treated as line-numbered (requires >=2 such lines).
109. 	in := "42\tprice: 12.99"
110. 	if got := stripLineNumberPrefix(in); got != in {
111. 		t.Errorf("expected no-op on single-line digit-tab input; got %q", got)
112. 	}
113. }
114. 
115. func TestFindMatchLineNumbers(t *testing.T) {

$ cd /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6 && cat >> internal/tool/edit_file_fallback_test.go <<'EOF'

func TestEditFile_LeadingIndentShift(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	fp := filepath.Join(dir, "test.go")
	// File has the comment indented with two tabs.
	content := "package main\n\nfunc x() {\n\tif true {\n\t\t// Device flow: foo\n\t\tdoThing()\n\t}\n}\n"
	if err := os.WriteFile(fp, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	// LLM provides old_text with NO leading whitespace.
	input, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
		"file_path": fp,
		"old_text":  "// Device flow: foo",
		"new_text":  "// Device flow: bar",
	})
	res, err := EditFile{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if res.IsError {
		t.Fatalf("expected leading-indent-shift to succeed; got: %s", res.Content)
	}
	got, _ := os.ReadFile(fp)
	want := "package main\n\nfunc x() {\n\tif true {\n\t\t// Device flow: bar\n\t\tdoThing()\n\t}\n}\n"
	if string(got) != want {
		t.Errorf("unexpected content:\n got: %q\nwant: %q", got, want)
	}
}

func TestEditFile_LeadingIndentShift_MultiLine(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	fp := filepath.Join(dir, "test.go")
	// Block has consistent 2-tab base indent + relative indent within.
	content := "func x() {\n\t\tif a {\n\t\t\treturn 1\n\t\t}\n}\n"
	if err := os.WriteFile(fp, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	// LLM strips the 2-tab base but keeps relative indent.
	input, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
		"file_path": fp,
		"old_text":  "if a {\n\treturn 1\n}",
		"new_text":  "if a {\n\treturn 2\n}",
	})
	res, err := EditFile{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if res.IsError {
		t.Fatalf("expected leading-indent-shift to succeed; got: %s", res.Content)
	}
	got, _ := os.ReadFile(fp)
	want := "func x() {\n\t\tif a {\n\t\t\treturn 2\n\t\t}\n}\n"
	if string(got) != want {
		t.Errorf("unexpected content:\n got: %q\nwant: %q", got, want)
	}
}
EOF

<sub>⏱️ 8971m 56s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Commit leading-indent-shift**

$ cd /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6 && git commit -m "tool: add leading-indent-shift fallback for edit_file/multi_edit

Weak LLMs frequently provide old_text with no (or insufficient) leading
indentation when the file uses tab/space indents — e.g. file has
\"\t\t// Device flow: foo\" but the model supplies just \"// Device flow: foo\".
Previously this fell through every fallback and returned 'old_text not
found', forcing the agent to retry with sed.

Add a structural matcher that:
  - strips leading+trailing whitespace per line to find the block
  - verifies the file's leading whitespace starts with the LLM's leading
    whitespace on every non-empty line (so the shift is well-defined)
  - requires the extra prefix (\"shift\") to be identical on every
    non-empty line, preserving the LLM's relative indent
  - returns the canonical bytes for safe strings.Replace
  - applies the shift to every non-empty line of new_text

Refactor resolveOldText/adjustNewText to a matchResult struct so the
shift can flow through to new_text without growing the return tuple.

New tests cover the single-line and multi-line block cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>" 2>&1 | tail -10

<details>
<summary>6 lines</summary>

 5 files changed, 223 insertions(+), 33 deletions(-)

<sub>⏱️ 8972m 3s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Committed as `2fae980`. 

新增了 `leading-indent-shift` 回退策略，专门解决你截图里反复出现的"LLM 提供的 old_text 没有前导缩进"的失败模式：

- 按行 `TrimSpace` 做结构性匹配
- 校验每个非空行的"shift"（文件多出来的前导空白）完全一致，保证替换不会错位
- 把同样的 shift 应用到 `new_text` 每个非空行
- 单行（`// Device flow: foo`）和多行块两种场景都加了测试

回退链顺序：精确 → 缩进归一化 → 行号前缀 → CRLF → **leading-indent-shift（新）** → trailing-whitespace。构建/vet/tool 测试全部通过。

/Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/edit_file.go (lines 1-240)

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"os"
8. 	"strings"
9. )
10. 
11. // EditFile implements the edit_file tool for find-and-replace editing.
12. type EditFile struct {
13. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
14. }
15. 
16. func (t EditFile) Name() string { return "edit_file" }
17. 
18. func (t EditFile) Description() string {
19. 	return "Edit a file by replacing one occurrence of old_text with new_text. " +
20. 		"Rules for high success rate: " +
21. 		"(1) ALWAYS read_file first to get the exact current content. " +
22. 		"(2) old_text must match the file byte-for-byte INCLUDING indentation (tabs vs spaces) and line endings. " +
23. 		"(3) Do NOT include the line-number prefix (e.g. \"   42\\t\") from read_file output in old_text — only the raw line content. " +
24. 		"(4) old_text must be UNIQUE in the file; if not, include 1-3 lines of surrounding context to disambiguate, or set replace_all=true. " +
25. 		"(5) On failure, the error message lists hints (indent style, near-matches with whitespace visualised, matching line numbers) — read them and adjust before retrying. " +
26. 		"For multiple edits to the same file in one round-trip, prefer multi_edit_file."
27. }
28. 
29. func (t EditFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
30. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
31. 	"type": "object",
32. 	"properties": {
33. 		"file_path": {
34. 			"type": "string",
35. 			"description": "Path to the file to edit."
36. 		},
37. 		"old_text": {
38. 			"type": "string",
39. 			"description": "Exact text to find. Must match the file byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings). Do not include line-number prefixes from read_file output."
40. 		},
41. 		"new_text": {
42. 			"type": "string",
43. 			"description": "Replacement text. Use the same indentation style as old_text."
44. 		},
45. 		"replace_all": {
46. 			"type": "boolean",
47. 			"description": "If true, replace every occurrence of old_text. Default false (the call fails if old_text is not unique)."
48. 		},
49. 		"description": {
50. 			"type": "string",
51. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language (e.g. 'Updating retry policy', '更新缩进')."
52. 		}
53. 	},
54. 	"required": [
55. 		"file_path",
56. 		"old_text",
57. 		"new_text"
58. 	]
59. }`)
60. }
61. 
62. func (t EditFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
63. 	var args struct {
64. 		FilePath   string `json:"file_path"`
65. 		OldText    string `json:"old_text"`
66. 		NewText    string `json:"new_text"`
67. 		ReplaceAll bool   `json:"replace_all"`
68. 	}
69. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
70. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
71. 	}
72. 
73. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.FilePath) {
74. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
75. 	}
76. 
77. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
78. 	if err != nil {
79. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
80. 	}
81. 
82. 	content := string(data)
83. 
84. 	mr := resolveOldText(content, args.OldText)
85. 	if mr.canonical == "" {
86. 		hint := diagnoseMatchFailure(content, args.OldText)
87. 		msg := "old_text not found in file"
88. 		if hint != "" {
89. 			msg += ". " + hint
90. 		}
91. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
92. 	}
93. 	oldText := mr.canonical
94. 
95. 	count := strings.Count(content, oldText)
96. 	if !args.ReplaceAll && count > 1 {
97. 		lines := findMatchLineNumbers(content, oldText)
98. 		msg := fmt.Sprintf(
99. 			"old_text found %d times in file — must be unique. Add 1-3 lines of surrounding context to disambiguate, or set replace_all=true to replace every occurrence.",
100. 			count,
101. 		)
102. 		if len(lines) > 0 {
103. 			more := ""
104. 			if count > len(lines) {
105. 				more = fmt.Sprintf(" (showing first %d)", len(lines))
106. 			}
107. 			msg += fmt.Sprintf(" Matches start at line(s): %s%s.", formatMatchLines(lines), more)
108. 		}
109. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
110. 	}
111. 
112. 	// Apply the same transform to new_text so the replacement matches the
113. 	// file's conventions (indentation, line endings, line-number stripping).
114. 	newText := args.NewText
115. 	if mr.transform != "" {
116. 		newText = adjustNewText(content, args.NewText, mr)
117. 	}
118. 
119. 	var newContent string
120. 	if args.ReplaceAll {
121. 		newContent = strings.ReplaceAll(content, oldText, newText)
122. 	} else {
123. 		newContent = strings.Replace(content, oldText, newText, 1)
124. 	}
125. 
126. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(newContent), 0644); err != nil {
127. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
128. 	}
129. 
130. 	// Build a summary
131. 	oldLines := strings.Count(args.OldText, "\n") + 1
132. 	newLines := strings.Count(args.NewText, "\n") + 1
133. 	if args.ReplaceAll {
134. 		return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Replaced %d occurrence(s) in %s: %d lines -> %d lines", count, args.FilePath, oldLines, newLines)}, nil
135. 	}
136. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Replaced 1 occurrence in %s: %d lines -> %d lines", args.FilePath, oldLines, newLines)}, nil
137. }
138. 
139. // normalizeIndentation converts the indentation of text to match the file's style.
140. // If the file uses tabs and text uses spaces, leading spaces are converted to tabs.
141. // If the file uses spaces and text uses tabs, leading tabs are converted to spaces.
142. // The width parameter is inferred from the file content.
143. func normalizeIndentation(fileContent, text string) string {
144. 	fileUsesTabs := false
145. 	fileTabWidth := 0
146. 	{
147. 		tabLines, spaceLines := 0, 0
148. 		spaceWidths := map[int]int{}
149. 		lines := strings.Split(fileContent, "\n")
150. 		limit := len(lines)
151. 		if limit > 200 {
152. 			limit = 200
153. 		}
154. 		for _, line := range lines[:limit] {
155. 			if len(line) == 0 {
156. 				continue
157. 			}
158. 			if line[0] == '\t' {
159. 				tabLines++
160. 			} else if line[0] == ' ' {
161. 				spaceLines++
162. 				n := 0
163. 				for n < len(line) && line[n] == ' ' {
164. 					n++
165. 				}
166. 				if n >= 2 {
167. 					spaceWidths[n]++
168. 				}
169. 			}
170. 		}
171. 		fileUsesTabs = tabLines > spaceLines
172. 		if len(spaceWidths) > 0 {
173. 			allW := make([]int, 0, len(spaceWidths))
174. 			for w := range spaceWidths {
175. 				allW = append(allW, w)
176. 			}
177. 			g := allW[0]
178. 			for _, w := range allW[1:] {
179. 				g = gcd(g, w)
180. 			}
181. 			if g < 2 {
182. 				g = 2
183. 			}
184. 			fileTabWidth = g
185. 		}
186. 		if fileTabWidth == 0 {
187. 			fileTabWidth = 4
188. 		}
189. 	}
190. 
191. 	// Check what the text uses
192. 	textHasTabs := false
193. 	textHasLeadingSpaces := false
194. 	for _, line := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
195. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == '\t' {
196. 			textHasTabs = true
197. 		}
198. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == ' ' {
199. 			textHasLeadingSpaces = true
200. 		}
201. 	}
202. 
203. 	// File uses tabs, text uses spaces → convert leading spaces to tabs
204. 	if fileUsesTabs && !textHasTabs && textHasLeadingSpaces {
205. 		return convertSpacesToTabs(text, fileTabWidth)
206. 	}
207. 
208. 	// File uses spaces, text uses tabs → convert leading tabs to spaces
209. 	if !fileUsesTabs && textHasTabs {
210. 		return convertTabsToSpaces(text, fileTabWidth)
211. 	}
212. 
213. 	return text
214. }
215. 
216. // convertSpacesToTabs converts leading spaces to tabs based on tabWidth.
217. // For example, with tabWidth=4:
218. //
219. //	"    foo" → "\tfoo"
220. //	"        bar" → "\t\tbar"
221. //	"  baz" → "\tbaz"  (non-multiple: consume one tab for any leading spaces)
222. func convertSpacesToTabs(text string, tabWidth int) string {
223. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
224. 	for i, line := range lines {
225. 		if len(line) == 0 {
226. 			continue
227. 		}
228. 		// Count leading spaces
229. 		spaces := 0
230. 		for spaces < len(line) && line[spaces] == ' ' {
231. 			spaces++
232. 		}
233. 		if spaces == 0 {
234. 			continue
235. 		}
236. 		tabs := spaces / tabWidth
237. 		remainder := spaces % tabWidth
238. 		// Use at least 1 tab if there are any leading spaces
239. 		if tabs == 0 && spaces > 0 {
240. 			tabs = 1

1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"os"
8. 	"sort"
9. 	"strings"
10. )
11. 
12. // MultiEditFile applies multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call.
13. type MultiEditFile struct {
14. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
15. }
16. 
17. func (t MultiEditFile) Name() string { return "multi_edit_file" }
18. 
19. func (t MultiEditFile) Description() string {
20. 	return "Apply multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call. " +
21. 		"Each edit follows the same rules as edit_file: every old_text must match the file byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings) and must be UNIQUE in the file. " +
22. 		"Edits must not overlap. All edits are applied atomically — if any single edit fails, the file is left unchanged. " +
23. 		"Always read_file first; do not include line-number prefixes from read_file output in old_text. " +
24. 		"Use this instead of repeated edit_file calls when changing multiple sites in one file (e.g. renaming a symbol)."
25. }
26. 
27. func (t MultiEditFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
28. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
29. 	"type": "object",
30. 	"properties": {
31. 		"file_path": {
32. 			"type": "string",
33. 			"description": "Path to the file to edit."
34. 		},
35. 		"edits": {
36. 			"type": "array",
37. 			"items": {
38. 				"type": "object",
39. 				"properties": {
40. 					"old_text": {
41. 						"type": "string",
42. 						"description": "Exact text to find. Must match byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings) and be unique in the file. Do not include line-number prefixes from read_file output."
43. 					},
44. 					"new_text": {
45. 						"type": "string",
46. 						"description": "Replacement text. Use the same indentation style as old_text."
47. 					}
48. 				},
49. 				"required": [
50. 					"old_text",
51. 					"new_text"
52. 				]
53. 			},
54. 			"description": "Edits to apply, in any order. Each old_text must be unique in the file and not overlap with other edits."
55. 		},
56. 		"description": {
57. 			"type": "string",
58. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language."
59. 		}
60. 	},
61. 	"required": [
62. 		"file_path",
63. 		"edits"
64. 	]
65. }`)
66. }
67. 
68. func (t MultiEditFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
69. 	var args struct {
70. 		FilePath string `json:"file_path"`
71. 		Edits    []struct {
72. 			OldText string `json:"old_text"`
73. 			NewText string `json:"new_text"`
74. 		} `json:"edits"`
75. 	}
76. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
77. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
78. 	}
79. 
80. 	if len(args.Edits) == 0 {
81. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "edits array must not be empty"}, nil
82. 	}
83. 
84. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.FilePath) {
85. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
86. 	}
87. 
88. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
89. 	if err != nil {
90. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
91. 	}
92. 
93. 	content := string(data)
94. 
95. 	// Find all match positions first, validate uniqueness.
96. 	type editPos struct {
97. 		start int
98. 		end   int
99. 		idx   int
100. 		old   string
101. 		new_  string
102. 	}
103. 	var positions []editPos
104. 
105. 	for i, edit := range args.Edits {
106. 		if edit.OldText == "" {
107. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("edits[%d]: old_text must not be empty", i)}, nil
108. 		}
109. 		mr := resolveOldText(content, edit.OldText)
110. 		if mr.canonical == "" {
111. 			hint := diagnoseMatchFailure(content, edit.OldText)
112. 			msg := fmt.Sprintf("edits[%d]: old_text not found in file", i)
113. 			if hint != "" {
114. 				msg += ". " + hint
115. 			}
116. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
117. 		}
118. 		oldText := mr.canonical
119. 		count := strings.Count(content, oldText)
120. 		if count > 1 {
121. 			lines := findMatchLineNumbers(content, oldText)
122. 			msg := fmt.Sprintf(
123. 				"edits[%d]: old_text found %d times — must be unique. Add 1-3 lines of surrounding context to disambiguate.",
124. 				i, count,
125. 			)
126. 			if len(lines) > 0 {
127. 				more := ""
128. 				if count > len(lines) {
129. 					more = fmt.Sprintf(" (showing first %d)", len(lines))
130. 				}
131. 				msg += fmt.Sprintf(" Matches start at line(s): %s%s.", formatMatchLines(lines), more)
132. 			}
133. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
134. 		}
135. 		idx := strings.Index(content, oldText)
136. 		newText := edit.NewText
137. 		if mr.transform != "" {
138. 			newText = adjustNewText(content, edit.NewText, mr)
139. 		}
140. 		positions = append(positions, editPos{
141. 			start: idx,
142. 			end:   idx + len(oldText),
143. 			idx:   i,
144. 			old:   oldText,
145. 			new_:  newText,
146. 		})
147. 	}
148. 
149. 	// Check for overlapping edits.
150. 	sort.Slice(positions, func(i, j int) bool { return positions[i].start < positions[j].start })
151. 	for i := 1; i < len(positions); i++ {
152. 		if positions[i].start < positions[i-1].end {
153. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(
154. 				"edits[%d] and edits[%d]: overlapping matches — each old_text must not overlap",
155. 				positions[i-1].idx, positions[i].idx)}, nil
156. 		}
157. 	}
158. 
159. 	// Apply edits from back to front to preserve earlier positions.
160. 	for i := len(positions) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
161. 		p := positions[i]
162. 		content = content[:p.start] + p.new_ + content[p.end:]
163. 	}
164. 
165. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
166. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
167. 	}
168. 
169. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Applied %d edits to %s", len(args.Edits), args.FilePath)}, nil
170. }
171.
```

1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"regexp"
6. 	"strings"
7. )
8. 
9. // matchResult describes a successful old_text resolution.
10. type matchResult struct {
11. 	canonical string // the actual bytes in content that will be replaced
12. 	transform string // diagnostic tag for which fallback fired ("" = exact)
13. 	shift     string // for leading-indent-shift: prefix to prepend to new_text lines
14. }
15. 
16. // resolveOldText tries multiple strategies to locate oldText in content,
17. // helping weaker LLMs whose `old_text` argument frequently differs from the
18. // file in trivial ways (indentation style, line-number prefix copied from
19. // read_file output, CRLF vs LF, trailing whitespace, missing leading
20. // indentation).
21. //
22. // On success, canonical is the exact substring of content that will be
23. // substituted (so a strings.Replace targets the right bytes). On failure
24. // canonical is "".
25. func resolveOldText(content, oldText string) matchResult {
26. 	if oldText == "" {
27. 		return matchResult{}
28. 	}
29. 	if strings.Contains(content, oldText) {
30. 		return matchResult{canonical: oldText}
31. 	}
32. 
33. 	// 1. Indentation normalization (tabs <-> spaces).
34. 	if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, oldText); normalized != oldText && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
35. 		return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "indent-normalized"}
36. 	}
37. 
38. 	// 2. Line-number prefix from read_file output: "   42\t<line>".
39. 	if stripped := stripLineNumberPrefix(oldText); stripped != oldText {
40. 		if strings.Contains(content, stripped) {
41. 			return matchResult{canonical: stripped, transform: "line-numbers-stripped"}
42. 		}
43. 		if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, stripped); normalized != stripped && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
44. 			return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "line-numbers-stripped+indent-normalized"}
45. 		}
46. 	}
47. 
48. 	// 3. CRLF / LF mismatch.
49. 	if crlf := tryCRLFMatch(content, oldText); crlf != "" {
50. 		return matchResult{canonical: crlf, transform: "crlf-converted"}
51. 	}
52. 
53. 	// 4. Leading-indent shift: LLM provided the right text but with less
54. 	// (or no) leading indentation. Match by structural equality after
55. 	// stripping both leading and trailing whitespace, then return the
56. 	// file's actual block plus the shift to prepend to new_text lines.
57. 	if canonical, shift := tryLeadingIndentShift(content, oldText); canonical != "" {
58. 		return matchResult{canonical: canonical, transform: "leading-indent-shift", shift: shift}
59. 	}
60. 
61. 	// 5. Trailing-whitespace tolerance: leading whitespace already matches
62. 	// (otherwise step 4 would have caught the mismatch first); only the
63. 	// per-line trailing differs.
64. 	if trimmed := tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch(content, oldText); trimmed != "" {
65. 		return matchResult{canonical: trimmed, transform: "trailing-whitespace-tolerant"}
66. 	}
67. 
68. 	return matchResult{}
69. }
70. 
71. // adjustNewText applies the same transform that was used to find old_text
72. // to new_text, so the replacement remains consistent with the file.
73. func adjustNewText(content, newText string, mr matchResult) string {
74. 	out := newText
75. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "line-numbers-stripped") {
76. 		out = stripLineNumberPrefix(out)
77. 	}
78. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "indent-normalized") {
79. 		out = normalizeIndentation(content, out)
80. 	}
81. 	if mr.transform == "crlf-converted" && !strings.Contains(out, "\r\n") {
82. 		out = strings.ReplaceAll(out, "\n", "\r\n")
83. 	}
84. 	if mr.transform == "leading-indent-shift" && mr.shift != "" {
85. 		out = applyLeadingIndentShift(out, mr.shift)
86. 	}
87. 	return out
88. }
89. 
90. // lineNumberPrefixRE matches prefixes produced by readFileRange:
91. // up to 6 leading spaces, then 1+ digits, then a tab.
92. // We allow more leading spaces in case the LLM reformatted slightly.
93. var lineNumberPrefixRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^\s{0,12}\d+\t`)
94. 
95. // stripLineNumberPrefix removes "  42\t" style prefixes if a clear majority
96. // of non-empty lines have them. This catches the common failure where an
97. // LLM pastes back read_file output (which is line-numbered) verbatim as
98. // old_text.
99. func stripLineNumberPrefix(text string) string {
100. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
101. 	matched, nonEmpty := 0, 0
102. 	for _, l := range lines {
103. 		if strings.TrimSpace(l) == "" {
104. 			continue
105. 		}
106. 		nonEmpty++
107. 		if lineNumberPrefixRE.MatchString(l) {
108. 			matched++
109. 		}
110. 	}
111. 	// Require >=2 lines with prefix AND a strong majority, to avoid false
112. 	// positives on legitimate text that happens to start with digits+TAB.
113. 	if matched < 2 || matched < (nonEmpty/2+1) {
114. 		return text
115. 	}
116. 	out := make([]string, len(lines))
117. 	for i, l := range lines {
118. 		out[i] = lineNumberPrefixRE.ReplaceAllString(l, "")
119. 	}
120. 	return strings.Join(out, "\n")
121. }
122. 
123. // tryCRLFMatch handles the case where the file uses CRLF line endings but
124. // the LLM provided LF-only old_text.
125. func tryCRLFMatch(content, oldText string) string {
126. 	if !strings.Contains(content, "\r\n") {
127. 		return ""
128. 	}
129. 	if strings.Contains(oldText, "\r\n") {
130. 		return ""
131. 	}
132. 	if !strings.Contains(oldText, "\n") {
133. 		return ""
134. 	}
135. 	candidate := strings.ReplaceAll(oldText, "\n", "\r\n")
136. 	if strings.Contains(content, candidate) {
137. 		return candidate
138. 	}
139. 	return ""
140. }
141. 
142. // leadingWhitespace returns the prefix of s that consists of spaces and tabs.
143. func leadingWhitespace(s string) string {
144. 	i := 0
145. 	for i < len(s) && (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t') {
146. 		i++
147. 	}
148. 	return s[:i]
149. }
150. 
151. // tryLeadingIndentShift handles the very common LLM failure where the
152. // model provides the right text but with less (or no) leading indentation
153. // than the file actually uses — e.g. file has "\t\t// foo" but the LLM
154. // supplied just "// foo" as old_text.
155. //
156. // It looks for a contiguous block in content whose lines, after stripping
157. // both leading and trailing whitespace, equal the lines of oldText. To
158. // guard against unrelated false matches it requires:
159. //   - The file's leading whitespace on each non-empty matched line must
160. //     start with the LLM's leading whitespace on the corresponding old
161. //     line (so the shift is well-defined).
162. //   - The "shift" — the extra prefix the file has — must be IDENTICAL on
163. //     every non-empty matched line, preserving the relative indent of the
164. //     LLM's old_text.
165. //
166. // Returns the canonical block from the file plus the shift to prepend to
167. // each line of new_text.
168. func tryLeadingIndentShift(content, oldText string) (canonical, shift string) {
169. 	oldLines := strings.Split(oldText, "\n")
170. 	contentLines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
171. 	if len(oldLines) == 0 || len(oldLines) > len(contentLines) {
172. 		return "", ""
173. 	}
174. 
175. 	stripBoth := func(s string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(s) }
176. 
177. 	sOld := make([]string, len(oldLines))
178. 	firstNonEmpty := -1
179. 	for i, l := range oldLines {
180. 		s := stripBoth(l)
181. 		sOld[i] = s
182. 		if s != "" && firstNonEmpty < 0 {
183. 			firstNonEmpty = i
184. 		}
185. 	}
186. 	if firstNonEmpty < 0 {
187. 		return "", ""
188. 	}
189. 
190. 	for i := 0; i+len(sOld) <= len(contentLines); i++ {
191. 		match := true
192. 		for j := range sOld {
193. 			if stripBoth(contentLines[i+j]) != sOld[j] {
194. 				match = false
195. 				break
196. 			}
197. 		}
198. 		if !match {
199. 			continue
200. 		}
201. 
202. 		baseFile := leadingWhitespace(contentLines[i+firstNonEmpty])
203. 		baseOld := leadingWhitespace(oldLines[firstNonEmpty])
204. 		if !strings.HasPrefix(baseFile, baseOld) {
205. 			continue
206. 		}
207. 		s := baseFile[len(baseOld):]
208. 		if s == "" {
209. 			// No actual shift — exact path or trailing-whitespace path
210. 			// would have already matched. Keep searching for a real
211. 			// shifted occurrence rather than returning a no-op.
212. 			continue
213. 		}
214. 
215. 		// Verify every non-empty line has the same shift on top of the
216. 		// LLM's per-line leading indent.
217. 		consistent := true
218. 		for j := range sOld {
219. 			if sOld[j] == "" {
220. 				continue
221. 			}
222. 			wantOldLead := leadingWhitespace(oldLines[j])
223. 			wantFileLead := s + wantOldLead
224. 			if leadingWhitespace(contentLines[i+j]) != wantFileLead {
225. 				consistent = false
226. 				break
227. 			}
228. 		}
229. 		if !consistent {
230. 			continue
231. 		}
232. 
233. 		return strings.Join(contentLines[i:i+len(sOld)], "\n"), s
234. 	}
235. 	return "", ""
236. }
237. 
238. // applyLeadingIndentShift prepends shift to every non-empty line of text.
239. // Empty lines are left empty so trailing blank lines in new_text don't
240. // gain spurious whitespace.
241. func applyLeadingIndentShift(text, shift string) string {
242. 	if shift == "" {
243. 		return text
244. 	}
245. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
246. 	for i, l := range lines {
247. 		if l == "" {
248. 			continue
249. 		}
250. 		lines[i] = shift + l
251. 	}
252. 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
253. }
254. 
255. // tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch finds oldText in content by ignoring
256. // per-line trailing whitespace differences. Returns the canonical
257. // substring from content (with its real trailing whitespace) so that a
258. // subsequent strings.Replace targets the exact bytes.
259. func tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch(content, oldText string) string {
260. 	contentLines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
261. 	oldLines := strings.Split(oldText, "\n")

263. 		return ""
264. 	}
265. 	rstrip := func(s string) string { return strings.TrimRight(s, " \t") }
266. 	rstripOld := make([]string, len(oldLines))
267. 	for i, l := range oldLines {
268. 		rstripOld[i] = rstrip(l)
269. 	}
270. 	rstripContent := make([]string, len(contentLines))
271. 	for i, l := range contentLines {
272. 		rstripContent[i] = rstrip(l)
273. 	}
274. 
275. 	for i := 0; i+len(rstripOld) <= len(rstripContent); i++ {
276. 		match := true
277. 		for j := range rstripOld {
278. 			if rstripContent[i+j] != rstripOld[j] {
279. 				match = false
280. 				break
