1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"os"
8. 	"strings"
9. )
10. 
11. // EditFile implements the edit_file tool for find-and-replace editing.
12. type EditFile struct {
13. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
14. }
15. 
16. func (t EditFile) Name() string { return "edit_file" }
17. 
18. func (t EditFile) Description() string {
19. 	return "Edit a file by replacing one occurrence of old_text with new_text. " +
20. 		"Rules for high success rate: " +
21. 		"(1) ALWAYS read_file first to get the exact current content. " +
22. 		"(2) Best practice: copy the relevant numbered lines directly from read_file into old_text. The tool understands read_file prefixes like \"   42\\t\" and uses them as anchors, which is especially helpful for single-line edits and duplicate text. " +
23. 		"(3) If you do not use line-number anchors, old_text must still match the file byte-for-byte INCLUDING indentation (tabs vs spaces) and line endings. " +
24. 		"(4) Without line-number anchors, old_text must be UNIQUE in the file; otherwise include 1-3 lines of surrounding context or set replace_all=true. " +
25. 		"(5) On failure, the error message lists hints (indent style, near-matches with whitespace visualised, matching line numbers) — read them and adjust before retrying. " +
26. 		"For multiple edits to the same file in one round-trip, prefer multi_edit_file."
27. }
28. 
29. func (t EditFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
30. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
31. 	"type": "object",
32. 	"properties": {
33. 		"file_path": {
34. 			"type": "string",
35. 			"description": "Path to the file to edit."
36. 		},
37. 		"old_text": {
38. 			"type": "string",
39. 			"description": "Text to find. Recommended: paste the numbered lines directly from read_file; the tool understands line-number prefixes and uses them as anchors. Without line numbers, old_text must match the file byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings)."
40. 		},
41. 		"new_text": {
42. 			"type": "string",
43. 			"description": "Replacement text. If you copied numbered lines from read_file, you may keep or remove those prefixes here; they are stripped automatically."
44. 		},
45. 		"replace_all": {
46. 			"type": "boolean",
47. 			"description": "If true, replace every occurrence of old_text. Default false (the call fails if old_text is not unique)."
48. 		},
49. 		"description": {
50. 			"type": "string",
51. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language (e.g. 'Updating retry policy', '更新缩进')."
52. 		}
53. 	},
54. 	"required": [
55. 		"file_path",
56. 		"old_text",
57. 		"new_text"
58. 	]
59. }`)
60. }
61. 
62. func (t EditFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
63. 	var args struct {
64. 		FilePath   string `json:"file_path"`
65. 		OldText    string `json:"old_text"`
66. 		NewText    string `json:"new_text"`
67. 		ReplaceAll bool   `json:"replace_all"`
68. 	}
69. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
70. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
71. 	}
72. 
73. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.FilePath) {
74. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
75. 	}
76. 
77. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
78. 	if err != nil {
79. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
80. 	}
81. 
82. 	content := string(data)
83. 
84. 	mr := resolveOldText(content, args.OldText)
85. 	if mr.canonical == "" {
86. 		hint := diagnoseMatchFailure(content, args.OldText)
87. 		msg := "old_text not found in file"
88. 		if hint != "" {
89. 			msg += ". " + hint
90. 		}
91. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
92. 	}
93. 	oldText := mr.canonical
94. 
95. 	count := strings.Count(content, oldText)
96. 	if !args.ReplaceAll && count > 1 && !mr.anchored {
97. 		lines := findMatchLineNumbers(content, oldText)
98. 		msg := fmt.Sprintf(
99. 			"old_text found %d times in file — must be unique. Add 1-3 lines of surrounding context to disambiguate, copy the exact numbered lines from read_file to anchor the intended occurrence, or set replace_all=true to replace every occurrence.",
100. 			count,
101. 		)
102. 		if len(lines) > 0 {
103. 			more := ""
104. 			if count > len(lines) {
105. 				more = fmt.Sprintf(" (showing first %d)", len(lines))
106. 			}
107. 			msg += fmt.Sprintf(" Matches start at line(s): %s%s.", formatMatchLines(lines), more)
108. 		}
109. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
110. 	}
111. 
112. 	// Apply the same transform to new_text so the replacement matches the
113. 	// file's conventions (indentation, line endings, line-number stripping).
114. 	newText := args.NewText
115. 	if mr.transform != "" {
116. 		newText = adjustNewText(content, args.NewText, mr)
117. 	}
118. 
119. 	var newContent string
120. 	if args.ReplaceAll {
121. 		newContent = strings.ReplaceAll(content, oldText, newText)
122. 	} else if mr.anchored {
123. 		newContent = content[:mr.start] + newText + content[mr.start+len(oldText):]
124. 	} else {
125. 		newContent = strings.Replace(content, oldText, newText, 1)
126. 	}
127. 
128. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(newContent), 0644); err != nil {
129. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
130. 	}
131. 
132. 	// Build a summary
133. 	oldLines := strings.Count(args.OldText, "\n") + 1
134. 	newLines := strings.Count(args.NewText, "\n") + 1
135. 	if args.ReplaceAll {
136. 		return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Replaced %d occurrence(s) in %s: %d lines -> %d lines", count, args.FilePath, oldLines, newLines)}, nil
137. 	}
138. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Replaced 1 occurrence in %s: %d lines -> %d lines", args.FilePath, oldLines, newLines)}, nil
139. }
140. 
141. // normalizeIndentation converts the indentation of text to match the file's style.
142. // If the file uses tabs and text uses spaces, leading spaces are converted to tabs.
143. // If the file uses spaces and text uses tabs, leading tabs are converted to spaces.
144. // The width parameter is inferred from the file content.
145. func normalizeIndentation(fileContent, text string) string {
146. 	fileUsesTabs := false
147. 	fileTabWidth := 0
148. 	{
149. 		tabLines, spaceLines := 0, 0
150. 		spaceWidths := map[int]int{}
151. 		lines := strings.Split(fileContent, "\n")
152. 		limit := len(lines)
153. 		if limit > 200 {
154. 			limit = 200
155. 		}
156. 		for _, line := range lines[:limit] {
157. 			if len(line) == 0 {
158. 				continue
159. 			}
160. 			if line[0] == '\t' {
161. 				tabLines++
162. 			} else if line[0] == ' ' {
163. 				spaceLines++
164. 				n := 0
165. 				for n < len(line) && line[n] == ' ' {
166. 					n++
167. 				}
168. 				if n >= 2 {
169. 					spaceWidths[n]++
170. 				}
171. 			}
172. 		}
173. 		fileUsesTabs = tabLines > spaceLines
174. 		if len(spaceWidths) > 0 {
175. 			allW := make([]int, 0, len(spaceWidths))
176. 			for w := range spaceWidths {
177. 				allW = append(allW, w)
178. 			}
179. 			g := allW[0]
180. 			for _, w := range allW[1:] {
181. 				g = gcd(g, w)
182. 			}
183. 			if g < 2 {
184. 				g = 2
185. 			}
186. 			fileTabWidth = g
187. 		}
188. 		if fileTabWidth == 0 {
189. 			fileTabWidth = 4
190. 		}
191. 	}
192. 
193. 	// Check what the text uses
194. 	textHasTabs := false
195. 	textHasLeadingSpaces := false
196. 	for _, line := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
197. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == '\t' {
198. 			textHasTabs = true
199. 		}
200. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == ' ' {
201. 			textHasLeadingSpaces = true
202. 		}
203. 	}
204. 
205. 	// File uses tabs, text uses spaces → convert leading spaces to tabs
206. 	if fileUsesTabs && !textHasTabs && textHasLeadingSpaces {
207. 		return convertSpacesToTabs(text, fileTabWidth)
208. 	}
209. 
210. 	// File uses spaces, text uses tabs → convert leading tabs to spaces
211. 	if !fileUsesTabs && textHasTabs {
212. 		return convertTabsToSpaces(text, fileTabWidth)
213. 	}
214. 
215. 	return text
216. }
217. 
218. // convertSpacesToTabs converts leading spaces to tabs based on tabWidth.
219. // For example, with tabWidth=4:
220. //
221. //	"    foo" → "\tfoo"
222. //	"        bar" → "\t\tbar"
223. //	"  baz" → "\tbaz"  (non-multiple: consume one tab for any leading spaces)
224. func convertSpacesToTabs(text string, tabWidth int) string {
225. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
226. 	for i, line := range lines {
227. 		if len(line) == 0 {
228. 			continue
229. 		}
230. 		// Count leading spaces
231. 		spaces := 0
232. 		for spaces < len(line) && line[spaces] == ' ' {
233. 			spaces++
234. 		}
235. 		if spaces == 0 {
236. 			continue
237. 		}
238. 		tabs := spaces / tabWidth
239. 		remainder := spaces % tabWidth
240. 		// Use at least 1 tab if there are any leading spaces
241. 		if tabs == 0 && spaces > 0 {
242. 			tabs = 1
243. 			remainder = 0
244. 		}
245. 		lines[i] = strings.Repeat("\t", tabs) + strings.Repeat(" ", remainder) + line[spaces:]
246. 	}
247. 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
248. }
249. 
250. // convertTabsToSpaces converts leading tabs to spaces based on tabWidth.
251. func convertTabsToSpaces(text string, tabWidth int) string {
252. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
253. 	for i, line := range lines {
254. 		if len(line) == 0 {
255. 			continue
256. 		}
257. 		tabs := 0
258. 		for tabs < len(line) && line[tabs] == '\t' {
259. 			tabs++
260. 		}
261. 		if tabs == 0 {
262. 			continue
263. 		}
264. 		rest := line[tabs:]
265. 		lines[i] = strings.Repeat(" ", tabs*tabWidth) + rest
266. 	}
267. 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
268. }
269. 
270. // diagnoseMatchFailure provides actionable hints when old_text is not found.
271. // It checks for common causes: tab vs space, trailing whitespace, line ending differences.
272. func diagnoseMatchFailure(content, oldText string) string {
273. 	oldHasTabs := strings.Contains(oldText, "\t")
274. 	oldHasLeadingSpaces := false
275. 	for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") {
276. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == ' ' {
277. 			oldHasLeadingSpaces = true
278. 			break
279. 		}
280. 	}
281. 
282. 	fileHasTabs := false
283. 	fileIndentSpaces := 0
284. 	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
285. 	limit := len(lines)
286. 	if limit > 100 {
287. 		limit = 100
288. 	}
289. 	for _, line := range lines[:limit] {
290. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == '\t' {
291. 			fileHasTabs = true
292. 		}
293. 		if len(line) > 0 && line[0] == ' ' {
294. 			fileIndentSpaces++
295. 		}
296. 	}
297. 
298. 	var hints []string
299. 
300. 	if fileHasTabs && !oldHasTabs && oldHasLeadingSpaces {
301. 		hints = append(hints, "file uses tab indentation — use \\t in old_text")
302. 		firstOldLine := strings.Split(oldText, "\n")[0]
303. 		trimmedOld := strings.TrimLeft(firstOldLine, " \t")
304. 		if trimmedOld != "" {
305. 			for _, line := range lines {
306. 				trimmed := strings.TrimLeft(line, " \t")
307. 				if trimmed == trimmedOld {
308. 					visual := visualizeWhitespace(line)
309. 					hints = append(hints, fmt.Sprintf("expected line: %s", visual))
310. 					break
311. 				}
312. 			}
313. 		}
314. 	} else if !fileHasTabs && oldHasTabs && fileIndentSpaces > 0 {
315. 		hints = append(hints, "file uses space indentation — remove \\t from old_text")
316. 	}
317. 
318. 	// Check for CRLF vs LF
319. 	if strings.Contains(content, "\r\n") && !strings.Contains(oldText, "\r\n") {
320. 		hints = append(hints, "file uses CRLF line endings — re-read the file to get exact content")
321. 	}
322. 
323. 	// Check if old_text is close to something in the file (first line matches partially)
324. 	if len(hints) == 0 {
325. 		firstOldLine := strings.Split(oldText, "\n")[0]
326. 		firstOldLine = strings.TrimSpace(firstOldLine)
327. 		if firstOldLine != "" {
328. 			for _, line := range lines {
329. 				trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
330. 				if trimmed == firstOldLine {
331. 					visual := visualizeWhitespace(line)
332. 					hints = append(hints, fmt.Sprintf(
333. 						"first line matches but whitespace differs. Expected line: %s — re-read the file with read_file and copy exact content",
334. 						visual,
335. 					))
336. 					break
337. 				}
338. 			}
339. 		}
340. 	}
341. 
342. 	if len(hints) == 0 {
343. 		hints = append(hints, "re-read the file with read_file and use exact content for old_text")
344. 	}
345. 
346. 	return strings.Join(hints, "; ")
347. }
348. 
349. // visualizeWhitespace replaces tab characters with the literal string "<TAB>"
350. // so the LLM can see the exact whitespace pattern in diagnostic messages.
351. func visualizeWhitespace(line string) string {
352. 	return strings.ReplaceAll(line, "\t", "<TAB>")
353. }
354.
```

</details>

1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"

12. // MultiEditFile applies multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call.
13. type MultiEditFile struct {
14. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
15. }
16. 
17. func (t MultiEditFile) Name() string { return "multi_edit_file" }
18. 
19. func (t MultiEditFile) Description() string {
20. 	return "Apply multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call. " +
21. 		"Each edit follows the same rules as edit_file: the best practice is to paste the numbered lines directly from read_file into each old_text, because the tool uses those line numbers as anchors. Without line-number anchors, every old_text must match the file byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings) and must be UNIQUE in the file. " +
22. 		"Edits must not overlap. All edits are applied atomically — if any single edit fails, the file is left unchanged. " +
23. 		"All old_text matches are resolved against the ORIGINAL file, not after earlier edits in the same call. Always read_file first. " +
24. 		"Use this instead of repeated edit_file calls when changing multiple sites in one file (e.g. renaming a symbol)."
25. }
26. 
27. func (t MultiEditFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
28. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
29. 	"type": "object",
30. 	"properties": {
31. 		"file_path": {
32. 			"type": "string",
33. 			"description": "Path to the file to edit."
34. 		},
35. 		"edits": {
36. 			"type": "array",
37. 			"items": {
38. 				"type": "object",
39. 				"properties": {
40. 					"old_text": {
41. 						"type": "string",
42. 						"description": "Text to find. Recommended: paste the numbered lines directly from read_file; the tool uses those prefixes as anchors. Without line numbers, old_text must match byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings) and be unique in the file."
43. 					},
44. 					"new_text": {
45. 						"type": "string",
46. 						"description": "Replacement text. If you copied numbered lines from read_file, you may keep or remove those prefixes here; they are stripped automatically."
47. 					}
48. 				},
49. 				"required": [
50. 					"old_text",
51. 					"new_text"
52. 				]
53. 			},
54. 			"description": "Edits to apply, in any order. Each old_text must be unique in the file and not overlap with other edits."
55. 		},
56. 		"description": {
57. 			"type": "string",
58. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language."
59. 		}
60. 	},
61. 	"required": [
62. 		"file_path",
63. 		"edits"
64. 	]
65. }`)
66. }
67. 
68. func (t MultiEditFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
69. 	var args struct {
70. 		FilePath string `json:"file_path"`
71. 		Edits    []struct {
72. 			OldText string `json:"old_text"`
73. 			NewText string `json:"new_text"`
74. 		} `json:"edits"`
75. 	}
76. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
77. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
78. 	}
79. 
80. 	if len(args.Edits) == 0 {
81. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "edits array must not be empty"}, nil
82. 	}
83. 
84. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.FilePath) {
85. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
86. 	}
87. 
88. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
89. 	if err != nil {
90. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
91. 	}
92. 
93. 	content := string(data)
94. 
95. 	// Find all match positions first, validate uniqueness.
96. 	type editPos struct {
97. 		start int
98. 		end   int
99. 		idx   int
100. 		old   string
101. 		new_  string
102. 	}
103. 	var positions []editPos
104. 
105. 	for i, edit := range args.Edits {
106. 		if edit.OldText == "" {
107. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("edits[%d]: old_text must not be empty", i)}, nil
108. 		}
109. 		mr := resolveOldText(content, edit.OldText)
110. 		if mr.canonical == "" {
111. 			hint := diagnoseMatchFailure(content, edit.OldText)
112. 			msg := fmt.Sprintf("edits[%d]: old_text not found in file", i)
113. 			if hint != "" {
114. 				msg += ". " + hint
115. 			}
116. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
117. 		}
118. 		oldText := mr.canonical
119. 		count := strings.Count(content, oldText)
120. 		if count > 1 && !mr.anchored {
121. 			lines := findMatchLineNumbers(content, oldText)
122. 			msg := fmt.Sprintf(
123. 				"edits[%d]: old_text found %d times — must be unique. Add 1-3 lines of surrounding context to disambiguate, or copy the exact numbered lines from read_file so this edit is line-number anchored.",
124. 				i, count,
125. 			)
126. 			if len(lines) > 0 {
127. 				more := ""
128. 				if count > len(lines) {
129. 					more = fmt.Sprintf(" (showing first %d)", len(lines))
130. 				}
131. 				msg += fmt.Sprintf(" Matches start at line(s): %s%s.", formatMatchLines(lines), more)
132. 			}
133. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
134. 		}
135. 		idx := mr.start
136. 		if !mr.anchored {
137. 			idx = strings.Index(content, oldText)
138. 		}
139. 		newText := edit.NewText
140. 		if mr.transform != "" {
141. 			newText = adjustNewText(content, edit.NewText, mr)
142. 		}
143. 		positions = append(positions, editPos{
144. 			start: idx,
145. 			end:   idx + len(oldText),
146. 			idx:   i,
147. 			old:   oldText,
148. 			new_:  newText,
149. 		})
150. 	}
151. 
152. 	// Check for overlapping edits.
153. 	sort.Slice(positions, func(i, j int) bool { return positions[i].start < positions[j].start })
154. 	for i := 1; i < len(positions); i++ {
155. 		if positions[i].start < positions[i-1].end {
156. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(
157. 				"edits[%d] and edits[%d]: overlapping matches — each old_text must not overlap",
158. 				positions[i-1].idx, positions[i].idx)}, nil
159. 		}
160. 	}
161. 
162. 	// Apply edits from back to front to preserve earlier positions.
163. 	for i := len(positions) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
164. 		p := positions[i]
165. 		content = content[:p.start] + p.new_ + content[p.end:]
166. 	}
167. 
168. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
169. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
170. 	}
171. 
172. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Applied %d edits to %s", len(args.Edits), args.FilePath)}, nil
173. }
174.

254. func isFileTool(name string) bool {

256. 	case "read_file", "write_file", "edit_file", "list_directory", "search_files", "glob":
257. 		return true
258. 	}
259. 	return false
260. }

278. }

288. func extractFilePath(input json.RawMessage) (string, bool) {
289. 	var m map[string]json.RawMessage
290. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &m); err != nil {
291. 		return "", false
292. 	}
293. 	for _, key := range []string{"file_path", "path", "directory"} {
294. 		if v, ok := m[key]; ok {
295. 			var s string
296. 			if err := json.Unmarshal(v, &s); err == nil {
297. 				return s, true
298. 			}
299. 		}
300. 	}
301. 	return "", false
302. }
303. 
304. func extractCommand(input json.RawMessage) (string, bool) {
305. 	var m map[string]json.RawMessage
306. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &m); err != nil {
307. 		return "", false
308. 	}
309. 	if v, ok := m["command"]; ok {
310. 		var s string
311. 		if err := json.Unmarshal(v, &s); err == nil {
312. 			return s, true
313. 		}
314. 	}
315. 	if v, ok := m["input"]; ok {
316. 		var s string
317. 		if err := json.Unmarshal(v, &s); err == nil {
318. 			return s, true
319. 		}
320. 	}

90. // IsReadOnlyTool returns true if the tool is safe for Plan mode (read-only).
91. func IsReadOnlyTool(name string) bool {
92. 	switch name {
93. 	case "read_file", "list_directory", "search_files", "glob", "grep":
94. 		return true
95. 	case "lsp_hover", "lsp_definition", "lsp_references", "lsp_symbols",
96. 		"lsp_diagnostics", "lsp_workspace_symbols", "lsp_code_actions",
97. 		"lsp_implementation", "lsp_prepare_call_hierarchy",
98. 		"lsp_incoming_calls", "lsp_outgoing_calls":
99. 		return true
100. 	case "sleep", "git_status", "git_diff", "git_log", "git_show",
101. 		"git_blame", "git_branch_list", "git_remote", "git_stash_list",
102. 		"web_fetch", "web_search",
103. 		"task_list", "task_get", "plan_status",
104. 		"cron_list", "list_commands", "read_command_output",
105. 		"wait_command", "get_config":
106. 		return true
107. 	}
108. 	// All MCP tools are allowed in plan mode (they have their own access control)
109. 	if strings.HasPrefix(name, "mcp__") {
110. 		return true
111. 	}
112. 	return false
113. }
114.
```

91. 	if !preResult.Allowed {

93. 	}
94.

97. 		return a.executeFileTool(ctx, t, tc, env)
98. 	}

100. 	// Sync working directory for tools that have a WorkingDir field.

107. 	result, err := a.safeExecute(t, ctx, tc.Arguments)
108. 	if err != nil {
109. 		return tool.Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("tool error: %v", err), IsError: true}
110. 	}
111. 
112. 	// Post-tool-use hooks
113. 	postResult := hooks.RunPostHooks(hookCfg.PostToolUse, env)
114. 	if postResult.Output != "" {
115. 		result.Content += "\n" + postResult.Output
116. 	}
117. 
118. 	return result
119. }
120. 
121. // safeExecute calls t.Execute with panic recovery. If the tool panics
122. // (e.g. nil pointer dereference from an unset dependency), it returns
123. // an error result instead of crashing the entire application.
124. func (a *Agent) safeExecute(t tool.Tool, ctx context.Context, args json.RawMessage) (result tool.Result, err error) {
125. 	defer func() {
126. 		if r := recover(); r != nil {
127. 			debug.Log("agent", "PANIC recovered in tool %s: %v\n%s", t.Name(), r, runtimedebug.Stack())
128. 			result = tool.Result{
129. 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("tool %s panicked: %v — this is a bug, please report it", t.Name(), r),
130. 				IsError: true,
131. 			}
132. 			err = nil
133. 		}
134. 	}()
135. 	return t.Execute(ctx, args)
136. }
137. 
138. // executeFileTool handles edit_file and write_file with diff preview and checkpointing.
139. func (a *Agent) executeFileTool(ctx context.Context, t tool.Tool, tc provider.ToolCallDelta, env hooks.HookEnv) tool.Result {
140. 	a.mu.Lock()
141. 	cpMgr := a.checkpoints
142. 	diffFn := a.diffConfirm
143. 	a.mu.Unlock()
144. 
145. 	// Determine file path and compute old/new content
146. 	filePath, oldContent, newContent, err := a.computeFileChange(tc)
147. 	if err != nil {
148. 		return tool.Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("file change error: %v", err), IsError: true}
149. 	}
150. 
151. 	// Show diff and ask for confirmation if diffConfirm is set
152. 	if diffFn != nil && diff.HasChanges(oldContent, newContent) {
153. 		diffText := diff.UnifiedDiff(oldContent, newContent, 3)
154. 		if !diffFn(ctx, filePath, diffText) {
155. 			return tool.Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("File write to %s cancelled by user.", filePath), IsError: true}
156. 		}
157. 	}
158. 
159. 	// Pre-tool-use hooks
160. 	a.mu.RLock()
161. 	hookCfg2 := a.hookConfig
162. 	a.mu.RUnlock()
163. 	preResult := hooks.RunPreHooks(hookCfg2.PreToolUse, env)
164. 	if !preResult.Allowed {
165. 		return tool.Result{Content: preResult.Output, IsError: true}
166. 	}
167. 
168. 	// Execute the actual tool (with panic recovery)
169. 	result, err := a.safeExecute(t, ctx, tc.Arguments)
170. 	if err != nil {
171. 		return tool.Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("tool error: %v", err), IsError: true}
172. 	}
173. 
174. 	// Save checkpoint
175. 	if cpMgr != nil && !result.IsError {
176. 		cpMgr.Save(filePath, oldContent, newContent, tc.Name)
177. 	}
178. 
179. 	// Post-tool-use hooks
180. 	postResult := hooks.RunPostHooks(hookCfg2.PostToolUse, env)
181. 	if postResult.Output != "" {
182. 		result.Content += "\n" + postResult.Output
183. 	}
184. 
185. 	return result
186. }
187. 
188. // computeFileChange reads the old content and computes the new content for a file tool call.
189. func (a *Agent) computeFileChange(tc provider.ToolCallDelta) (filePath, oldContent, newContent string, err error) {
190. 	switch tc.Name {
191. 	case "edit_file":
192. 		var args struct {
193. 			FilePath string `json:"file_path"`
194. 			OldText  string `json:"old_text"`
195. 			NewText  string `json:"new_text"`
196. 		}
197. 		if err := json.Unmarshal(tc.Arguments, &args); err != nil {
198. 			return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid arguments: %w", err)
199. 		}
200. 		filePath = args.FilePath
201. 		data, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)
202. 		if err != nil {
203. 			// File may not exist yet — that's OK for write_file, but edit_file needs it
204. 			return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot read file: %w", err)
205. 		}
206. 		oldContent = string(data)
207. 		newContent = replaceFirst(oldContent, args.OldText, args.NewText)
208. 		return filePath, oldContent, newContent, nil
209. 
210. 	case "write_file":
211. 		var args struct {
212. 			Path    string `json:"path"`
213. 			Content string `json:"content"`
214. 		}
215. 		if err := json.Unmarshal(tc.Arguments, &args); err != nil {
216. 			return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid arguments: %w", err)
217. 		}
218. 		filePath = args.Path
219. 		data, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)
220. 		if err != nil {
221. 			oldContent = ""
222. 		} else {
223. 			oldContent = string(data)
224. 		}
225. 		newContent = args.Content
226. 		return filePath, oldContent, newContent, nil
227. 
228. 	default:
229. 		return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("not a file tool: %s", tc.Name)
230. 	}
231. }
232. 
233. // replaceFirst replaces the first occurrence of old in s with new.
234. func replaceFirst(s, old, new string) string {
235. 	idx := indexOf(s, old)
236. 	if idx < 0 {
237. 		return s
238. 	}
239. 	return s[:idx] + new + s[idx+len(old):]
240. }
241. 
242. // indexOf returns the index of the first occurrence of substr in s, or -1.
243. func indexOf(s, substr string) int {
244. 	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {
245. 		if s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {
246. 			return i
247. 		}
248. 	}
249. 	return -1
250. }
251. 
252. // toolWorkingDirMu is a safety-net mutex for syncToolWorkingDir. With Registry.Clone(),
253. // each agent has its own tool instances and this mutex should never be contended.
254. // It exists as a last resort in case a tool without a Clone() implementation is
255. // shared between agents and has a WorkingDir field that needs mutation.
256. var toolWorkingDirMu sync.Mutex
257. 
258. // syncToolWorkingDir uses reflection to set the WorkingDir field on tools
259. // that have one. This ensures tools always use the agent's current working
260. // directory, even after it changes (e.g., after enter_worktree).
261. //
262. // Note: With Registry.Clone(), each agent has independent tool instances, so
263. // this reflection is only mutating per-agent copies. The mutex is a safety net.
264. func syncToolWorkingDir(t tool.Tool, dir string) {
265. 	toolWorkingDirMu.Lock()
266. 	defer toolWorkingDirMu.Unlock()
267. 
268. 	// Dereference pointer if needed
269. 	v := reflect.ValueOf(t)
270. 	if v.Kind() == reflect.Ptr {
271. 		if v.IsNil() {
272. 			return
273. 		}
274. 		v = v.Elem()
275. 	}
276. 	if v.Kind() != reflect.Struct {
277. 		return
278. 	}
279. 	f := v.FieldByName("WorkingDir")
280. 	if f.IsValid() && f.CanSet() && f.Kind() == reflect.String {
281. 		f.SetString(dir)
282. 	}
283. }
284.
```

</details>

166. // TestAllWorkingDirToolsImplementCloner is a compile-time/runtime safety check:
167. // every tool type that has a WorkingDir field MUST implement the Cloner interface.
168. // If someone adds a new tool with WorkingDir but forgets Clone(), this test fails.
169. func TestAllWorkingDirToolsImplementCloner(t *testing.T) {
170. 	// List of tool instances that have WorkingDir — must all implement Cloner
171. 	toolsWithWorkingDir := []Tool{
172. 		&RunCommand{},
173. 		&GitStatus{},
174. 		&GitDiff{},
175. 		&GitLog{},

191. 		if _, ok := t2.(Cloner); !ok {
192. 			t.Errorf("%T has WorkingDir but does not implement Cloner — add Clone() Tool method", t2)
193. 		}
194. 	}
195. }

197. // TestCloneFiltersCorrectly tests the pattern used in BuildToolSet:
198. // clone → unregister specific tools → verify only allowed tools remain.
199. func TestCloneFiltersCorrectly(t *testing.T) {
200. 	orig := NewRegistry()
201. 	orig.Register(&RunCommand{WorkingDir: "/base"})
202. 	orig.Register(&GitStatus{WorkingDir: "/base"})
203. 	orig.Register(SpawnAgentTool{})
204. 	orig.Register(SleepTool{})
205. 
206. 	// Clone and remove agent lifecycle tools (like BuildToolSet does)
207. 	cloned := orig.Clone()
208. 	cloned.Unregister("spawn_agent")
209. 	cloned.Unregister("wait_agent")
210. 	cloned.Unregister("list_agents")
211. 
212. 	names := cloned.ToolNames()
213. 	for _, n := range names {
214. 		if n == "spawn_agent" || n == "wait_agent" || n == "list_agents" {
215. 			t.Errorf("agent lifecycle tool %q should have been removed", n)
216. 		}
217. 	}
218. 
219. 	// Run command and git should still be there
220. 	if _, ok := cloned.Get("run_command"); !ok {
221. 		t.Error("run_command should still be in cloned registry")
222. 	}
223. 	if _, ok := cloned.Get("git_status"); !ok {
224. 		t.Error("git_status should still be in cloned registry")
225. 	}
226. 
227. 	// Original should still have spawn_agent
228. 	if _, ok := orig.Get("spawn_agent"); !ok {
229. 		t.Error("original registry should still have spawn_agent")
230. 	}

1. package hooks
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"bytes"
5. 	"fmt"
6. 	"os"
7. 	"os/exec"
8. 	"path/filepath"
9. 	"strings"
10. 
11. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/util"
12. )
13. 
14. // RunPreHooks runs all matching pre-tool-use hooks.
15. // Returns HookResult with Allowed=false if any hook blocks execution.
16. func RunPreHooks(hooks []Hook, env HookEnv) HookResult {
17. 	for _, h := range hooks {
18. 		if !matchTool(h.Match, env.ToolName, env.RawInput) {
19. 			continue
20. 		}
21. 		result := runHook(h.Command, env)
22. 		if !result.Allowed {
23. 			return result
24. 		}
25. 	}
26. 	return HookResult{Allowed: true}
27. }
28. 
29. // RunPostHooks runs all matching post-tool-use hooks.
30. // Collects output from hooks with inject_output=true.
31. func RunPostHooks(hooks []Hook, env HookEnv) HookResult {
32. 	var allOutput strings.Builder
33. 	for _, h := range hooks {
34. 		if !matchTool(h.Match, env.ToolName, env.RawInput) {
35. 			continue
36. 		}
37. 		result := runHook(h.Command, env)
38. 		if result.Err != nil {
39. 			allOutput.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("[hook error: %v]\n", result.Err))
40. 			continue
41. 		}
42. 		if h.InjectOutput && result.Output != "" {
43. 			allOutput.WriteString(result.Output)
44. 			if !strings.HasSuffix(result.Output, "\n") {
45. 				allOutput.WriteString("\n")
46. 			}
47. 		}
48. 	}
49. 	return HookResult{Allowed: true, Output: allOutput.String()}
50. }
51. 
52. // runHook executes a shell command and returns the result.
53. // Exit code 2 means block the tool execution.
54. // Sensitive data (RAW_INPUT) is passed via environment variable to avoid leaking to process list.
55. func runHook(command string, env HookEnv) HookResult {
56. 	cmd := os.Expand(command, func(key string) string {
57. 		switch key {
58. 		case "TOOL_NAME":
59. 			return env.ToolName
60. 		case "FILE_PATH":
61. 			return env.FilePath
62. 		case "WORKING_DIR":
63. 			return env.WorkingDir
64. 		case "RAW_INPUT":
65. 			return env.RawInput
66. 		default:
67. 			return os.Getenv(key)
68. 		}
69. 	})
70. 
71. 	c, _, err := util.NewShellCommand(cmd)
72. 	if err != nil {
73. 		return HookResult{
74. 			Allowed: true,
75. 			Err:     fmt.Errorf("resolve hook shell: %w", err),
76. 		}
77. 	}
78. 	c.Dir = env.WorkingDir
79. 	// Pass RAW_INPUT via environment variable instead of embedding in command string
80. 	c.Env = append(os.Environ(), "GGCODE_RAW_INPUT="+env.RawInput)
81. 	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
82. 	c.Stdout = &stdout
83. 	c.Stderr = &stderr
84. 
85. 	err = c.Run()
86. 	if err != nil {
87. 		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
88. 			if exitErr.ExitCode() == 2 {
89. 				errMsg := stderr.String()
90. 				if errMsg == "" {
91. 					errMsg = stdout.String()
92. 				}
93. 				return HookResult{
94. 					Allowed: false,
95. 					Output:  fmt.Sprintf("Blocked by pre-tool-use hook: %s", strings.TrimSpace(errMsg)),
96. 					Err:     err,
97. 				}
98. 			}
99. 		}
100. 		return HookResult{
101. 			Allowed: true,
102. 			Output:  stdout.String(),
103. 			Err:     fmt.Errorf("hook command failed: %w", err),
104. 		}
105. 	}
106. 
107. 	return HookResult{Allowed: true, Output: stdout.String()}
108. }
109. 
110. // ExtractFilePath attempts to extract a file path from common tool argument patterns.
111. func ExtractFilePath(toolName string, rawInput string) string {
112. 	for _, key := range []string{"file_path", "path", "filename", "file"} {
113. 		idx := strings.Index(rawInput, `"`+key+`"`)
114. 		if idx < 0 {
115. 			continue
116. 		}
117. 		rest := rawInput[idx:]
118. 		colonIdx := strings.Index(rest, ":")
119. 		if colonIdx < 0 {
120. 			continue
121. 		}
122. 		val := strings.TrimSpace(rest[colonIdx+1:])
123. 		val = strings.TrimPrefix(val, `"`)
124. 		val = strings.TrimSuffix(val, `"`)
125. 		if i := strings.Index(val, `"`); i > 0 {
126. 			val = val[:i]
127. 		}
128. 		if val != "" {
129. 			return val
130. 		}
131. 	}
132. 	return ""
133. }
134. 
135. // matchTool checks if a hook's match pattern applies to a tool call.
136. func matchTool(pattern, toolName, rawInput string) bool {
137. 	// Function call pattern: tool_name(args...)
138. 	if parenIdx := strings.Index(pattern, "("); parenIdx > 0 {
139. 		patTool := pattern[:parenIdx]
140. 		patArgs := pattern[parenIdx+1 : len(pattern)-1]
141. 
142. 		if patTool != toolName {
143. 			return false
144. 		}
145. 		if patArgs == "*" || patArgs == "" {
146. 			return true
147. 		}
148. 		if strings.HasSuffix(patArgs, "*") {
149. 			prefix := strings.TrimSuffix(patArgs, "*")
150. 			return strings.Contains(rawInput, prefix)
151. 		}
152. 		return strings.Contains(rawInput, patArgs)
153. 	}
154. 
155. 	// Simple glob match on tool name
156. 	matched, _ := filepath.Match(pattern, toolName)
157. 	if matched {
158. 		return true
159. 	}
160. 
161. 	// Pipe-separated patterns
162. 	if strings.Contains(pattern, "|") {
163. 		for _, p := range strings.Split(pattern, "|") {
164. 			p = strings.TrimSpace(p)
165. 			if m, _ := filepath.Match(p, toolName); m {
166. 				return true
167. 			}
168. 		}
169. 	}
170. 
171. 	return false
172. }
173.
```

</details>

1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"regexp"
6. 	"strings"
7. )
8. 
9. // matchResult describes a successful old_text resolution.
10. type matchResult struct {
11. 	canonical string // the actual bytes in content that will be replaced
12. 	transform string // diagnostic tag for which fallback fired ("" = exact)
13. 	shift     string // for leading-indent-shift: prefix to prepend to new_text lines
14. 	start     int    // byte offset of canonical in content when the match is anchored
15. 	anchored  bool
16. }
17. 
18. // resolveOldText tries multiple strategies to locate oldText in content,
19. // helping weaker LLMs whose `old_text` argument frequently differs from the
20. // file in trivial ways (indentation style, line-number prefix copied from
21. // read_file output, CRLF vs LF, trailing whitespace, missing leading
22. // indentation).
23. //
24. // On success, canonical is the exact substring of content that will be
25. // substituted (so a strings.Replace targets the right bytes). On failure
26. // canonical is "".
27. func resolveOldText(content, oldText string) matchResult {
28. 	if oldText == "" {
29. 		return matchResult{}
30. 	}
31. 	if strings.Contains(content, oldText) {
32. 		return matchResult{canonical: oldText}
33. 	}
34. 	if trimmed, changed := trimReadFileWrapperLines(oldText); changed {
35. 		if mr := resolveOldText(content, trimmed); mr.canonical != "" {
36. 			mr.transform = prependTransform("read-file-wrapper-stripped", mr.transform)
37. 			return mr
38. 		}
39. 	}
40. 
41. 	// 0. read_file line-number anchors. This is the most reliable path for weak
42. 	// models because it lets them paste numbered lines directly from read_file,
43. 	// including single-line edits and duplicate text that would otherwise be
44. 	// ambiguous.
45. 	if anchored := tryReadFileLineAnchor(content, oldText); anchored.canonical != "" {
46. 		return anchored
47. 	}
48. 
49. 	// 1. Indentation normalization (tabs <-> spaces).
50. 	if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, oldText); normalized != oldText && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
51. 		return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "indent-normalized"}
52. 	}
53. 
54. 	// 2. Line-number prefix from read_file output: "   42\t<line>".
55. 	if stripped := stripLineNumberPrefix(oldText); stripped != oldText {
56. 		if strings.Contains(content, stripped) {
57. 			return matchResult{canonical: stripped, transform: "line-numbers-stripped"}
58. 		}
59. 		if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, stripped); normalized != stripped && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
60. 			return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "line-numbers-stripped+indent-normalized"}
61. 		}
62. 	}
63. 
64. 	// 3. CRLF / LF mismatch.
65. 	if crlf := tryCRLFMatch(content, oldText); crlf != "" {
66. 		return matchResult{canonical: crlf, transform: "crlf-converted"}
67. 	}
68. 
69. 	// 4. Leading-indent shift: LLM provided the right text but with less
70. 	// (or no) leading indentation. Match by structural equality after
71. 	// stripping both leading and trailing whitespace, then return the
72. 	// file's actual block plus the shift to prepend to new_text lines.
73. 	if canonical, shift := tryLeadingIndentShift(content, oldText); canonical != "" {
74. 		return matchResult{canonical: canonical, transform: "leading-indent-shift", shift: shift}
75. 	}
76. 
77. 	// 5. Trailing-whitespace tolerance: leading whitespace already matches
78. 	// (otherwise step 4 would have caught the mismatch first); only the
79. 	// per-line trailing differs.
80. 	if trimmed := tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch(content, oldText); trimmed != "" {
81. 		return matchResult{canonical: trimmed, transform: "trailing-whitespace-tolerant"}
82. 	}
83. 
84. 	return matchResult{}
85. }
86. 
87. var readFileLineRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^\s{0,12}(\d+)\t(.*)$`)
88. 
89. type numberedBlock struct {
90. 	startLine int
91. 	lines     []string
92. }
93. 
94. type fileLine struct {
95. 	text  string
96. 	start int
97. 	end   int
98. }
99. 
100. func splitFileLines(content string) []fileLine {
101. 	if content == "" {
102. 		return nil
103. 	}
104. 	lines := make([]fileLine, 0, strings.Count(content, "\n")+1)
105. 	start := 0
106. 	for start < len(content) {
107. 		rel := strings.IndexByte(content[start:], '\n')
108. 		if rel < 0 {
109. 			lines = append(lines, fileLine{
110. 				text:  content[start:],
111. 				start: start,
112. 				end:   len(content),
113. 			})
114. 			break
115. 		}
116. 		end := start + rel
117. 		lines = append(lines, fileLine{
118. 			text:  content[start:end],
119. 			start: start,
120. 			end:   end,
121. 		})
122. 		start = end + 1
123. 	}
124. 	return lines
125. }
126. 
127. func parseReadFileNumberedBlock(text string) (numberedBlock, bool) {
128. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
129. 	if len(lines) == 0 {
130. 		return numberedBlock{}, false
131. 	}
132. 
133. 	body := make([]string, len(lines))
134. 	startLine := 0
135. 	for i, line := range lines {
136. 		m := readFileLineRE.FindStringSubmatch(line)
137. 		if m == nil {
138. 			return numberedBlock{}, false
139. 		}
140. 		n := 0
141. 		fmt.Sscanf(m[1], "%d", &n)
142. 		if n <= 0 {
143. 			return numberedBlock{}, false
144. 		}
145. 		if i == 0 {
146. 			startLine = n
147. 		} else if n != startLine+i {
148. 			return numberedBlock{}, false
149. 		}
150. 		body[i] = m[2]
151. 	}
152. 
153. 	return numberedBlock{
154. 		startLine: startLine,
155. 		lines:     body,
156. 	}, true
157. }
158. 
159. func resolveAnchoredCandidate(candidate, oldText string) matchResult {
160. 	if candidate == oldText {
161. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate}
162. 	}
163. 	if normalized := normalizeIndentation(candidate, oldText); normalized == candidate {
164. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "indent-normalized"}
165. 	}
166. 	if strings.Contains(candidate, "\r\n") && !strings.Contains(oldText, "\r\n") {
167. 		if strings.ReplaceAll(oldText, "\n", "\r\n") == candidate {
168. 			return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "crlf-converted"}
169. 		}
170. 	}
171. 	if canonical, shift := tryLeadingIndentShift(candidate, oldText); canonical == candidate {
172. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "leading-indent-shift", shift: shift}
173. 	}
174. 	if trimmed := tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch(candidate, oldText); trimmed == candidate {
175. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "trailing-whitespace-tolerant"}
176. 	}
177. 	return matchResult{}
178. }
179. 
180. func prependTransform(prefix, suffix string) string {
181. 	switch {
182. 	case prefix == "":
183. 		return suffix
184. 	case suffix == "":
185. 		return prefix
186. 	default:
187. 		return prefix + "+" + suffix
188. 	}
189. }
190. 
191. func tryReadFileLineAnchor(content, oldText string) matchResult {
192. 	block, ok := parseReadFileNumberedBlock(oldText)
193. 	if !ok {
194. 		return matchResult{}
195. 	}
196. 
197. 	lines := splitFileLines(content)
198. 	if block.startLine <= 0 || block.startLine+len(block.lines)-1 > len(lines) {
199. 		return matchResult{}
200. 	}
201. 
202. 	startIdx := block.startLine - 1
203. 	endIdx := startIdx + len(block.lines) - 1
204. 	candidate := content[lines[startIdx].start:lines[endIdx].end]
205. 	mr := resolveAnchoredCandidate(candidate, strings.Join(block.lines, "\n"))
206. 	if mr.canonical == "" {
207. 		return matchResult{}
208. 	}
209. 	mr.canonical = candidate
210. 	mr.transform = prependTransform("line-numbers-stripped", mr.transform)
211. 	mr.start = lines[startIdx].start
212. 	mr.anchored = true
213. 	return mr
214. }
215. 
216. // adjustNewText applies the same transform that was used to find old_text
217. // to new_text, so the replacement remains consistent with the file.
218. func adjustNewText(content, newText string, mr matchResult) string {
219. 	out := newText
220. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "read-file-wrapper-stripped") {
221. 		if trimmed, changed := trimReadFileWrapperLines(out); changed {
222. 			out = trimmed
223. 		}
224. 	}
225. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "line-numbers-stripped") {
226. 		out = stripAllLineNumberPrefixes(out)
227. 	}
228. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "indent-normalized") {
229. 		out = normalizeIndentation(content, out)
230. 	}
231. 	if mr.transform == "crlf-converted" && !strings.Contains(out, "\r\n") {
232. 		out = strings.ReplaceAll(out, "\n", "\r\n")
233. 	}
234. 	if mr.transform == "leading-indent-shift" && mr.shift != "" {
235. 		out = applyLeadingIndentShift(out, mr.shift)
236. 	}
237. 	return out
238. }
239. 
240. // lineNumberPrefixRE matches prefixes produced by readFileRange:
241. // up to 6 leading spaces, then 1+ digits, then a tab.
242. // We allow more leading spaces in case the LLM reformatted slightly.
243. var lineNumberPrefixRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^\s{0,12}\d+\t`)
244. var readFileWrapperLineRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^\[(indent:|encoding:|Extracted from |File truncated:|File has )`)
245. 
246. // stripLineNumberPrefix removes "  42\t" style prefixes if a clear majority
247. // of non-empty lines have them. This catches the common failure where an
248. // LLM pastes back read_file output (which is line-numbered) verbatim as
249. // old_text.
250. func stripLineNumberPrefix(text string) string {
251. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
252. 	matched, nonEmpty := 0, 0
253. 	for _, l := range lines {
254. 		if strings.TrimSpace(l) == "" {
255. 			continue
256. 		}
257. 		nonEmpty++
258. 		if lineNumberPrefixRE.MatchString(l) {
259. 			matched++
260. 		}
```

```
260. 		}
261. 	}
262. 	// Require >=2 lines with prefix AND a strong majority, to avoid false
263. 	// positives on legitimate text that happens to start with digits+TAB.
264. 	if matched < 2 || matched < (nonEmpty/2+1) {
265. 		return text
266. 	}
267. 	out := make([]string, len(lines))
268. 	for i, l := range lines {
269. 		out[i] = lineNumberPrefixRE.ReplaceAllString(l, "")
270. 	}
271. 	return strings.Join(out, "\n")
272. }
273. 
274. func stripAllLineNumberPrefixes(text string) string {
275. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
276. 	for i, l := range lines {
277. 		lines[i] = lineNumberPrefixRE.ReplaceAllString(l, "")
278. 	}
279. 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
280. }
281. 
282. func trimReadFileWrapperLines(text string) (string, bool) {
283. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
284. 	start, end := 0, len(lines)
285. 	for start < end && readFileWrapperLineRE.MatchString(lines[start]) {
286. 		start++
287. 	}
288. 	for end > start && readFileWrapperLineRE.MatchString(lines[end-1]) {
289. 		end--
290. 	}
291. 	if start == 0 && end == len(lines) {
292. 		return text, false
293. 	}
294. 	trimmed := strings.Join(lines[start:end], "\n")
295. 	if trimmed == "" {
296. 		return text, false
297. 	}
298. 	return trimmed, true
299. }
300. 
301. // tryCRLFMatch handles the case where the file uses CRLF line endings but
302. // the LLM provided LF-only old_text.
303. func tryCRLFMatch(content, oldText string) string {
304. 	if !strings.Contains(content, "\r\n") {
305. 		return ""
306. 	}
307. 	if strings.Contains(oldText, "\r\n") {
308. 		return ""
309. 	}
310. 	if !strings.Contains(oldText, "\n") {
311. 		return ""
312. 	}
313. 	candidate := strings.ReplaceAll(oldText, "\n", "\r\n")
314. 	if strings.Contains(content, candidate) {
315. 		return candidate
316. 	}
317. 	return ""
318. }
319. 
320. // leadingWhitespace returns the prefix of s that consists of spaces and tabs.
321. func leadingWhitespace(s string) string {
322. 	i := 0
323. 	for i < len(s) && (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t') {
324. 		i++
325. 	}
326. 	return s[:i]
327. }
328. 
329. // tryLeadingIndentShift handles the very common LLM failure where the
330. // model provides the right text but with less (or no) leading indentation
331. // than the file actually uses — e.g. file has "\t\t// foo" but the LLM
332. // supplied just "// foo" as old_text.
333. //
334. // It looks for a contiguous block in content whose lines, after stripping
335. // both leading and trailing whitespace, equal the lines of oldText. To
336. // guard against unrelated false matches it requires:
337. //   - The file's leading whitespace on each non-empty matched line must
338. //     start with the LLM's leading whitespace on the corresponding old
339. //     line (so the shift is well-defined).
340. //   - The "shift" — the extra prefix the file has — must be IDENTICAL on
341. //     every non-empty matched line, preserving the relative indent of the
342. //     LLM's old_text.
343. //
344. // Returns the canonical block from the file plus the shift to prepend to
345. // each line of new_text.
346. func tryLeadingIndentShift(content, oldText string) (canonical, shift string) {
347. 	oldLines := strings.Split(oldText, "\n")
348. 	contentLines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
349. 	if len(oldLines) == 0 || len(oldLines) > len(contentLines) {
350. 		return "", ""
351. 	}
352. 
353. 	stripBoth := func(s string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(s) }
354. 
355. 	sOld := make([]string, len(oldLines))
356. 	firstNonEmpty := -1
357. 	for i, l := range oldLines {
358. 		s := stripBoth(l)
359. 		sOld[i] = s
360. 		if s != "" && firstNonEmpty < 0 {
361. 			firstNonEmpty = i
362. 		}
363. 	}
364. 	if firstNonEmpty < 0 {
365. 		return "", ""
366. 	}
367. 
368. 	for i := 0; i+len(sOld) <= len(contentLines); i++ {
369. 		match := true
370. 		for j := range sOld {
371. 			if stripBoth(contentLines[i+j]) != sOld[j] {
372. 				match = false
373. 				break
374. 			}
375. 		}
376. 		if !match {
377. 			continue
378. 		}
379. 
380. 		baseFile := leadingWhitespace(contentLines[i+firstNonEmpty])
381. 		baseOld := leadingWhitespace(oldLines[firstNonEmpty])
382. 		if !strings.HasPrefix(baseFile, baseOld) {
383. 			continue
384. 		}
385. 		s := baseFile[len(baseOld):]
386. 		if s == "" {
387. 			// No actual shift — exact path or trailing-whitespace path
388. 			// would have already matched. Keep searching for a real
389. 			// shifted occurrence rather than returning a no-op.
390. 			continue
391. 		}
392. 
393. 		// Verify every non-empty line has the same shift on top of the
394. 		// LLM's per-line leading indent.
395. 		consistent := true
396. 		for j := range sOld {
397. 			if sOld[j] == "" {
398. 				continue
399. 			}
400. 			wantOldLead := leadingWhitespace(oldLines[j])
401. 			wantFileLead := s + wantOldLead
402. 			if leadingWhitespace(contentLines[i+j]) != wantFileLead {
403. 				consistent = false
404. 				break
405. 			}
406. 		}
407. 		if !consistent {
408. 			continue
409. 		}
410. 
411. 		return strings.Join(contentLines[i:i+len(sOld)], "\n"), s
412. 	}
413. 	return "", ""
414. }
415. 
416. // applyLeadingIndentShift prepends shift to every non-empty line of text.
417. // Empty lines are left empty so trailing blank lines in new_text don't
418. // gain spurious whitespace.
419. func applyLeadingIndentShift(text, shift string) string {
420. 	if shift == "" {
```

23. // DiffConfirmFunc is called before a file write to request user confirmation.
24. // It receives a context, the file path and unified diff string, and returns
25. // true if approved. Implementations MUST honor ctx.Done() so the agent
26. // goroutine doesn't leak when the TUI shuts down while a confirmation is in
27. // flight.
28. type DiffConfirmFunc func(ctx context.Context, filePath, diffText string) bool
29. 
30. // ApprovalFunc is called when a tool requires interactive approval. It MUST
31. // honor ctx.Done() to avoid a goroutine leak if the TUI exits while a
32. // permission prompt is awaiting user input.
33. type ApprovalFunc func(ctx context.Context, toolName string, input string) permission.Decision
34. 
35. type interruptionHandler func() string
36. type runResultHandler func([]provider.ContentBlock, error)
37. 
38. var errStreamInterruptedForReplan = errors.New("stream interrupted for replan")
39. 
40. // Agent orchestrates the agentic loop: send messages to LLM, execute tool calls, loop.
41. type Agent struct {
42. 	provider       provider.Provider
43. 	tools          *tool.Registry
44. 	contextManager ctxpkg.ContextManager
45. 	maxIter        int
46. 	policy         permission.PermissionPolicy
47. 	onApproval     ApprovalFunc
48. 	onUsage        func(usage provider.TokenUsage)
49. 	onCheckpoint   func(messages []provider.Message, tokenCount int)
50. 	onRunResult    runResultHandler
51. 	hookConfig     hooks.HookConfig
52. 	workingDir     string
53. 	checkpoints    *checkpoint.Manager
54. 	diffConfirm    DiffConfirmFunc
55. 	onInterrupt    interruptionHandler
56. 	projectMemory  map[string]struct{}
57. 	supportsVision bool
58. 	precompact     *precompactState
59. 	shutdownCtx    context.Context
60. 	shutdownCancel context.CancelFunc // cancels on Close()
61. 	probeKey       string             // "vendor|baseURL|model" for context window auto-detection
62. 	mu             sync.RWMutex
63. }
64. 
65. type providerAwareContextManager interface {
66. 	SetProvider(provider.Provider)
67. }
68. 
69. type usageAwareContextManager interface {
70. 	RecordUsage(provider.TokenUsage)
71. }
72. 
73. type todoPathAwareContextManager interface {
74. 	SetTodoFilePath(path string)
75. }
76. 
77. type modeAwarePolicy interface {
78. 	Mode() permission.PermissionMode
79. }
80. 
81. // NewAgent creates a new agent with optional permission policy.
82. func NewAgent(p provider.Provider, tools *tool.Registry, systemPrompt string, maxIter int) *Agent {
83. 	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
84. 	a := &Agent{
85. 		provider:       p,
86. 		tools:          tools,
87. 		maxIter:        maxIter,
88. 		contextManager: ctxpkg.NewManager(128000),
89. 		projectMemory:  make(map[string]struct{}),
90. 		shutdownCtx:    ctx,
91. 		shutdownCancel: cancel,
92. 	}
93. 	a.syncContextManagerProviderLocked()
94. 	a.syncContextManagerTodoPathLocked()
95. 	if systemPrompt != "" {
96. 		a.contextManager.Add(provider.Message{
97. 			Role:    "system",
98. 			Content: []provider.ContentBlock{{Type: "text", Text: systemPrompt}},
99. 		})
100. 	}
101. 	return a
102. }
103. 
104. // SetProbeKey sets the probe cache key ("vendor|baseURL|model") used for
105. // context window auto-detection from overflow errors.
106. func (a *Agent) SetProbeKey(key string) {
107. 	a.mu.Lock()
108. 	defer a.mu.Unlock()
109. 	a.probeKey = key
110. }
111.

113. func (a *Agent) SetPermissionPolicy(policy permission.PermissionPolicy) {
114. 	a.mu.Lock()
115. 	defer a.mu.Unlock()
116. 	a.policy = policy
117. }

1. package checkpoint
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"crypto/rand"
5. 	"encoding/hex"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"sync"
8. 	"time"
9. 
10. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/util"
11. )
12. 
13. // Checkpoint represents a saved file state before a tool edit.
14. type Checkpoint struct {
15. 	ID         string    `json:"id"`
16. 	FilePath   string    `json:"file_path"`
17. 	OldContent string    `json:"old_content"`
18. 	NewContent string    `json:"new_content"`
19. 	Timestamp  time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
20. 	ToolCall   string    `json:"tool_call"`
21. }
22. 
23. // Manager manages file checkpoints for undo support.
24. type Manager struct {
25. 	checkpoints    []Checkpoint
26. 	maxCheckpoints int
27. 	mu             sync.Mutex
28. }
29. 
30. // NewManager creates a new checkpoint manager with the given max limit.
31. func NewManager(maxCheckpoints int) *Manager {
32. 	if maxCheckpoints <= 0 {
33. 		maxCheckpoints = 50
34. 	}
35. 	return &Manager{maxCheckpoints: maxCheckpoints}
36. }
37. 
38. // Save records a checkpoint before a file edit.
39. func (m *Manager) Save(filePath, oldContent, newContent, toolCall string) Checkpoint {
40. 	m.mu.Lock()
41. 	defer m.mu.Unlock()
42. 
43. 	cp := Checkpoint{
44. 		ID:         generateID(),
45. 		FilePath:   filePath,
46. 		OldContent: oldContent,
47. 		NewContent: newContent,
48. 		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
49. 		ToolCall:   toolCall,
50. 	}
51. 
52. 	m.checkpoints = append(m.checkpoints, cp)
53. 
54. 	// Evict oldest if over limit
55. 	if len(m.checkpoints) > m.maxCheckpoints {
56. 		m.checkpoints = m.checkpoints[len(m.checkpoints)-m.maxCheckpoints:]
57. 	}
58. 
59. 	return cp
60. }
61. 
62. // Undo rolls back the most recent checkpoint by writing OldContent back to the file.
63. func (m *Manager) Undo() (*Checkpoint, error) {
64. 	m.mu.Lock()
65. 	defer m.mu.Unlock()
66. 
67. 	if len(m.checkpoints) == 0 {
68. 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no checkpoints to undo")
69. 	}
70. 
71. 	cp := &m.checkpoints[len(m.checkpoints)-1]
72. 
73. 	if err := util.AtomicWriteFile(cp.FilePath, []byte(cp.OldContent), 0644); err != nil {
74. 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to write file: %w", err)
75. 	}
76. 
77. 	m.checkpoints = m.checkpoints[:len(m.checkpoints)-1]
78. 	return cp, nil
79. }
80. 
81. // Revert rolls back to a specific checkpoint by ID, writing OldContent back to the file.
82. // It also removes all checkpoints newer than the target.
83. func (m *Manager) Revert(id string) (*Checkpoint, error) {
84. 	m.mu.Lock()
85. 	defer m.mu.Unlock()
86. 
87. 	idx := -1
88. 	for i, cp := range m.checkpoints {
89. 		if cp.ID == id {
90. 			idx = i
91. 			break
92. 		}
93. 	}
94. 
95. 	if idx < 0 {
96. 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("checkpoint %q not found", id)
97. 	}
98. 
99. 	cp := &m.checkpoints[idx]
100. 
101. 	if err := util.AtomicWriteFile(cp.FilePath, []byte(cp.OldContent), 0644); err != nil {
102. 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to write file: %w", err)
103. 	}
104. 
105. 	m.checkpoints = m.checkpoints[:idx]
106. 	return cp, nil
107. }
108. 
109. // List returns all checkpoints (most recent last).
110. func (m *Manager) List() []Checkpoint {
111. 	m.mu.Lock()
112. 	defer m.mu.Unlock()
113. 
114. 	out := make([]Checkpoint, len(m.checkpoints))
115. 	copy(out, m.checkpoints)
116. 	return out
117. }
118. 
119. // Last returns the most recent checkpoint, or nil if empty.
120. func (m *Manager) Last() *Checkpoint {
121. 	m.mu.Lock()
122. 	defer m.mu.Unlock()
123. 
124. 	if len(m.checkpoints) == 0 {
125. 		return nil
126. 	}
127. 	return &m.checkpoints[len(m.checkpoints)-1]
128. }
129. 
130. // Clear removes all checkpoints.
131. func (m *Manager) Clear() {
132. 	m.mu.Lock()
133. 	defer m.mu.Unlock()
134. 	m.checkpoints = nil
135. }
136. 
137. func generateID() string {
138. 	b := make([]byte, 8)
139. 	_, _ = rand.Read(b)
140. 	return hex.EncodeToString(b)
141. }
142.
```

</details>

8. func TestDangerousDetector_Critical(t *testing.T) {
9. 	d := NewDangerousDetector()
10. 	criticals := []string{
11. 		"rm -rf /",
12. 		"rm -rf /*",
13. 		"mkfs /dev/sda1",
14. 		"dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda",
15. 		"shred -n 3 /etc/passwd",
16. 		":(){ :|:& };:",
17. 		"chmod -R 777 /",
18. 	}
19. 	for _, cmd := range criticals {
20. 		if !d.IsDangerous(cmd) {
21. 			t.Errorf("expected %q to be dangerous", cmd)
22. 		}
23. 		check := d.Check(cmd)
24. 		if check.Level < DangerCritical {
25. 			t.Errorf("expected %q to be critical, got %s", cmd, check.Level)
26. 		}
27. 	}
28. }
29. 
30. func TestDangerousDetector_High(t *testing.T) {
31. 	d := NewDangerousDetector()
32. 	highs := []string{
33. 		"sudo rm -rf /home",
34. 		"sudo mkfs",
35. 		"kill -9 -1",
36. 		"systemctl stop sshd",
37. 		"iptables -F",
38. 	}
39. 	for _, cmd := range highs {
40. 		if !d.IsDangerous(cmd) {
41. 			t.Errorf("expected %q to be dangerous", cmd)
42. 		}
43. 	}
44. }
45. 
46. func TestDangerousDetector_Safe(t *testing.T) {
47. 	d := NewDangerousDetector()
48. 	safe := []string{
49. 		"ls -la",
50. 		"cat file.txt",
51. 		"echo hello",
52. 		"go build ./...",
53. 		"git status",
54. 		"pwd",
55. 	}
56. 	for _, cmd := range safe {
57. 		if d.IsDangerous(cmd) {
58. 			check := d.Check(cmd)
59. 			t.Errorf("expected %q to be safe, but got %s: %s", cmd, check.Level, check.Reason)
60. 		}
61. 	}
62. }
63. 
64. func TestDangerousDetector_Medium(t *testing.T) {
65. 	d := NewDangerousDetector()
66. 	mediums := []string{

156. func TestConfigPolicy_DangerousCommand(t *testing.T) {
157. 	rules := map[string]Decision{"run_command": Allow}
158. 	p := NewConfigPolicy(rules, nil)
159. 	d, err := p.Check("run_command", json.RawMessage(`{"command":"rm -rf /"}`))
160. 	if err != nil {
161. 		t.Fatal(err)
162. 	}
163. 	if d != Deny {
164. 		t.Errorf("expected Deny for dangerous command, got %s", d)
165. 	}
166. }

168. func TestConfigPolicy_SetOverride(t *testing.T) {
169. 	p := NewConfigPolicy(nil, nil)
170. 	if p.GetDecision("read_file") != Ask {
171. 		t.Error("expected default Ask")
172. 	}
173. 	p.SetOverride("read_file", Allow)
174. 	if p.GetDecision("read_file") != Allow {
175. 		t.Error("expected Allow after override")
176. 	}

179. func TestConfigPolicy_ClearOverride(t *testing.T) {
180. 	p := NewConfigPolicy(nil, nil)
181. 	p.SetOverride("write_file", Deny)
182. 	if p.GetDecision("write_file") != Deny {
183. 		t.Error("expected Deny after override")
184. 	}
185. 	p.ClearOverride("write_file")
186. 	if p.GetDecision("write_file") == Deny {
187. 		t.Error("expected Deny to be cleared")
188. 	}
189. }
190. 
191. func TestConfigPolicy_IsDangerous(t *testing.T) {
192. 	p := NewConfigPolicy(nil, nil)
193. 	if !p.IsDangerous("rm -rf /") {
194. 		t.Error("expected rm -rf / to be dangerous")
195. 	}
196. 	if p.IsDangerous("ls") {
197. 		t.Error("expected ls to not be dangerous")
198. 	}
199. }
200. 
201. func TestConfigPolicy_ReadOnlySandboxAppliesOnlyToReadTools(t *testing.T) {
202. 	p := NewConfigPolicyWithModeAndReadOnlyDirs(nil, []string{"/tmp/worktree"}, []string{"/tmp/project/.ggcode"}, AutoMode)
203. 	if !p.AllowedPathForTool("read_file", "/tmp/project/.ggcode/harness.yaml") {
204. 		t.Fatal("expected read_file to allow read-only sandbox path")
205. 	}
206. 	if !p.AllowedPathForTool("glob", "/tmp/project/.ggcode") {
207. 		t.Fatal("expected glob to allow read-only sandbox path")
208. 	}
209. 	// In non-plan modes, AllowedPathForTool defers to the permission layer.
210. 	// If execution reaches here, the permission layer has already approved,
211. 	// so write_file is allowed even for read-only sandbox paths.
212. 	if !p.AllowedPathForTool("write_file", "/tmp/project/.ggcode/harness.yaml") {
213. 		t.Fatal("expected write_file to allow in non-plan mode after permission approval")
214. 	}
215. 	if !p.AllowedPathForTool("edit_file", "/tmp/project/.ggcode/harness.yaml") {
216. 		t.Fatal("expected edit_file to allow in non-plan mode after permission approval")
217. 	}
218. 
219. 	// In PlanMode, strict sandbox enforcement applies — write tools are denied
220. 	pp := NewConfigPolicyWithModeAndReadOnlyDirs(nil, []string{"/tmp/worktree"}, []string{"/tmp/project/.ggcode"}, PlanMode)
221. 	if pp.AllowedPathForTool("write_file", "/tmp/project/.ggcode/harness.yaml") {
222. 		t.Fatal("expected write_file to deny read-only sandbox path in PlanMode")
223. 	}
224. 	if pp.AllowedPathForTool("edit_file", "/tmp/project/.ggcode/harness.yaml") {
225. 		t.Fatal("expected edit_file to deny read-only sandbox path in PlanMode")
226. 	}
227. }
228.
```

</details>

25. func TestAgent_Provider(t *testing.T) {
26. 	original := &mockProvider{}
27. 	a := NewAgent(original, tool.NewRegistry(), "", 1)
28. 	got := a.Provider()
29. 	if got == nil {
30. 		t.Fatal("Provider() returned nil after construction")
31. 	}
32. 	// Verify it returns the same instance we passed in
33. 	if got != original {
34. 		t.Fatal("Provider() returned a different instance than the one passed to NewAgent")
35. 	}
36. 	// Verify setter updates the instance
37. 	replacement := &mockProvider{tokenCount: 42}
38. 	a.SetProvider(replacement)
39. 	if a.Provider() == original {
40. 		t.Fatal("Provider() still returns old instance after SetProvider")
41. 	}
42. }

183. func TestAgent_SetDiffConfirm(t *testing.T) {
184. 	a := NewAgent(&mockProvider{}, tool.NewRegistry(), "", 1)
185. 	// Verify the callback is actually wired through by executing a write_file
186. 	// that triggers the diff confirm flow.
187. 	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
188. 	filePath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "test.txt")
189. 	os.WriteFile(filePath, []byte("original"), 0644)
190. 
191. 	registry := tool.NewRegistry()
192. 	writeTool := &fileWriteTool{}
193. 	if err := registry.Register(writeTool); err != nil {
194. 		t.Fatalf("register: %v", err)
195. 	}
196. 	a.tools = registry
197. 
198. 	var capturedPath, capturedDiff string
199. 	a.SetDiffConfirm(func(_ context.Context, filePath, diffText string) bool {
200. 		capturedPath = filePath
201. 		capturedDiff = diffText
202. 		return true
203. 	})
204. 
205. 	result := a.executeFileTool(context.Background(), writeTool, provider.ToolCallDelta{
206. 		ID:        "c1",
207. 		Name:      "write_file",
208. 		Arguments: json.RawMessage(fmt.Sprintf(`{"path":%q,"content":"modified"}`, filePath)),
209. 	}, hooks.HookEnv{})
210. 	if result.IsError {
211. 		t.Fatalf("expected success, got error: %s", result.Content)
212. 	}
213. 	if capturedPath != filePath {
214. 		t.Fatalf("expected diff confirm called with path %q, got %q", filePath, capturedPath)
215. 	}
216. 	if !strings.Contains(capturedDiff, "modified") {
217. 		t.Fatalf("expected diff to contain 'modified', got %q", capturedDiff)
218. 	}
219. }

221. func TestAgent_SetHookConfig(t *testing.T) {
222. 	a := NewAgent(&mockProvider{}, tool.NewRegistry(), "", 1)
223. 	// Verify hook config is wired through by setting a pre-tool-use hook
224. 	// that blocks execution with a specific message.
225. 	registry := tool.NewRegistry()
226. 	if err := registry.Register(mockTool{name: "echo", result: tool.Result{Content: "ok"}}); err != nil {
227. 		t.Fatalf("register: %v", err)
228. 	}
229. 	a.tools = registry
230. 
231. 	a.SetHookConfig(hooks.HookConfig{
232. 		PreToolUse: []hooks.Hook{
233. 			{Match: "echo", Command: "echo blocked"},
234. 		},
235. 	})
236. 	// Hooks.RunPreHooks requires a real command executor; with an empty
237. 	// command in test env the hook may not block. Instead, verify the
238. 	// config is stored by checking that a round-trip through the agent's
239. 	// internal state is consistent: execute the tool and confirm it
240. 	// doesn't crash with the configured hooks.
241. 	result := a.executeTool(context.Background(), provider.ToolCallDelta{
242. 		ID:        "c1",
243. 		Name:      "echo",
244. 		Arguments: json.RawMessage(`{}`),
245. 	})
246. 	// The tool should either succeed (hook doesn't block) or return a
247. 	// hook-related message — but it must not panic.
248. 	_ = result
249. }
250.

8. func TestPermissionModeString(t *testing.T) {
9. 	tests := []struct {
10. 		mode PermissionMode
11. 		want string
12. 	}{
13. 		{SupervisedMode, "supervised"},
14. 		{PlanMode, "plan"},
15. 		{AutoMode, "auto"},
16. 		{BypassMode, "bypass"},
17. 		{AutopilotMode, "autopilot"},
18. 		{PermissionMode(99), "supervised"},
19. 	}
20. 	for _, tt := range tests {
21. 		if got := tt.mode.String(); got != tt.want {
22. 			t.Errorf("PermissionMode(%d).String() = %q, want %q", tt.mode, got, tt.want)
23. 		}
24. 	}

35. 		{"auto", AutoMode},
36. 		{"bypass", BypassMode},
37. 		{"autopilot", AutopilotMode},

39. 		{"unknown", SupervisedMode},
40. 	}
41. 	for _, tt := range tests {
42. 		if got := ParsePermissionMode(tt.input); got != tt.want {
43. 			t.Errorf("ParsePermissionMode(%q) = %v, want %v", tt.input, got, tt.want)
44. 		}
45. 	}
46. }

48. func TestPermissionModeNext(t *testing.T) {
49. 	if SupervisedMode.Next() != PlanMode {
50. 		t.Error("supervised.Next() should be plan")
51. 	}
52. 	if PlanMode.Next() != AutoMode {
53. 		t.Error("plan.Next() should be auto")
54. 	}
55. 	if AutoMode.Next() != BypassMode {
56. 		t.Error("auto.Next() should be bypass")
57. 	}
58. 	if BypassMode.Next() != AutopilotMode {
59. 		t.Error("bypass.Next() should be autopilot")
60. 	}
61. 	if AutopilotMode.Next() != SupervisedMode {
62. 		t.Error("autopilot.Next() should be supervised")
63. 	}
64. }

66. func TestIsReadOnlyTool(t *testing.T) {
67. 	readOnly := []string{"read_file", "list_directory", "search_files", "grep"}
68. 	write := []string{"write_file", "edit_file", "run_command", "start_command", "write_command_input", "bash"}
69. 	for _, name := range readOnly {
70. 		if !IsReadOnlyTool(name) {
71. 			t.Errorf("IsReadOnlyTool(%q) should be true", name)
72. 		}
73. 	}
74. 	for _, name := range write {
75. 		if IsReadOnlyTool(name) {
76. 			t.Errorf("IsReadOnlyTool(%q) should be false", name)
77. 		}
78. 	}
79. }

81. func TestPlanModeDeniesWrites(t *testing.T) {
82. 	policy := NewConfigPolicyWithMode(nil, []string{"."}, PlanMode)
83. 
84. 	readInput := json.RawMessage(`{"file_path":"test.go"}`)
85. 	writeInput := json.RawMessage(`{"file_path":"test.go","content":"hello"}`)
86. 	cmdInput := json.RawMessage(`{"command":"rm -rf /"}`)
87. 
88. 	// Read tools should be allowed
89. 	d, err := policy.Check("read_file", readInput)
90. 	if err != nil || d != Allow {
91. 		t.Errorf("PlanMode: read_file should be Allow, got %v err=%v", d, err)
92. 	}
93. 
94. 	// Write tools should be denied
95. 	d, err = policy.Check("write_file", writeInput)
96. 	if err != nil || d != Deny {
97. 		t.Errorf("PlanMode: write_file should be Deny, got %v err=%v", d, err)
98. 	}
99. 
100. 	// Run command should be denied
101. 	d, err = policy.Check("run_command", cmdInput)
102. 	if err != nil || d != Deny {
103. 		t.Errorf("PlanMode: run_command should be Deny, got %v err=%v", d, err)
104. 	}
105. 	d, err = policy.Check("start_command", cmdInput)
106. 	if err != nil || d != Deny {
107. 		t.Errorf("PlanMode: start_command should be Deny, got %v err=%v", d, err)
108. 	}
109. 	d, err = policy.Check("write_command_input", json.RawMessage(`{"job_id":"cmd-1","input":"rm -rf /"}`))
110. 	if err != nil || d != Deny {
111. 		t.Errorf("PlanMode: write_command_input should be Deny, got %v err=%v", d, err)
112. 	}
113. }
114. 
115. func TestAutoModeDeniesDangerous(t *testing.T) {
116. 	policy := NewConfigPolicyWithMode(nil, []string{"."}, AutoMode)
117. 
118. 	safeInput := json.RawMessage(`{"command":"ls -la"}`)
119. 	dangerousInput := json.RawMessage(`{"command":"rm -rf /"}`)
120. 
121. 	d, err := policy.Check("run_command", safeInput)
122. 	if err != nil || d != Allow {
123. 		t.Errorf("AutoMode: safe command should be Allow, got %v err=%v", d, err)
124. 	}
125. 	d, err = policy.Check("start_command", safeInput)
126. 	if err != nil || d != Allow {
127. 		t.Errorf("AutoMode: safe start_command should be Allow, got %v err=%v", d, err)
128. 	}
129. 	d, err = policy.Check("write_command_input", json.RawMessage(`{"job_id":"cmd-1","input":"echo ok"}`))
130. 	if err != nil || d != Allow {
131. 		t.Errorf("AutoMode: safe write_command_input should be Allow, got %v err=%v", d, err)
132. 	}
133. 
134. 	d, err = policy.Check("run_command", dangerousInput)
135. 	if err != nil || d != Deny {
136. 		t.Errorf("AutoMode: dangerous command should be Deny, got %v err=%v", d, err)
137. 	}
138. 	d, err = policy.Check("start_command", dangerousInput)
139. 	if err != nil || d != Deny {
140. 		t.Errorf("AutoMode: dangerous start_command should be Deny, got %v err=%v", d, err)

**view the file at /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/read_file_range.go.**

/Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/read_file_range.go (lines 1-120)

<details>
<summary>59 lines</summary>

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"strings"
6. )
7. 
8. const maxOutputLines = 2000
9. 
10. // readFileRange formats content with cat -n style line numbers, returning
11. // lines [offset, offset+limit) from the full content.
12. // offset is 1-based; 0 or 1 means start from the beginning.
13. // limit <= 0 means read to end (capped at maxOutputLines).
14. func readFileRange(content string, offset, limit int, totalLines int) string {
15. 	lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
16. 
17. 	// Handle trailing newline: strings.Split on "abc\n" gives ["abc", ""]
18. 	if len(lines) > 0 && lines[len(lines)-1] == "" {
19. 		lines = lines[:len(lines)-1]
20. 	}
21. 	totalLines = len(lines)
22. 
23. 	// Convert 1-based offset to 0-based index
24. 	startIdx := offset - 1
25. 	if startIdx < 0 {
26. 		startIdx = 0
27. 	}
28. 	if startIdx >= totalLines {
29. 		return fmt.Sprintf("[File has %d lines. Offset %d is beyond end.]", totalLines, offset)
30. 	}
31. 
32. 	// Determine end index
33. 	endIdx := totalLines
34. 	if limit > 0 {
35. 		endIdx = startIdx + limit
36. 		if endIdx > totalLines {
37. 			endIdx = totalLines
38. 		}
39. 	} else if startIdx == 0 {
40. 		// No limit specified and starting from beginning: cap at maxOutputLines
41. 		if endIdx > maxOutputLines {
42. 			endIdx = maxOutputLines
43. 		}
44. 	}
45. 
46. 	var buf strings.Builder
47. 	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
48. 		fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "%6d\t%s\n", i+1, lines[i])
49. 	}
50. 
51. 	// Truncation notice
52. 	if endIdx < totalLines {
53. 		fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "[File truncated: showing lines %d-%d of %d. Use read_file with offset/limit for more.]\n",
54. 			startIdx+1, endIdx, totalLines)
55. 	}
56. 
57. 	return buf.String()
58. }
59.
```

</details>

0 or 1 means start from the beginning.\n // limit <= 0 means read to end (capped at maxOutputLines).\n func readFileRange(content string, offset, limit int, totalLines int) string {\n+\treturn readFileRangeWithOptions(content, offset, limit, readFileRangeOptions{\n+\t\tdefaultLimit: maxOutputLines,\n+\t\tmoreHint:     \"Use read_file with offset/limit for more.\",\n+\t})\n+}\n+\n+func readFileRangeWithOptions(content string, offset, limit int, opts readFileRangeOptions) string {\n \tlines := strings.Split(content, \"\\n\")\n \n \t// Handle trailing newline: strings.Split on \"abc\\n\" gives [\"abc\", \"\"]\n \tif len(lines) > 0 && lines[len(lines)-1] == \"\" {\n \t\tlines = lines[:len(lines)-1]\n \t}\n-\ttotalLines = len(lines)\n+\ttotalLines := len(lines)\n+\tif opts.defaultLimit <= 0 {\n+\t\topts.defaultLimit = maxOutputLines\n+\t}\n+\tif opts.moreHint == \"\" {\n+\t\topts.moreHint = \"Use read_file with offset/limit for more.\"\n+\t}\n \n \t// Convert 1-based offset to 0-based index\n \tstartIdx := offset - 1\n \tif startIdx < 0 {\n@@\n \tif limit > 0 {\n \t\tendIdx = startIdx + limit\n \t\tif endIdx > totalLines {\n \t\t\tendIdx = totalLines\n \t\t}\n \t} else if startIdx == 0 {\n-\t\t// No limit specified and starting from beginning: cap at maxOutputLines\n-\t\tif endIdx > maxOutputLines {\n-\t\t\tendIdx = maxOutputLines\n+\t\t// No limit specified and starting from beginning: cap at defaultLimit.\n+\t\tif endIdx > opts.defaultLimit {\n+\t\t\tendIdx = opts.defaultLimit\n \t\t}\n \t}\n@@\n \t// Truncation notice\n \tif endIdx < totalLines {\n-\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[File truncated: showing lines %d-%d of %d. Use read_file with offset/limit for more.]\\n\",\n-\t\t\tstartIdx+1, endIdx, totalLines)\n+\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[File truncated: showing lines %d-%d of %d. %s]\\n\",\n+\t\t\tstartIdx+1, endIdx, totalLines, opts.moreHint)\n \t}\n \n \treturn buf.String()\n }\n*** End Patch\n"
