Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language.\"\n+\t\t}\n+\t},\n+\t\"required\": [\"files\"]\n+}`)\n+}\n+\n+func (t MultiFileEdit) PreviewChanges(input json.RawMessage) ([]PlannedFileEdit, error) {\n+\tmode, entries, errRes := t.parseInput(input)\n+\tif errRes != nil {\n+\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(errRes.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tplans, _, hasFailures := t.planEntries(entries)\n+\tif mode == \"atomic\" && hasFailures {\n+\t\treturn nil, nil\n+\t}\n+\treturn plans, nil\n+}\n+\n+func (t MultiFileEdit) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {\n+\t_ = ctx\n+\tmode, entries, errRes := t.parseInput(input)\n+\tif errRes != nil {\n+\t\treturn *errRes, nil\n+\t}\n+\tplans, results, hasFailures := t.planEntries(entries)\n+\tbyPath := make(map[string]int, len(results))\n+\tfor i, r := range results {\n+\t\tbyPath[r.Path] = i\n+\t}\n+\n+\tout := MultiFileEditContent{\n+\t\tMode:         mode,\n+\t\tPlannedFiles: len(plans),\n+\t\tResults:      results,\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif mode == \"atomic\" && hasFailures {\n+\t\tfor _, plan := range plans {\n+\t\t\tidx := byPath[plan.Path]\n+\t\t\tout.Results[idx].Status = \"planned\"\n+\t\t\tout.Results[idx].AppliedEditCount = plan.AppliedEditCount\n+\t\t\tout.SkippedPaths = append(out.SkippedPaths, plan.Path)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tout.FailedFiles = countMultiFileEditStatus(out.Results, \"error\")\n+\t\tout.SkippedFiles = len(out.SkippedPaths)\n+\t\tout.FailedPaths = collectMultiFileEditPaths(out.Results, \"error\")\n+\t\tout.Summary = fmt.Sprintf(\"Requested %d files: 0 written, %d failed, %d skipped by atomic mode\", len(entries), out.FailedFiles, out.SkippedFiles)\n+\t\tcontent, err := json.Marshal(out)\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\treturn Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"error marshaling result: %v\", err)}, nil\n+\t\t}\n+\t\treturn Result{IsError: true, Content: string(content)}, nil\n+\t}\n+\n+\tif mode == \"atomic\" {\n+\t\tif failedPath, writeErr := applyAtomicPlans(plans); writeErr != nil {\n+\t\t\tfor _, plan := range plans {\n+\t\t\t\tidx := byPath[plan.Path]\n+\t\t\t\tif plan.Path == failedPath {\n+\t\t\t\t\tout.Results[idx].Status = \"error\"\n+\t\t\t\t\tout.Results[idx].Error = writeErr.Error()\n+\t\t\t\t\tout.FailedPaths = append(out.FailedPaths, plan.Path)\n+\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t\tout.Results[idx].Status = \"planned\"\n+\t\t\t\tout.Results[idx].AppliedEditCount = plan.AppliedEditCount\n+\t\t\t\tout.SkippedPaths = append(out.SkippedPaths, plan.Path)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tout.FailedFiles = len(out.FailedPaths)\n+\t\t\tout.SkippedFiles = len(out.SkippedPaths)\n+\t\t\tout.Summary = fmt.Sprintf(\"Requested %d files: 0 written, %d failed, %d skipped by atomic mode\", len(entries), out.FailedFiles, out.SkippedFiles)\n+\t\t\tcontent, err := json.Marshal(out)\n+\t\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\treturn Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"error marshaling result: %v\", err)}, nil\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\treturn Result{IsError: true, Content: string(content)}, nil\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tfor _, plan := range plans {\n+\t\t\tidx := byPath[plan.Path]\n+\t\t\tout.Results[idx].Status = \"success\"\n+\t\t\tout.Results[idx].AppliedEditCount = plan.AppliedEditCount\n+\t\t\tout.WrittenPaths = append(out.WrittenPaths, plan.Path)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tout.WrittenFiles = len(out.WrittenPaths)\n+\t\tout.Applied = true\n+\t\tout.Summary = fmt.Sprintf(\"Requested %d files: %d written, 0 failed, 0 skipped\", len(entries), out.WrittenFiles)\n+\t\tcontent, err := json.Marshal(out)\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\treturn Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"error marshaling result: %v\", err)}, nil\n+\t\t}\n+\t\treturn Result{IsError: false, Content: string(content)}, nil\n+\t}\n+\n+\tplanByPath := make(map[string]PlannedFileEdit, len(plans))\n+\tfor _, plan := range plans {\n+\t\tplanByPath[plan.Path] = plan\n+\t}\n+\tfor _, plan := range plans {\n+\t\tif err := atomicWriteFile(plan.Path, []byte(plan.NewContent), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tidx := byPath[plan.Path]\n+\t\t\tout.Results[idx].Status = \"error\"\n+\t\t\tout.Results[idx].Error = fmt.Sprintf(\"error writing file: %v\", err)\n+\t\t\tout.FailedPaths = append(out.FailedPaths, plan.Path)\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tidx := byPath[plan.Path]\n+\t\tout.Results[idx].Status = \"success\"\n+\t\tout.Results[idx].AppliedEditCount = plan.AppliedEditCount\n+\t\tout.WrittenPaths = append(out.WrittenPaths, plan.Path)\n+\t}\n+\tout.WrittenFiles = len(out.WrittenPaths)\n+\tout.FailedFiles = countMultiFileEditStatus(out.Results, \"error\")\n+\tfor _, result := range out.Results {\n+\t\tif result.Status == \"error\" && result.Path != \"\" && !containsString(out.FailedPaths, result.Path) {\n+\t\t\tout.FailedPaths = append(out.FailedPaths, result.Path)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\tout.Applied = out.FailedFiles == 0 && out.WrittenFiles == len(entries)\n+\tout.Summary = fmt.Sprintf(\"Requested %d files: %d written, %d failed, %d skipped\", len(entries), out.WrittenFiles, out.FailedFiles, out.SkippedFiles)\n+\tcontent, err := json.Marshal(out)\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\treturn Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"error marshaling result: %v\", err)}, nil\n+\t}\n+\treturn Result{IsError: out.FailedFiles > 0, Content: string(content)}, nil\n+}\n+\n+type multiFileEditEntry struct {\n+\tPath  string\n+\tEdits []textEdit\n+}\n+\n+func (t MultiFileEdit) parseInput(input json.RawMessage) (string, []multiFileEditEntry, *Result) {\n+\tvar args struct {\n+\t\tMode  string `json:\"mode\"`\n+\t\tFiles []struct {\n+\t\t\tPath  string `json:\"path\"`\n+\t\t\tEdits []struct {\n+\t\t\t\tOldText string `json:\"old_text\"`\n+\t\t\t\tNewText string `json:\"new_text\"`\n+\t\t\t} `json:\"edits\"`\n+\t\t} `json:\"files\"`\n+\t}\n+\tif err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {\n+\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid input: %v\", err)}\n+\t}\n+\tmode := args.Mode\n+\tif mode == \"\" {\n+\t\tmode = \"atomic\"\n+\t}\n+\tif mode != \"atomic\" && mode != \"partial_success\" {\n+\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported mode %q\", mode)}\n+\t}\n+\tif len(args.Files) == 0 {\n+\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: \"files array must not be empty\"}\n+\t}\n+\tif len(args.Files) > maxMultiFileEditFiles {\n+\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"too many files: got %d, max %d. Split the edit into smaller batches.\", len(args.Files), maxMultiFileEditFiles)}\n+\t}\n+\ttotalEdits := 0\n+\ttotalPayloadBytes := 0\n+\tentries := make([]multiFileEditEntry, len(args.Files))\n+\tseen := map[string]struct{}{}\n+\tfor i, f := range args.Files {\n+\t\tpath, err := cleanAbsolutePath(f.Path)\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"files[%d]: %v\", i, err)}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif _, ok := seen[path]; ok {\n+\t\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"files[%d]: duplicate path %s\", i, path)}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tseen[path] = struct{}{}\n+\t\tif len(f.Edits) == 0 {\n+\t\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"files[%d].edits must not be empty\", i)}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif len(f.Edits) > maxMultiFileEditEditsPerFile {\n+\t\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"files[%d]: too many edits: got %d, max %d\", i, len(f.Edits), maxMultiFileEditEditsPerFile)}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tentry := multiFileEditEntry{Path: path, Edits: make([]textEdit, len(f.Edits))}\n+\t\tfor j, edit := range f.Edits {\n+\t\t\ttotalEdits++\n+\t\t\ttotalPayloadBytes += len(edit.OldText) + len(edit.NewText)\n+\t\t\tif edit.OldText == \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"files[%d].edits[%d]: old_text must not be empty\", i, j)}\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tentry.Edits[j] = textEdit{OldText: edit.OldText, NewText: edit.NewText}\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tentries[i] = entry\n+\t}\n+\tif totalEdits > maxMultiFileEditTotalEdits {\n+\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"too many edits: got %d, max %d. Split the edit into smaller batches.\", totalEdits, maxMultiFileEditTotalEdits)}\n+\t}\n+\tif totalPayloadBytes > maxMultiFileEditPayloadBytes {\n+\t\treturn \"\", nil, &Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf(\"combined old_text/new_text payload too large (%d bytes, max %d). Split the edit into smaller batches.\", totalPayloadBytes, maxMultiFileEditPayloadBytes)}\n+\t}\n+\treturn mode, entries, nil\n+}\n+\n+func (t MultiFileEdit) planEntries(entries []multiFileEditEntry) ([]PlannedFileEdit, []MultiFileEditFileResult, bool) {\n+\tplans := make([]PlannedFileEdit, 0, len(entries))\n+\tresults := make([]MultiFileEditFileResult, len(entries))\n+\thasFailures := false\n+\tfor i, entry := range entries {\n+\t\tresults[i].Path = entry.Path\n+\t\tif t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(entry.Path) {\n+\t\t\tresults[i].Status = \"error\"\n+\t\t\tresults[i].Error = \"Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy\"\n+\t\t\thasFailures = true\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(entry.Path)\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\tresults[i].Status = \"error\"\n+\t\t\tresults[i].Error = fmt.Sprintf(\"error reading file: %v\", err)\n+\t\t\thasFailures = true\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\toldContent := string(data)\n+\t\tnewContent, applied, msg := planTextEdits(oldContent, entry.Edits)\n+\t\tif msg != \"\" {\n+\t\t\tresults[i].Status = \"error\"\n+\t\t\tresults[i].Error = msg\n+\t\t\thasFailures = true\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tplans = append(plans, PlannedFileEdit{\n+\t\t\tPath:             entry.Path,\n+\t\t\tOldContent:       oldContent,\n+\t\t\tNewContent:       newContent,\n+\t\t\tAppliedEditCount: applied,\n+\t\t})\n+\t}\n+\treturn plans, results, hasFailures\n+}\n+\n+func applyAtomicPlans(plans []PlannedFileEdit) (string, error) {\n+\twritten := make([]PlannedFileEdit, 0, len(plans))\n+\tfor _, plan := range plans {\n+\t\tif err := atomicWriteFile(plan.Path, []byte(plan.NewContent), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tfor i := len(written) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n+\t\t\t\t_ = atomicWriteFile(written[i].Path, []byte(written[i].OldContent), 0644)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\treturn plan.Path, fmt.Errorf(\"error writing file: %v\", err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\twritten = append(written, plan)\n+\t}\n+\treturn \"\", nil\n+}\n+\n+func formatMultiFileReadFileBlock(path, text string) string {\n+\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"=== FILE: %s ===\\n%s[end file]\\n\", path, ensureTrailingNewline(text))\n+}\n+\n+func formatMultiFileReadErrorBlock(path, errText string) string {\n+\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"=== ERROR: %s ===\\n%s\\n[end error]\\n\", path, strings.TrimSpace(errText))\n+}\n+\n+func formatMultiFileReadSkippedBlock(path string) string {\n+\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"=== FILE: %s ===\\n[skipped: combined output limit reached; split into a smaller batch]\\n[end file]\\n\", path)\n+}\n+\n+func ensureTrailingNewline(s string) string {\n+\tif strings.HasSuffix(s, \"\\n\") {\n+\t\treturn s\n+\t}\n+\treturn s + \"\\n\"\n+}\n+\n+func countMultiFileEditStatus(results []MultiFileEditFileResult, status string) int {\n+\tcount := 0\n+\tfor _, result := range results {\n+\t\tif result.Status == status {\n+\t\t\tcount++\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\treturn count\n+}\n+\n+func collectMultiFileEditPaths(results []MultiFileEditFileResult, status string) []string {\n+\tvar paths []string\n+\tfor _, result := range results {\n+\t\tif result.Status == status && result.Path != \"\" {\n+\t\t\tpaths = append(paths, result.Path)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\treturn paths\n+}\n+\n+func containsString(values []string, want string) bool {\n+\tfor _, value := range values {\n+\t\tif value == want {\n+\t\t\treturn true\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+\treturn false\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

943. type askAlwaysPolicy struct{}

947. }

949. func (p *askAlwaysPolicy) AllowedPath(path string) bool                              { return true }

966. // fileWriteTool is a test tool that simulates write_file.
967. type fileWriteTool struct{}
968. 
969. func (t *fileWriteTool) Name() string                { return "write_file" }
970. func (t *fileWriteTool) Description() string         { return "test write_file" }
971. func (t *fileWriteTool) Parameters() json.RawMessage { return json.RawMessage(`{"type":"object"}`) }
972. func (t *fileWriteTool) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (tool.Result, error) {
973. 	var args struct {
974. 		Path    string `json:"path"`
975. 		Content string `json:"content"`
976. 	}
977. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
978. 		return tool.Result{Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}, nil
979. 	}
980. 	if err := os.WriteFile(args.Path, []byte(args.Content), 0644); err != nil {
981. 		return tool.Result{Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}, nil
982. 	}
983. 	return tool.Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Wrote %d bytes to %s", len(args.Content), args.Path)}, nil
984. }
985. 
986. // fileEditTool is a test tool that simulates edit_file.
987. type fileEditTool struct{}
988. 
989. func (t *fileEditTool) Name() string                { return "edit_file" }
990. func (t *fileEditTool) Description() string         { return "test edit_file" }
991. func (t *fileEditTool) Parameters() json.RawMessage { return json.RawMessage(`{"type":"object"}`) }
992. func (t *fileEditTool) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (tool.Result, error) {
993. 	var args struct {
994. 		FilePath string `json:"file_path"`
995. 		OldText  string `json:"old_text"`
996. 		NewText  string `json:"new_text"`
997. 	}
998. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
999. 		return tool.Result{Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}, nil
1000. 	}
1001. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
1002. 	if err != nil {
1003. 		return tool.Result{Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}, nil
1004. 	}
1005. 	newContent := strings.Replace(string(data), args.OldText, args.NewText, 1)
1006. 	if err := os.WriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(newContent), 0644); err != nil {
1007. 		return tool.Result{Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}, nil
1008. 	}
1009. 	return tool.Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Edited %s", args.FilePath)}, nil
1010. }
1011.

1},\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": missing},\n+\t\t},\n+\t})\n+\tres, err := MultiFileRead{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif res.IsError {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected success, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(res.Content, \"[multi_file_read summary] requested=3 succeeded=2 failed=1 skipped=0\") {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected summary: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tfirst := strings.Index(res.Content, \"=== FILE: \"+a+\" ===\")\n+\tsecond := strings.Index(res.Content, \"=== FILE: \"+b+\" ===\")\n+\tthird := strings.Index(res.Content, \"=== ERROR: \"+missing+\" ===\")\n+\tif !(first >= 0 && second > first && third > second) {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected blocks in input order, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(res.Content, \"     1\\tpackage main\") {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected numbered output for first file, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(res.Content, \"[indent: 2 spaces]\") || !strings.Contains(res.Content, \"     2\\t  key: value\") {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected metadata and offset/limit range for second file, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+func TestMultiFileRead_RejectsBadPathsAndLimits(t *testing.T) {\n+\tinput, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{\n+\t\t\"files\": []map[string]any{\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": \"relative.txt\"},\n+\t\t},\n+\t})\n+\tres, err := MultiFileRead{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !res.IsError || !strings.Contains(res.Content, \"path must be absolute\") {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected absolute-path error, got: %+v\", res)\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+func TestMultiFileRead_CombinedOutputLimitSkipsRemaining(t *testing.T) {\n+\tdir := t.TempDir()\n+\tmakeBig := func(name string) string {\n+\t\tfp := filepath.Join(dir, name)\n+\t\tlines := make([]string, 220)\n+\t\tfor i := range lines {\n+\t\t\tlines[i] = strings.Repeat(\"x\", 900)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(fp, []byte(strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\treturn fp\n+\t}\n+\ta := makeBig(\"a.txt\")\n+\tb := makeBig(\"b.txt\")\n+\tc := makeBig(\"c.txt\")\n+\tinput, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{\n+\t\t\"files\": []map[string]any{\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": a},\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": b},\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": c},\n+\t\t},\n+\t})\n+\tres, err := MultiFileRead{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif res.IsError {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected runtime limit to be reported in-band, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(res.Content, \"[skipped: combined output limit reached; split into a smaller batch]\") {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected skipped marker, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tif !strings.Contains(res.Content, \"skipped=\") {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected summary with skipped count, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+func TestMultiFileEdit_AtomicPlanningFailureDoesNotWrite(t *testing.T) {\n+\tdir := t.TempDir()\n+\ta := filepath.Join(dir, \"a.txt\")\n+\tb := filepath.Join(dir, \"b.txt\")\n+\tif err := os.WriteFile(a, []byte(\"hello\\n\"), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif err := os.WriteFile(b, []byte(\"world\\n\"), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\n+\tinput, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{\n+\t\t\"files\": []map[string]any{\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": a, \"edits\": []map[string]string{{\"old_text\": \"hello\", \"new_text\": \"HELLO\"}}},\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": b, \"edits\": []map[string]string{{\"old_text\": \"missing\", \"new_text\": \"WORLD\"}}},\n+\t\t},\n+\t})\n+\tres, err := MultiFileEdit{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !res.IsError {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected atomic planning failure to be an error, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tvar out MultiFileEditContent\n+\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(res.Content), &out); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected JSON content, got %q: %v\", res.Content, err)\n+\t}\n+\tif out.WrittenFiles != 0 || out.FailedFiles != 1 || out.SkippedFiles != 1 || out.PlannedFiles != 1 {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected result counts: %+v\", out)\n+\t}\n+\tgotA, _ := os.ReadFile(a)\n+\tgotB, _ := os.ReadFile(b)\n+\tif string(gotA) != \"hello\\n\" || string(gotB) != \"world\\n\" {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"atomic failure should not write files, got a=%q b=%q\", gotA, gotB)\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+func TestMultiFileEdit_PartialSuccessWritesAndReports(t *testing.T) {\n+\tdir := t.TempDir()\n+\ta := filepath.Join(dir, \"a.txt\")\n+\tb := filepath.Join(dir, \"b.txt\")\n+\tif err := os.WriteFile(a, []byte(\"hello\\n\"), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif err := os.WriteFile(b, []byte(\"world\\n\"), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\n+\tinput, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{\n+\t\t\"mode\": \"partial_success\",\n+\t\t\"files\": []map[string]any{\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": a, \"edits\": []map[string]string{{\"old_text\": \"hello\", \"new_text\": \"HELLO\"}}},\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": b, \"edits\": []map[string]string{{\"old_text\": \"missing\", \"new_text\": \"WORLD\"}}},\n+\t\t},\n+\t})\n+\tres, err := MultiFileEdit{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif !res.IsError {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected partial success with a failure to set IsError, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tvar out MultiFileEditContent\n+\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(res.Content), &out); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected JSON content, got %q: %v\", res.Content, err)\n+\t}\n+\tif out.WrittenFiles != 1 || out.FailedFiles != 1 || len(out.WrittenPaths) != 1 || out.WrittenPaths[0] != a {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected partial-success result: %+v\", out)\n+\t}\n+\tgotA, _ := os.ReadFile(a)\n+\tgotB, _ := os.ReadFile(b)\n+\tif string(gotA) != \"HELLO\\n\" || string(gotB) != \"world\\n\" {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected file contents: a=%q b=%q\", gotA, gotB)\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+func TestMultiFileEdit_IgnoresMultiFileReadWrappers(t *testing.T) {\n+\tdir := t.TempDir()\n+\tfp := filepath.Join(dir, \"test.go\")\n+\tif err := os.WriteFile(fp, []byte(\"package main\\n\\nfunc main() {\\n\\tprintln(\\\"hello\\\")\\n}\\n\"), 0644); err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\n+\toldText := \"[multi_file_read summary] requested=1 succeeded=1 failed=0 skipped=0\\n\\n=== FILE: \" + fp + \" ===\\n     4\\t\\tprintln(\\\"hello\\\")\\n[end file]\"\n+\tnewText := \"=== FILE: \" + fp + \" ===\\n     4\\t\\tprintln(\\\"world\\\")\\n[end file]\"\n+\tinput, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{\n+\t\t\"files\": []map[string]any{\n+\t\t\t{\"path\": fp, \"edits\": []map[string]string{{\"old_text\": oldText, \"new_text\": newText}}},\n+\t\t},\n+\t})\n+\tres, err := MultiFileEdit{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)\n+\tif err != nil {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n+\t}\n+\tif res.IsError {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected wrapper lines to be ignored, got: %s\", res.Content)\n+\t}\n+\tgot, _ := os.ReadFile(fp)\n+\tif !strings.Contains(string(got), `println(\"world\")`) {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected file to be updated, got: %q\", got)\n+\t}\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

226. func (t MultiFileEdit) PreviewChanges(input json.RawMessage) ([]PlannedFileEdit, error) {
227. 	mode, entries, errRes := t.parseInput(input)
228. 	if errRes != nil {
229. 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errRes.Content)

233. 		return nil, nil
234. 	}
235. 	return plans, nil
236. }

185. func TestMultiFileEdit_IgnoresMultiFileReadWrappers(t *testing.T) {
186. 	dir := t.TempDir()
187. 	fp := filepath.Join(dir, "test.go")
188. 	if err := os.WriteFile(fp, []byte("package main\n\nfunc main() {\n\tprintln(\"hello\")\n}\n"), 0644); err != nil {
189. 		t.Fatal(err)
190. 	}
191. 
192. 	oldText := "[multi_file_read summary] requested=1 succeeded=1 failed=0 skipped=0\n\n=== FILE: " + fp + " ===\n     4\t\tprintln(\"hello\")\n[end file]"
193. 	newText := "=== FILE: " + fp + " ===\n     4\t\tprintln(\"world\")\n[end file]"
194. 	input, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
195. 		"files": []map[string]any{
196. 			{"path": fp, "edits": []map[string]string{{"old_text": oldText, "new_text": newText}}},
197. 		},
198. 	})
199. 	res, err := MultiFileEdit{}.Execute(context.Background(), input)
200. 	if err != nil {
201. 		t.Fatal(err)
202. 	}
203. 	if res.IsError {
204. 		t.Fatalf("expected wrapper lines to be ignored, got: %s", res.Content)
205. 	}
206. 	got, _ := os.ReadFile(fp)
207. 	if !strings.Contains(string(got), `println("world")`) {
208. 		t.Fatalf("expected file to be updated, got: %q", got)
209. 	}
210. }
211.

1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"regexp"
6. 	"strings"
7. )
8. 
9. // matchResult describes a successful old_text resolution.
10. type matchResult struct {
11. 	canonical string // the actual bytes in content that will be replaced
12. 	transform string // diagnostic tag for which fallback fired ("" = exact)
13. 	shift     string // for leading-indent-shift: prefix to prepend to new_text lines
14. 	start     int    // byte offset of canonical in content when the match is anchored
15. 	anchored  bool
16. }
17. 
18. // resolveOldText tries multiple strategies to locate oldText in content,
19. // helping weaker LLMs whose `old_text` argument frequently differs from the
20. // file in trivial ways (indentation style, line-number prefix copied from
21. // read_file output, CRLF vs LF, trailing whitespace, missing leading
22. // indentation).
23. //
24. // On success, canonical is the exact substring of content that will be
25. // substituted (so a strings.Replace targets the right bytes). On failure
26. // canonical is "".
27. func resolveOldText(content, oldText string) matchResult {
28. 	if oldText == "" {
29. 		return matchResult{}
30. 	}
31. 	if strings.Contains(content, oldText) {
32. 		return matchResult{canonical: oldText}
33. 	}
34. 	if trimmed, changed := trimReadFileWrapperLines(oldText); changed {
35. 		if mr := resolveOldText(content, trimmed); mr.canonical != "" {
36. 			mr.transform = prependTransform("read-file-wrapper-stripped", mr.transform)
37. 			return mr
38. 		}
39. 	}
40. 
41. 	// 0. read_file line-number anchors. This is the most reliable path for weak
42. 	// models because it lets them paste numbered lines directly from read_file,
43. 	// including single-line edits and duplicate text that would otherwise be
44. 	// ambiguous.
45. 	if anchored := tryReadFileLineAnchor(content, oldText); anchored.canonical != "" {
46. 		return anchored
47. 	}
48. 
49. 	// 1. Indentation normalization (tabs <-> spaces).
50. 	if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, oldText); normalized != oldText && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
51. 		return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "indent-normalized"}
52. 	}
53. 
54. 	// 2. Line-number prefix from read_file output: "   42\t<line>".
55. 	if stripped := stripLineNumberPrefix(oldText); stripped != oldText {
56. 		if strings.Contains(content, stripped) {
57. 			return matchResult{canonical: stripped, transform: "line-numbers-stripped"}
58. 		}
59. 		if normalized := normalizeIndentation(content, stripped); normalized != stripped && strings.Contains(content, normalized) {
60. 			return matchResult{canonical: normalized, transform: "line-numbers-stripped+indent-normalized"}
61. 		}
62. 	}
63. 
64. 	// 3. CRLF / LF mismatch.
65. 	if crlf := tryCRLFMatch(content, oldText); crlf != "" {
66. 		return matchResult{canonical: crlf, transform: "crlf-converted"}
67. 	}
68. 
69. 	// 4. Leading-indent shift: LLM provided the right text but with less
70. 	// (or no) leading indentation. Match by structural equality after
71. 	// stripping both leading and trailing whitespace, then return the
72. 	// file's actual block plus the shift to prepend to new_text lines.
73. 	if canonical, shift := tryLeadingIndentShift(content, oldText); canonical != "" {
74. 		return matchResult{canonical: canonical, transform: "leading-indent-shift", shift: shift}
75. 	}
76. 
77. 	// 5. Trailing-whitespace tolerance: leading whitespace already matches
78. 	// (otherwise step 4 would have caught the mismatch first); only the
79. 	// per-line trailing differs.
80. 	if trimmed := tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch(content, oldText); trimmed != "" {
81. 		return matchResult{canonical: trimmed, transform: "trailing-whitespace-tolerant"}
82. 	}
83. 
84. 	return matchResult{}
85. }
86. 
87. var readFileLineRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^\s{0,12}(\d+)\t(.*)$`)
88. 
89. type numberedBlock struct {
90. 	startLine int
91. 	lines     []string
92. }
93. 
94. type fileLine struct {
95. 	text  string
96. 	start int
97. 	end   int
98. }
99. 
100. func splitFileLines(content string) []fileLine {
101. 	if content == "" {
102. 		return nil
103. 	}
104. 	lines := make([]fileLine, 0, strings.Count(content, "\n")+1)
105. 	start := 0
106. 	for start < len(content) {
107. 		rel := strings.IndexByte(content[start:], '\n')
108. 		if rel < 0 {
109. 			lines = append(lines, fileLine{
110. 				text:  content[start:],
111. 				start: start,
112. 				end:   len(content),
113. 			})
114. 			break
115. 		}
116. 		end := start + rel
117. 		lines = append(lines, fileLine{
118. 			text:  content[start:end],
119. 			start: start,
120. 			end:   end,
121. 		})
122. 		start = end + 1
123. 	}
124. 	return lines
125. }
126. 
127. func parseReadFileNumberedBlock(text string) (numberedBlock, bool) {
128. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
129. 	if len(lines) == 0 {
130. 		return numberedBlock{}, false
131. 	}
132. 
133. 	body := make([]string, len(lines))
134. 	startLine := 0
135. 	for i, line := range lines {
136. 		m := readFileLineRE.FindStringSubmatch(line)
137. 		if m == nil {
138. 			return numberedBlock{}, false
139. 		}
140. 		n := 0
141. 		fmt.Sscanf(m[1], "%d", &n)
142. 		if n <= 0 {
143. 			return numberedBlock{}, false
144. 		}
145. 		if i == 0 {
146. 			startLine = n
147. 		} else if n != startLine+i {
148. 			return numberedBlock{}, false
149. 		}
150. 		body[i] = m[2]
151. 	}
152. 
153. 	return numberedBlock{
154. 		startLine: startLine,
155. 		lines:     body,
156. 	}, true
157. }
158. 
159. func resolveAnchoredCandidate(candidate, oldText string) matchResult {
160. 	if candidate == oldText {
161. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate}
162. 	}
163. 	if normalized := normalizeIndentation(candidate, oldText); normalized == candidate {
164. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "indent-normalized"}
165. 	}
166. 	if strings.Contains(candidate, "\r\n") && !strings.Contains(oldText, "\r\n") {
167. 		if strings.ReplaceAll(oldText, "\n", "\r\n") == candidate {
168. 			return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "crlf-converted"}
169. 		}
170. 	}
171. 	if canonical, shift := tryLeadingIndentShift(candidate, oldText); canonical == candidate {
172. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "leading-indent-shift", shift: shift}
173. 	}
174. 	if trimmed := tryTrailingWhitespaceMatch(candidate, oldText); trimmed == candidate {
175. 		return matchResult{canonical: candidate, transform: "trailing-whitespace-tolerant"}
176. 	}
177. 	return matchResult{}
178. }
179. 
180. func prependTransform(prefix, suffix string) string {
181. 	switch {
182. 	case prefix == "":
183. 		return suffix
184. 	case suffix == "":
185. 		return prefix
186. 	default:
187. 		return prefix + "+" + suffix
188. 	}
189. }
190. 
191. func tryReadFileLineAnchor(content, oldText string) matchResult {
192. 	block, ok := parseReadFileNumberedBlock(oldText)
193. 	if !ok {
194. 		return matchResult{}
195. 	}
196. 
197. 	lines := splitFileLines(content)
198. 	if block.startLine <= 0 || block.startLine+len(block.lines)-1 > len(lines) {
199. 		return matchResult{}
200. 	}
201. 
202. 	startIdx := block.startLine - 1
203. 	endIdx := startIdx + len(block.lines) - 1
204. 	candidate := content[lines[startIdx].start:lines[endIdx].end]
205. 	mr := resolveAnchoredCandidate(candidate, strings.Join(block.lines, "\n"))
206. 	if mr.canonical == "" {
207. 		return matchResult{}
208. 	}
209. 	mr.canonical = candidate
210. 	mr.transform = prependTransform("line-numbers-stripped", mr.transform)
211. 	mr.start = lines[startIdx].start
212. 	mr.anchored = true
213. 	return mr
214. }
215. 
216. // adjustNewText applies the same transform that was used to find old_text
217. // to new_text, so the replacement remains consistent with the file.
218. func adjustNewText(content, newText string, mr matchResult) string {
219. 	out := newText
220. 	if strings.Contains(mr.transform, "read-file-wrapper-stripped") {

239. 
240. // lineNumberPrefixRE matches prefixes produced by readFileRange:
241. // up to 6 leading spaces, then 1+ digits, then a tab.
242. // We allow more leading spaces in case the LLM reformatted slightly.
243. var lineNumberPrefixRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^\s{0,12}\d+\t`)
244. var readFileWrapperLineRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^(?:\[(?:indent:|encoding:|Extracted from |File truncated:|File has |multi_file_read summary)|=== (?:FILE|ERROR): |\[end (?:file|error)\]$|\[skipped:)`)
245. 
246. // stripLineNumberPrefix removes "  42\t" style prefixes if a clear majority
247. // of non-empty lines have them. This catches the common failure where an
248. // LLM pastes back read_file output (which is line-numbered) verbatim as
249. // old_text.
250. func stripLineNumberPrefix(text string) string {
251. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
252. 	matched, nonEmpty := 0, 0
253. 	for _, l := range lines {
254. 		if strings.TrimSpace(l) == "" {
255. 			continue
256. 		}
257. 		nonEmpty++
258. 		if lineNumberPrefixRE.MatchString(l) {
259. 			matched++
260. 		}
261. 	}
262. 	// Require >=2 lines with prefix AND a strong majority, to avoid false
263. 	// positives on legitimate text that happens to start with digits+TAB.
264. 	if matched < 2 || matched < (nonEmpty/2+1) {
265. 		return text
266. 	}
267. 	out := make([]string, len(lines))
268. 	for i, l := range lines {
269. 		out[i] = lineNumberPrefixRE.ReplaceAllString(l, "")
270. 	}
271. 	return strings.Join(out, "\n")
272. }
273. 
274. func stripAllLineNumberPrefixes(text string) string {
275. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
276. 	for i, l := range lines {
277. 		lines[i] = lineNumberPrefixRE.ReplaceAllString(l, "")
278. 	}
279. 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
280. }
281. 
282. func trimReadFileWrapperLines(text string) (string, bool) {
283. 	lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
284. 	start, end := 0, len(lines)
285. 	for start < end && readFileWrapperLineRE.MatchString(lines[start]) {
286. 		start++
287. 	}
288. 	for end > start && readFileWrapperLineRE.MatchString(lines[end-1]) {
289. 		end--
290. 	}
291. 	if start == 0 && end == len(lines) {
292. 		return text, false
293. 	}
294. 	trimmed := strings.Join(lines[start:end], "\n")
295. 	if trimmed == "" {
296. 		return text, false
297. 	}
298. 	return trimmed, true
299. }
300. 
301. // tryCRLFMatch handles the case where the file uses CRLF line endings but
302. // the LLM provided LF-only old_text.
303. func tryCRLFMatch(content, oldText string) string {
304. 	if !strings.Contains(content, "\r\n") {
305. 		return ""
306. 	}
307. 	if strings.Contains(oldText, "\r\n") {
308. 		return ""
309. 	}
310. 	if !strings.Contains(oldText, "\n") {
311. 		return ""
312. 	}
313. 	candidate := strings.ReplaceAll(oldText, "\n", "\r\n")
314. 	if strings.Contains(content, candidate) {
315. 		return candidate
316. 	}
317. 	return ""
318. }
319. 
320. // leadingWhitespace returns the prefix of s that consists of spaces and tabs.
321. func leadingWhitespace(s string) string {
322. 	i := 0
323. 	for i < len(s) && (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t') {
324. 		i++
325. 	}
326. 	return s[:i]
327. }
328. 
329. // tryLeadingIndentShift handles the very common LLM failure where the
330. // model provides the right text but with less (or no) leading indentation
331. // than the file actually uses — e.g. file has "\t\t// foo" but the LLM
332. // supplied just "// foo" as old_text.
333. //
334. // It looks for a contiguous block in content whose lines, after stripping
335. // both leading and trailing whitespace, equal the lines of oldText. To
336. // guard against unrelated false matches it requires:
337. //   - The file's leading whitespace on each non-empty matched line must
338. //     start with the LLM's leading whitespace on the corresponding old
339. //     line (so the shift is well-defined).
340. //   - The "shift" — the extra prefix the file has — must be IDENTICAL on
```

<details>
<summary>31 lines</summary>

```
210. 
211. ## Runtime Permission Modes
212. 
213. | Mode | Behavior |
214. |------|----------|
215. | `supervised` | Default. Respects per-tool rules, asks for unspecified tools |
216. | `plan` | Read-only: allows `read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`; denies writes/commands |
217. | `auto` | Allows safe operations, denies dangerous ones automatically |
218. | `bypass` | Allows almost everything, warns on critical operations |
219. | `autopilot` | Bypass permissions + automatically continues when model asks for input; escalates external blockers to `ask_user` |
220. 
221. ## Built-in Tools
222. 
223. Registered in `internal/tool/builtin.go` (core tools) + `cmd/ggcode/root.go` and `internal/tui/repl.go` (additional tools):
224. 
225. **File operations** (6): `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, `glob`
226. **Execution** (7): `run_command`, `start_command`, `read_command_output`, `wait_command`, `stop_command`, `write_command_input`, `list_commands`
227. **Git** (3): `git_status`, `git_diff`, `git_log`
228. **Web** (2): `web_fetch`, `web_search`
229. **Productivity** (3, in `builtin.go`): `ask_user`, `todo_write` (+ `save_memory` registered separately in `cmd/ggcode/root.go`)
230. **Agent** (3, registered in `internal/tui/repl.go`): `spawn_agent`, `wait_agent`, `list_agents`
231. **MCP** (3, registered in `cmd/ggcode/root.go`): `list_mcp_capabilities`, `get_mcp_prompt`, `read_mcp_resource`
232. **Skill** (1, registered in `cmd/ggcode/root.go`): `skill`
233. 
234. Plus dynamically registered MCP-adapted tools and external plugin tools.
235. 
236. ## Project Memory Files
237. 
238. Loaded at startup by `internal/memory/project.go`:
239. - `GGCODE.md` — primary project conventions (this file)
240. - `AGENTS.md` — agent-specific instructions (used by TeamClaw)
```

</details>

1. package tool
2. 
3. import "github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/permission"
4. 
5. // RegisterBuiltinTools registers all built-in tools.
6. // If policy is nil, no sandbox path checking is enforced (permissive mode).
7. func RegisterBuiltinTools(registry *Registry, policy permission.PermissionPolicy, workingDir string) error {
8. 	sandboxFor := func(toolName string) AllowedPathChecker {
9. 		if policy == nil {
10. 			return nil
11. 		}
12. 		return func(path string) bool {
13. 			return policy.AllowedPathForTool(toolName, path)
14. 		}
15. 	}
16. 	jobManager := NewCommandJobManager(workingDir)
17. 	tools := []Tool{
18. 		// File operations
19. 		ReadFile{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("read_file")},
20. 		MultiFileRead{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("multi_file_read")},
21. 		WriteFile{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("write_file")},
22. 		ListDir{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("list_directory")},
23. 		EditFile{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("edit_file")},
24. 		MultiFileEdit{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("multi_file_edit")},
25. 
26. 		// Search
27. 		SearchFiles{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("search_files")},
28. 		Grep{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("grep")},
29. 		Glob{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("glob")},
30. 	}
31. 	tools = append(tools, NewLSPTools(workingDir, sandboxFor("read_file"), sandboxFor("edit_file"))...)
32. 	tools = append(tools,
33. 
34. 		// Multi-edit and notebook
35. 		MultiEditFile{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("multi_edit_file")},
36. 		NotebookEdit{SandboxCheck: sandboxFor("notebook_edit")},
37. 
38. 		// Sleep
39. 		SleepTool{},
40. 
41. 		// Worktree
42. 		&EnterWorktree{WorkingDir: workingDir},
43. 		&ExitWorktree{WorkingDir: workingDir},
44. 
45. 		// Execution
46. 		&RunCommand{WorkingDir: workingDir, Policy: policy},
47. 		StartCommandTool{Manager: jobManager, Policy: policy},
48. 		ReadCommandOutputTool{Manager: jobManager},
49. 		WaitCommandTool{Manager: jobManager},
50. 		StopCommandTool{Manager: jobManager},
51. 		WriteCommandInputTool{Manager: jobManager},
52. 		ListCommandsTool{Manager: jobManager},
53. 
54. 		// Git (read-only)
55. 		&GitStatus{},
56. 		&GitDiff{},
57. 		&GitLog{},
58. 		&GitShow{},
59. 		&GitBlame{},
60. 		&GitBranchList{},
61. 		&GitRemote{},
62. 		&GitStashList{},
63. 
64. 		// Git (write — require approval)
65. 		&GitAdd{},
66. 		&GitCommit{},
67. 		&GitStash{},
68. 
69. 		// Web
70. 		WebFetch{},
71. 		WebSearch{},
72. 
73. 		// Productivity
74. 		NewAskUserTool(),
75. 		NewWorkspaceTodoWrite(workingDir),
76. 	)
77. 	for _, t := range tools {
78. 		if err := registry.Register(t); err != nil {
79. 			return err
80. 		}
81. 	}
82. 	return nil
83. }
84.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 15510m 2s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

已把这套 **multi-file read/edit** 能力完整接进去了，当前实现已经闭环到工具、权限、agent diff/checkpoint 和文档层。

1. **新工具落地**
   - 新增 `multi_file_read`、`multi_file_edit`
   - `multi_file_read` 复用统一的带行号文本渲染，输出适合弱模型直接复制
   - `multi_file_edit` 默认 **atomic**，支持显式 `partial_success`，结果按设计返回稳定 JSON 字符串

2. **编辑成功率相关优化一并打通**
   - 抽出了共享 helper，单文件/多文件共用同一套 edit planning
   - `multi_file_edit` 直接复用了已有的容错匹配能力：行号锚点、wrapper 剥离、缩进归一、CRLF 容忍、leading-indent-shift 等
   - 额外补了 `multi_file_read` wrapper/分隔符兼容，避免模型把整段读出结果原样贴回时失败

3. **接入层细节补齐**
   - `internal/tool/builtin.go` 已注册新工具
   - `internal/permission` 已支持从 `files[].path` 做批量路径判定，并补齐 `multi_file_read` / `multi_file_edit` 的只读/写入分类
   - `internal/agent/agent_tool.go` 已把 `multi_file_edit` 接到 diff 预览和 checkpoint 保存；部分成功时也只为真正写入成功的文件落 checkpoint
   - 这两个新工具保持 **无 `WorkingDir` 状态**，不会和多 agent 的工作目录同步逻辑互相污染

4. **文档和配置**
   - 设计文档已补全为 read/edit 成对协议与接入要求
   - `GGCODE.md`、`ggcode.example.yaml` 已更新到新工具名和权限示例

另外，`internal/tool`、`internal/permission`、`internal/agent` 都补了对应测试，仓库级 `gofmt` / `go test -tags goolm ./...` / `go vet -tags goolm ./...` / `go build -tags goolm ./...` 已通过。

<sub>⏱️ 15513m 56s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/multi_file_tools.go.**

/Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6/internal/tool/multi_file_tools.go (lines 1-260)

<details>
<summary>260 lines</summary>

```
1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"errors"
7. 	"fmt"
8. 	"os"
9. 	"strings"
10. )
11. 
12. type MultiFileRead struct {
13. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
14. }
15. 
16. func (t MultiFileRead) Name() string { return "multi_file_read" }
17. 
18. func (t MultiFileRead) Description() string {
19. 	return "Read multiple existing files in one call for coordinated edits. " +
20. 		"Use this before multi_file_edit when one task spans several files. " +
21. 		"Output preserves the same numbered-line format as read_file inside deterministic file blocks, so you can copy numbered lines directly into multi_file_edit old_text. " +
22. 		"Keep batches small and use offset/limit on larger files."
23. }
24. 
25. func (t MultiFileRead) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
26. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
27. 	"type": "object",
28. 	"properties": {
29. 		"files": {
30. 			"type": "array",
31. 			"description": "Absolute file paths to read. Keep batches small and use offset/limit to narrow large files.",
32. 			"items": {
33. 				"type": "object",
34. 				"properties": {
35. 					"path": {
36. 						"type": "string",
37. 						"description": "Absolute path to the file to read."
38. 					},
39. 					"offset": {
40. 						"type": "integer",
41. 						"description": "1-based line number to start from. Omit or use 0 to read from the beginning."
42. 					},
43. 					"limit": {
44. 						"type": "integer",
45. 						"description": "Maximum number of lines to read for this file. Omit to use the multi_file_read default cap."
46. 					}
47. 				},
48. 				"required": ["path"]
49. 			}
50. 		},
51. 		"description": {
52. 			"type": "string",
53. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language."
54. 		}
55. 	},
56. 	"required": ["files"]
57. }`)
58. }
59. 
60. func (t MultiFileRead) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
61. 	_ = ctx
62. 	var args struct {
63. 		Files []struct {
64. 			Path   string `json:"path"`
65. 			Offset int    `json:"offset"`
66. 			Limit  int    `json:"limit"`
67. 		} `json:"files"`
68. 	}
69. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
70. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
71. 	}
72. 	if len(args.Files) == 0 {
73. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "files array must not be empty"}, nil
74. 	}
75. 	if len(args.Files) > maxMultiFileReadFiles {
76. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("too many files: got %d, max %d. Split the read into smaller batches.", len(args.Files), maxMultiFileReadFiles)}, nil
77. 	}
78. 
79. 	type readReq struct {
80. 		Path   string
81. 		Offset int
82. 		Limit  int
83. 	}
84. 	reqs := make([]readReq, len(args.Files))
85. 	seen := map[string]struct{}{}
86. 	for i, f := range args.Files {
87. 		path, err := cleanAbsolutePath(f.Path)
88. 		if err != nil {
89. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("files[%d]: %v", i, err)}, nil
90. 		}
91. 		if _, ok := seen[path]; ok {
92. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("files[%d]: duplicate path %s", i, path)}, nil
93. 		}
94. 		seen[path] = struct{}{}
95. 		if f.Offset < 0 {
96. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("files[%d]: offset must be >= 0", i)}, nil
97. 		}
98. 		if f.Limit < 0 {
99. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("files[%d]: limit must be >= 0", i)}, nil
100. 		}
101. 		if f.Limit > maxExplicitMultiFileReadLimit {
102. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("files[%d]: limit %d exceeds max %d. Narrow the range or split the batch.", i, f.Limit, maxExplicitMultiFileReadLimit)}, nil
103. 		}
104. 		reqs[i] = readReq{Path: path, Offset: f.Offset, Limit: f.Limit}
105. 	}
106. 
107. 	var body strings.Builder
108. 	succeeded, failed, skipped := 0, 0, 0
109. 	currentLines, currentBytes := 0, 0
110. 	limitReached := false
111. 	for i, req := range reqs {
112. 		if limitReached {
113. 			body.WriteString(formatMultiFileReadSkippedBlock(req.Path))
114. 			skipped++
115. 			continue
116. 		}
117. 
118. 		var block string
119. 		if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(req.Path) {
120. 			block = formatMultiFileReadErrorBlock(req.Path, "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy")
121. 		} else {
122. 			text, err := readTextContentAtPath(req.Path, req.Offset, req.Limit, readFileRangeOptions{
123. 				defaultLimit: defaultMultiFileReadLimit,
124. 				moreHint:     "Use multi_file_read or read_file with a narrower range for more.",
125. 			})
126. 			if err != nil {
127. 				block = formatMultiFileReadErrorBlock(req.Path, err.Error())
128. 			} else {
129. 				block = formatMultiFileReadFileBlock(req.Path, text)
130. 			}
131. 		}
132. 
133. 		blockLines := strings.Count(block, "\n")
134. 		blockBytes := len(block)
135. 		if currentLines+blockLines > maxMultiFileReadTotalLines || currentBytes+blockBytes > maxMultiFileReadTotalBytes {
136. 			limitReached = true
137. 			for _, remaining := range reqs[i:] {
138. 				body.WriteString(formatMultiFileReadSkippedBlock(remaining.Path))
139. 				skipped++
140. 			}
141. 			break
142. 		}
143. 		body.WriteString(block)
144. 		currentLines += blockLines
145. 		currentBytes += blockBytes
146. 		if strings.HasPrefix(block, "=== ERROR: ") {
147. 			failed++
148. 		} else {
149. 			succeeded++
150. 		}
151. 	}
152. 
153. 	summary := fmt.Sprintf("[multi_file_read summary] requested=%d succeeded=%d failed=%d skipped=%d", len(reqs), succeeded, failed, skipped)
154. 	if skipped > 0 {
155. 		summary += " [split batch or narrow ranges to read skipped files]"
156. 	}
157. 	content := summary
158. 	if body.Len() > 0 {
159. 		content += "\n\n" + strings.TrimSuffix(body.String(), "\n")
160. 	}
161. 	return Result{Content: content, IsError: false}, nil
162. }
163. 
164. type MultiFileEdit struct {
165. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
166. }
167. 
168. func (t MultiFileEdit) Name() string { return "multi_file_edit" }
169. 
170. func (t MultiFileEdit) Description() string {
171. 	return "Apply edits across multiple existing files in one call. " +
172. 		"Best practice: read files first with multi_file_read or read_file, then paste the numbered lines directly into old_text anchors. " +
173. 		"Default mode is atomic: if any file fails to plan, no files are written. Use mode=partial_success only when mixed write outcomes are acceptable. " +
174. 		"Group all edits for the same file into one files[] entry."
175. }
176. 
177. func (t MultiFileEdit) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
178. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
179. 	"type": "object",
180. 	"properties": {
181. 		"mode": {
182. 			"type": "string",
183. 			"enum": ["atomic", "partial_success"],
184. 			"description": "Optional. Defaults to atomic. Atomic writes no files if any file fails to plan. partial_success writes successful files and reports failures separately."
185. 		},
186. 		"files": {
187. 			"type": "array",
188. 			"description": "Files to edit. Each path must be absolute and unique within the request.",
189. 			"items": {
190. 				"type": "object",
191. 				"properties": {
192. 					"path": {
193. 						"type": "string",
194. 						"description": "Absolute path to the file to edit."
195. 					},
196. 					"edits": {
197. 						"type": "array",
198. 						"description": "Edits for this file. These follow the same rules as multi_edit_file.",
199. 						"items": {
200. 							"type": "object",
201. 							"properties": {
202. 								"old_text": {
203. 									"type": "string",
204. 									"description": "Text to find. Recommended: paste the numbered lines directly from multi_file_read/read_file."
205. 								},
206. 								"new_text": {
207. 									"type": "string",
208. 									"description": "Replacement text. Numbered prefixes and multi_file_read/read_file wrapper lines are stripped automatically."
209. 								}
210. 							},
211. 							"required": ["old_text", "new_text"]
212. 						}
213. 					}
214. 				},
215. 				"required": ["path", "edits"]
216. 			}
217. 		},
218. 		"description": {
219. 			"type": "string",
220. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language."
221. 		}
222. 	},
223. 	"required": ["files"]
224. }`)
225. }
226. 
227. func (t MultiFileEdit) PreviewChanges(input json.RawMessage) ([]PlannedFileEdit, error) {
228. 	mode, entries, errRes := t.parseInput(input)
229. 	if errRes != nil {
230. 		return nil, errors.New(errRes.Content)
231. 	}
232. 	plans, _, hasFailures := t.planEntries(entries)
233. 	if mode == "atomic" && hasFailures {
234. 		return nil, nil
235. 	}
236. 	return plans, nil
237. }
238. 
239. func (t MultiFileEdit) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
240. 	_ = ctx
241. 	mode, entries, errRes := t.parseInput(input)
242. 	if errRes != nil {
243. 		return *errRes, nil
244. 	}
245. 	plans, results, hasFailures := t.planEntries(entries)
246. 	byPath := make(map[string]int, len(results))
247. 	for i, r := range results {
248. 		byPath[r.Path] = i
249. 	}
250. 
251. 	out := MultiFileEditContent{
252. 		Mode:         mode,
253. 		PlannedFiles: len(plans),
254. 		Results:      results,
255. 	}
256. 
257. 	if mode == "atomic" && hasFailures {
258. 		for _, plan := range plans {
259. 			idx := byPath[plan.Path]
260. 			out.Results[idx].Status = "planned"
```

</details>

1. # Multi-file read/edit implementation plan
2. 
3. 1. Extract shared file read and single-file edit planning helpers from `read_file` and `multi_edit_file`.
4. 2. Implement `multi_file_read` and `multi_file_edit` with the documented limits, path normalization, and result formats.
5. 3. Wire built-in registration, permission/path extraction, and agent diff/checkpoint handling for `multi_file_edit`.
6. 4. Add tests across `internal/tool`, `internal/permission`, and `internal/agent`, then run gofmt, go test, go vet, and go build with `-tags goolm`.
7.
```

25. func (t ReadFile) Description() string {
26. 	return "Read file contents into the agent's context. ALWAYS read a file with this tool before editing it. " +
27. 		"Output is line-numbered using `cat -n` format (\"   42\\t<line content>\"). Recommended workflow: copy those numbered lines directly into edit_file/multi_edit_file old_text, because the edit tools understand the prefixes and use them as anchors for single-line edits and duplicate text. " +
28. 		"Use offset/limit for files larger than ~2000 lines. " +
29. 		"Supports text, images (png/jpg/gif/webp), PDF, Office (docx/xlsx/pptx), OpenDocument (odt/ods/odp), EPUB, RTF, archives (zip/tar/tar.gz/tar.bz2), iWork, and SVG."
30. }
31. 
32. func (t ReadFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
33. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
34. 	"type": "object",
35. 	"properties": {
36. 		"path": {
37. 			"type": "string",
38. 			"description": "Absolute path to the file to read."
39. 		},
40. 		"offset": {
41. 			"type": "integer",
42. 			"description": "1-based line number to start reading from. Only applies to text files and extracted documents."
43. 		},
44. 		"limit": {
45. 			"type": "integer",
46. 			"description": "Maximum number of lines to read. Output is capped at 2000 lines when no limit is supplied."
47. 		},
48. 		"description": {
49. 			"type": "string",
50. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language."
51. 		}
52. 	},
53. 	"required": [
54. 		"path"
55. 	]
56. }`)
57. }
58. 
59. const maxFileSize = 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10MB pre-check limit
60. 
61. func (t ReadFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
62. 	var args struct {
63. 		Path   string `json:"path"`
64. 		Offset int    `json:"offset"`
65. 		Limit  int    `json:"limit"`
66. 	}
67. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
68. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
69. 	}
70. 
71. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.Path) {
72. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil

82. 	}

88.

91. 		img, err := image.Decode(data)
92. 		if err != nil {
93. 			return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error decoding image: %v", err)}, nil
94. 		}
95. 		b64 := image.EncodeBase64(img)
96. 		return Result{
97. 			Content: image.Placeholder(filepath.Base(args.Path), img),
98. 			Images: []ResultImage{{
99. 				MIME:       img.MIME,
100. 				Base64:     b64,
101. 				Width:      img.Width,
102. 				Height:     img.Height,
103. 				SourcePath: args.Path,
104. 			}},
105. 		}, nil
106. 	}
107. 
108. 	text, err := readTextContentFromBytes(args.Path, data, args.Offset, args.Limit, readFileRangeOptions{
109. 		defaultLimit: maxOutputLines,
110. 		moreHint:     "Use read_file with offset/limit for more.",
111. 	})
112. 	if err != nil {
113. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: err.Error()}, nil
114. 	}
115. 	return Result{Content: text}, nil
116. }

1. package tool
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"os"
8. )
9. 
10. // MultiEditFile applies multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call.
11. type MultiEditFile struct {
12. 	SandboxCheck AllowedPathChecker
13. }
14. 
15. func (t MultiEditFile) Name() string { return "multi_edit_file" }
16. 
17. func (t MultiEditFile) Description() string {
18. 	return "Apply multiple find-and-replace edits to a single file in one call. " +
19. 		"Each edit follows the same rules as edit_file: the best practice is to paste the numbered lines directly from read_file into each old_text, because the tool uses those line numbers as anchors. Without line-number anchors, every old_text must match the file byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings) and must be UNIQUE in the file. " +
20. 		"Edits must not overlap. All edits are applied atomically — if any single edit fails, the file is left unchanged. " +
21. 		"All old_text matches are resolved against the ORIGINAL file, not after earlier edits in the same call. Always read_file first. " +
22. 		"Use this instead of repeated edit_file calls when changing multiple sites in one file (e.g. renaming a symbol)."
23. }
24. 
25. func (t MultiEditFile) Parameters() json.RawMessage {
26. 	return json.RawMessage(`{
27. 	"type": "object",
28. 	"properties": {
29. 		"file_path": {
30. 			"type": "string",
31. 			"description": "Path to the file to edit."
32. 		},
33. 		"edits": {
34. 			"type": "array",
35. 			"items": {
36. 				"type": "object",
37. 				"properties": {
38. 					"old_text": {
39. 						"type": "string",
40. 						"description": "Text to find. Recommended: paste the numbered lines directly from read_file; the tool uses those prefixes as anchors. Without line numbers, old_text must match byte-for-byte (indentation, line endings) and be unique in the file."
41. 					},
42. 					"new_text": {
43. 						"type": "string",
44. 						"description": "Replacement text. If you copied numbered lines from read_file, you may keep or remove those prefixes here; they are stripped automatically."
45. 					}
46. 				},
47. 				"required": [
48. 					"old_text",
49. 					"new_text"
50. 				]
51. 			},
52. 			"description": "Edits to apply, in any order. Each old_text must be unique in the file and not overlap with other edits."
53. 		},
54. 		"description": {
55. 			"type": "string",
56. 			"description": "Optional. Brief activity label shown in the UI in the user's language."
57. 		}
58. 	},
59. 	"required": [
60. 		"file_path",
61. 		"edits"
62. 	]
63. }`)
64. }
65. 
66. func (t MultiEditFile) Execute(ctx context.Context, input json.RawMessage) (Result, error) {
67. 	var args struct {
68. 		FilePath string `json:"file_path"`
69. 		Edits    []struct {
70. 			OldText string `json:"old_text"`
71. 			NewText string `json:"new_text"`
72. 		} `json:"edits"`
73. 	}
74. 	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &args); err != nil {
75. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid input: %v", err)}, nil
76. 	}
77. 
78. 	if len(args.Edits) == 0 {
79. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "edits array must not be empty"}, nil
80. 	}
81. 
82. 	if t.SandboxCheck != nil && !t.SandboxCheck(args.FilePath) {
83. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: "Error: path not allowed by sandbox policy"}, nil
84. 	}
85. 
86. 	data, err := os.ReadFile(args.FilePath)
87. 	if err != nil {
88. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error reading file: %v", err)}, nil
89. 	}
90. 
91. 	content := string(data)
92. 	edits := make([]textEdit, len(args.Edits))
93. 	for i, edit := range args.Edits {
94. 		edits[i] = textEdit{OldText: edit.OldText, NewText: edit.NewText}
95. 	}
96. 	content, _, msg := planTextEdits(content, edits)
97. 	if msg != "" {
98. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: msg}, nil
99. 	}
100. 
101. 	if err := atomicWriteFile(args.FilePath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
102. 		return Result{IsError: true, Content: fmt.Sprintf("error writing file: %v", err)}, nil
103. 	}
104. 
105. 	return Result{Content: fmt.Sprintf("Applied %d edits to %s", len(args.Edits), args.FilePath)}, nil
106. }
107.
```

</details>

Group all edits for the same file into one entry.\",\n \t\t\t\"items\": {\n \t\t\t\t\"type\": \"object\",\n \t\t\t\t\"properties\": {\n \t\t\t\t\t\"path\": {\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\"type\": \"string\",\n-\t\t\t\t\t\t\"description\": \"Absolute path to the file to edit.\"\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"description\": \"Absolute path to the existing file to edit.\"\n \t\t\t\t\t},\n \t\t\t\t\t\"edits\": {\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\"type\": \"array\",\n-\t\t\t\t\t\t\"description\": \"Edits for this file. These follow the same rules as multi_edit_file.\",\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"description\": \"Non-overlapping edits for this file. All old_text matches are resolved against the original file content, not after earlier edits in this same files[] entry.\",\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\"items\": {\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"type\": \"object\",\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"properties\": {\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"old_text\": {\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"type\": \"string\",\n-\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"description\": \"Text to find. Recommended: paste the numbered lines directly from multi_file_read/read_file.\"\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"description\": \"Text to find. Best practice: paste the numbered lines directly from multi_file_read/read_file, including the line-number prefixes. Without those anchors, old_text must still closely match the file content.\"\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"new_text\": {\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"type\": \"string\",\n-\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"description\": \"Replacement text. Implement `multi_file_read` and `multi_file_edit` with the documented limits, path normalization, and result formats.\n 3. <sub>⏱️ 15515m 17s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**还差一点，我已经补强了。** 之前这两个 tool 的定义对强模型基本够用，但对弱模型还不算“无障碍”，主要少了三类关键信号：

1. **推荐调用顺序**不够硬：先 `multi_file_read`，再把带行号的块直接贴进 `multi_file_edit.old_text`
2. **block 格式**没讲透：`=== FILE: ... ===`、`cat -n` 行号、`[end file]` 这些是可直接复制的锚点
3. **原始文件语义**没说清：同一文件内多个 edit 都是基于 **ORIGINAL file content** 匹配，不是前一个 edit 生效后的内容

我已经把这些都补进了 `internal/tool/multi_file_tools.go` 的 `Description()` 和参数 schema 里，包括：
- `multi_file_read` 明确说明输出块格式和“可直接复制带行号内容”
- `multi_file_edit` 明确说明推荐 workflow、同文件合并到一个 `files[]` entry、以及 **original-file matching**
- `old_text` / `new_text` 的字段说明更明确，弱模型更容易正确构造 payload

**结论**：现在比刚才明显更接近“无障碍”了，尤其对会照着 tool schema 组织参数的模型更友好；但如果说 **100% 靠文案就保证所有弱模型都不犯错**，那还是做不到。真正剩下的误用风险主要不在定义文案本身，而在模型是否能稳定拿到**绝对路径**、以及是否会在大改动时仍然坚持先读后改。

---

I'll review the uncommitted changes for cross-platform compatibility issues.Now let me look at the rest of the diff and examine the actual files to understand the context better:Let me check if there's a "tunnel" category defined in the debug package and look at the indentation issue I noticed in the diff:Now let me verify if the debug.Log function can handle unregistered tags and check the actual implementation:Good, the build succeeds. Now let me run the full test suite and check the actual behavior of debug.Log with an unregistered tag:Let me check what happens when debug.Log is called with an unregistered tag and verify the full tunnel.go change:Now let me examine the specific issue in tunnel.go and check if there's a potential bug:Perfect! I found an issue. Let me verify the indentation problems in relay_client.go more carefully:I can see the indentation issues clearly in the octal dump - there are extra tabs. Let me verify if this will cause a build or formatting issue:Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me verify the "tunnel" tag issue one more time by searching all Categories:Based on my review of the uncommitted changes, I've found **two concrete issues**:

## Issue: Incorrect double TrimPrefix logic in handleTunnelCommand
**File:** internal/tui/tunnel.go:28-29
**Severity:** Medium
**Problem:** The sequential TrimPrefix calls create incorrect behavior. When input is "/sharetunnel", it first removes "/share" leaving "tunnel", then removes "/tunnel", leaving an empty string. While "/sharetunnel" isn't a valid command, this pattern is fragile and unexpected. The correct approach is to check which prefix exists, not blindly remove both.
**Evidence:** Verified with test showing `/sharetunnel` produces `args: 'tunnel'` instead of being rejected.
**Suggested fix:** Use a conditional to check which command was used:
```go
var args string
if strings.HasPrefix(text, "/share") {
    args = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/share"))
} else if strings.HasPrefix(text, "/tunnel") {
    args = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/tunnel"))
}
```

## Issue: Incorrect indentation in relay_client.go
**File:** internal/tunnel/relay_client.go:89, 103, 111, 162, 179, 182, 196
**Severity:** Medium
**Problem:** Multiple debug.Log() calls have incorrect indentation (extra tabs). While Go ignores this for compilation, gofmt will change these lines, causing CI/linting failures and creating merge conflicts.
**Evidence:** `gofmt -d` output shows 7 lines with incorrect indentation that need fixing.
**Suggested fix:** Run `gofmt -w internal/tunnel/relay_client.go` to fix all indentation issues automatically.

---

**Note:** The use of the "tunnel" tag in debug.Log() calls is safe - when a tag isn't registered in Categories, the debug system still accepts it but routes it to the main log file only (cat will be empty string, and the message still gets written via mai

1. package debug
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"io/fs"
6. 	"log"
7. 	"os"
8. 	"os/user"
9. 	"path/filepath"

16. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/util"
17. )
18. 
19. func init() {
20. 	// Wire up safego's logger to break the circular import.
21. 	safego.SetLogger(Log)
22. }
23. 
24. var (
25. 	defaultLogDir string // resolved once in Init() via resolveDebugDir()
26. )
27. 
28. const (
29. 	defaultMaxLogSize   int64 = 50 * 1024 * 1024
30. 	defaultMaxLogFiles        = 3
31. 	defaultAsyncBufSize       = 1024
32. 	maxMessageLen             = 4096
33. 	envKey                    = "GGCODE_DEBUG"
34. )
35. 
36. // Category represents a debug log output channel. Each category maps to its
37. // own log file and can be independently enabled via GGCODE_DEBUG_<CATEGORY>.
38. type Category struct {
39. 	Name        string            // e.g. "agent"
40. 	EnvSuffix   string            // e.g. "AGENT" → GGCODE_DEBUG_AGENT=1
41. 	Description string            // human-readable
42. 	Tags        []string          // debug.Log tags routed to this category
43. 	aliases     map[string]string // tag → canonical tag (populated by init)
44. }
45. 
46. // Categories is the ordered list of all debug log categories.
47. // Order matters: listed first = checked first when building tag routing.
48. var Categories = []Category{
49. 	// ── Agent ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

$ cd /Users/zhanju/.openclaw/workspace-teamclaw/projects/https-github-com-topcheer-cc-git-ggcode-coding-age-s7ccb6 && grep -A30 "^func Log" internal/debug/debug.go | head -40

<details>
<summary>37 lines</summary>

```
func Log(pkg, format string, args ...interface{}) {
	mu.RLock()
	if !enabled {
		mu.RUnlock()
		return
	}
	cat := tagToCategory[pkg]
	l := loggers[cat]
	ms := mainSink
	filt := tagFilter
	mu.RUnlock()

	// Apply category filter
	if filt != nil {
		if cat == "" || !filt[cat] {
			return
		}
	}

	msg := fmt.Sprintf("[%s] "+format, append([]interface{}{pkg}, args...)...)
	if len(msg) > maxMessageLen {
		msg = msg[:maxMessageLen]
	}
	msg += "\n"

	// Write to category-specific file
	if l != nil {
		l.Print(msg)
	}

	// Write to main file with timestamp
--

### 💬 Copilot

Based on my review of the uncommitted changes, I've found **two concrete issues**:

## Issue: Incorrect double TrimPrefix logic in handleTunnelCommand
**File:** internal/tui/tunnel.go:28-29
**Severity:** Medium
**Problem:** The sequential TrimPrefix calls create incorrect behavior. When input is "/sharetunnel", it first removes "/share" leaving "tunnel", then removes "/tunnel", leaving an empty string. While "/sharetunnel" isn't a valid command, this pattern is fragile and unexpected. The correct approach is to check which prefix exists, not blindly remove both.
**Evidence:** Verified with test showing `/sharetunnel` produces `args: 'tunnel'` instead of being rejected.
**Suggested fix:** Use a conditional to check which command was used:
```go
var args string
if strings.HasPrefix(text, "/share") {
    args = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/share"))
} else if strings.HasPrefix(text, "/tunnel") {
    args = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/tunnel"))
}
```

## Issue: Incorrect indentation in relay_client.go
**File:** internal/tunnel/relay_client.go:89, 103, 111, 162, 179, 182, 196
**Severity:** Medium
**Problem:** Multiple debug.Log() calls have incorrect indentation (extra tabs). While Go ignores this for compilation, gofmt will change these lines, causing CI/linting failures and creating merge conflicts.
**Evidence:** `gofmt -d` output shows 7 lines with incorrect indentation that need fixing.
**Suggested fix:** Run `gofmt -w internal/tunnel/relay_client.go` to fix all indentation issues automatically.

---

**Note:** The use of the "tunnel" tag in debug.Log() calls is safe - when a tag isn't registered in Categories, the debug system still accepts it but routes it to the main log file only (cat will be empty string, and the message still gets written via mainSink). This is by design for flexibility.


---

<details>
<summary>135 lines</summary>

```
1. package tui
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"context"
5. 	"encoding/json"
6. 	"fmt"
7. 	"strings"
8. 	"time"
9. 
10. 	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
11. 
12. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/tunnel"
13. )
14. 
15. // tunnelStartMsg is sent when the tunnel is ready.
16. type tunnelStartMsg struct {
17. 	info    *tunnel.SessionInfo
18. 	session *tunnel.Session
19. 	broker  *tunnel.Broker
20. 	err     error
21. }
22. 
23. // tunnelStopMsg is sent when the tunnel has stopped.
24. type tunnelStopMsg struct{}
25. 
26. func (m *Model) handleTunnelCommand(text string) tea.Cmd {
27. 	// Accept both /tunnel and /share as prefix
28. 	text = strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/share")
29. 	text = strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/tunnel")
30. 	args := strings.TrimSpace(text)
31. 
32. 	switch args {
33. 	case "stop", "close", "off":
34. 		if m.tunnelSession != nil {
35. 			m.tunnelSession.Stop()
36. 			m.tunnelSession = nil
37. 			m.tunnelBroker = nil
38. 			m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), "Tunnel closed.")
39. 		} else {
40. 			m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), "No active tunnel.")
41. 		}
42. 		return nil
43. 
44. 	case "status":
45. 		if m.tunnelSession == nil {
46. 			m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), "No active tunnel. Use /tunnel to start one.")
47. 		} else {
48. 			info := m.tunnelSession.Info()
49. 			m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), fmt.Sprintf("Relay active:\n  Connect: %s", info.ConnectURL))
50. 		}
51. 		return nil
52. 
53. 	case "", "start", "on":
54. 		if m.tunnelSession != nil {
55. 			info := m.tunnelSession.Info()
56. 			m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), fmt.Sprintf("Tunnel already active:\n  URL: %s", info.ConnectURL))
57. 			return nil
58. 		}
59. 		m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), "Starting tunnel...")
60. 		return m.startTunnel()
61. 
62. 	default:
63. 		m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), "Usage: /tunnel [start|stop|status]")
64. 		return nil
65. 	}
66. }
67. 
68. func (m *Model) startTunnel() tea.Cmd {
69. 	return func() tea.Msg {
70. 		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
71. 		defer cancel()
72. 
73. 		sess := tunnel.NewSession(tunnel.DefaultRelayURL)
74. 		info, err := sess.Start(ctx)
75. 		if err != nil {
76. 			return tunnelStartMsg{err: err}
77. 		}
78. 
79. 		broker := tunnel.NewBroker(sess)
80. 		broker.OnCommand(func(msg tunnel.GatewayMessage) {
81. 			m.handleTunnelClientMessage(msg)
82. 		})
83. 
84. 		return tunnelStartMsg{info: info, session: sess, broker: broker}
85. 	}
86. }
87. 
88. func (m *Model) handleTunnelStartMsg(msg tunnelStartMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
89. 	if msg.err != nil {
90. 		m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), fmt.Sprintf("Tunnel failed: %v", msg.err))
91. 		m.chatListScrollToBottom()
92. 		return m, nil
93. 	}
94. 
95. 	m.tunnelSession = msg.session
96. 	m.tunnelBroker = msg.broker
97. 
98. 	// Open QR overlay with connect URL and QR code.
99. 	m.openQROverlayDirect(
100. 		"Mobile Tunnel",
101. 		"Scan with GGCode Mobile to connect",
102. 		msg.info.QRCode,
103. 		msg.info.ConnectURL,
104. 	)
105. 
106. 	return m, nil
107. }
108. 
109. func (m *Model) handleTunnelClientMessage(msg tunnel.GatewayMessage) {
110. 	switch msg.Type {
111. 	case tunnel.CmdMessage:
112. 		var data tunnel.MessageData
113. 		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg.Data, &data); err != nil {
114. 			return
115. 		}
116. 		// TODO: route to agent as user input
117. 	case tunnel.CmdInterrupt:
118. 		if m.pending != nil {
119. 			m.pending.enqueue("/interrupt")
120. 		}
121. 	case tunnel.CmdApprovalResponse:
122. 		var data tunnel.ApprovalResponseData
123. 		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg.Data, &data); err != nil {
124. 			return
125. 		}
126. 		_ = data // TODO: route to approval handler
127. 	case tunnel.CmdModeChange:
128. 		var data tunnel.ModeChangeData
129. 		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg.Data, &data); err != nil {
130. 			return
131. 		}
132. 		_ = data // TODO: route to mode handler
133. 	}
134. }
135.
```

</details>

14. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/harness"
15. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/memory"
16. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/permission"
17. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/provider"
18. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/safego"
19. 	"time"
20. )
21. 
22. func (m *Model) updateAutoComplete() {
23. 	// Check for slash command
24. 	if active, prefix := DetectSlashCommand(m.input.Value(), inputCursor(&m.input)); active {
25. 		m.refreshCommands()
26. 		matches := CompleteSlashCommand("/"+prefix, m.customCmds)
27. 		if len(matches) > 0 {
28. 			m.autoCompleteActive = true
29. 			m.autoCompleteKind = "slash"
30. 			m.autoCompleteItems = matches
31. 			// Reset index if the filtered list changed
32. 			if m.autoCompleteIndex >= len(matches) {
33. 				m.autoCompleteIndex = 0
34. 			}
35. 			return
36. 		}
37. 	}
38. 
39. 	// Check for @mention
40. 	if active, prefix := DetectMention(m.input.Value(), inputCursor(&m.input)); active {
41. 		workDir, _ := os.Getwd()
42. 		matches := CompleteMention(prefix, workDir)
43. 		if len(matches) > 0 {
44. 			m.autoCompleteActive = true
45. 			m.autoCompleteKind = "mention"
46. 			m.autoCompleteWorkDir = workDir
47. 			m.autoCompleteItems = matches
48. 			if m.autoCompleteIndex >= len(matches) {
49. 				m.autoCompleteIndex = 0
50. 			}
51. 			return
52. 		}
53. 	}
54. 
55. 	// No autocomplete active
56. 	m.autoCompleteActive = false
57. 	m.autoCompleteItems = nil
58. }
59. 
60. func (m *Model) applyAutoComplete() tea.Cmd {
61. 	if m.autoCompleteIndex >= len(m.autoCompleteItems) {
62. 		return nil
63. 	}
64. 	selected := m.autoCompleteItems[m.autoCompleteIndex]
65. 
66. 	value := m.input.Value()
67. 	cursor := inputCursor(&m.input)
68. 
69. 	var replacement string
70. 	if m.autoCompleteKind == "slash" {
71. 		if m.loading {
72. 			if shouldExecuteWhileBusy(selected) {
73. 				m.input.SetValue("")
74. 				m.autoCompleteActive = false
75. 				m.autoCompleteItems = nil
76. 				m.autoCompleteIndex = 0
77. 				m.inputHint = ""
78. 				m.history = append(m.history, selected)
79. 				m.historyIdx = len(m.history)
80. 				return m.handleCommand(selected)
81. 			}
82. 			m.input.SetValue(selected)
83. 			m.autoCompleteActive = false
84. 			m.autoCompleteItems = nil
85. 			m.autoCompleteIndex = 0
86. 			m.inputHint = ""
87. 			return nil
88. 		}
89. 		// Fillable commands: put command in input with placeholder hint.
90. 		// User can press Enter (no args) or type arguments.
91. 		if placeholder, ok := SlashCommandPlaceholders[selected]; ok && placeholder != "" {
92. 			m.input.SetValue(selected + " ")
93. 			m.input.CursorEnd()
94. 			m.inputHint = placeholder
95. 			m.autoCompleteActive = false
96. 			m.autoCompleteItems = nil
97. 			m.autoCompleteIndex = 0
98. 			return nil
99. 		}
100. 		// Non-fillable commands: execute immediately
101. 		m.input.SetValue("")
102. 		m.autoCompleteActive = false
103. 		m.autoCompleteItems = nil
104. 		m.autoCompleteIndex = 0
105. 		m.inputHint = ""
106. 		m.history = append(m.history, selected)
107. 		m.historyIdx = len(m.history)
108. 		return m.handleCommand(selected)
109. 	}
110. 
111. 	if m.autoCompleteKind == "mention" {
112. 		// Replace from the "@" to cursor with the selected path
113. 		atPos := cursor - 1
114. 		for atPos >= 0 && value[atPos] != '@' {
115. 			atPos--
116. 		}
117. 		if strings.HasSuffix(selected, "/") {
118. 			// Directory: no trailing space so user can keep navigating
119. 			replacement = "@" + selected
120. 		} else {
121. 			replacement = "@" + selected + " "
122. 		}
123. 		value = value[:atPos] + replacement + value[cursor:]
124. 	}
125. 
126. 	m.input.SetValue(value)
127. 	m.input.CursorEnd()
128. 
129. 	// For directory mentions, re-trigger autocomplete to show contents
130. 	if m.autoCompleteKind == "mention" && strings.HasSuffix(selected, "/") {
131. 		m.updateAutoComplete()
132. 		return nil
133. 	}
134. 
135. 	m.autoCompleteActive = false
136. 	m.autoCompleteItems = nil
137. 	m.autoCompleteIndex = 0
138. 	return nil
139. }
140. 
141. func (m *Model) submitText(text string, addToHistory bool) tea.Cmd {
142. 	// Notify Knight that user is active (resets idle timer)
143. 	if m.knight != nil && strings.TrimSpace(text) != "" {
144. 		m.knight.NotifyActivity()
145. 	}
146. 	text = m.stripPendingImagePlaceholder(text)
147. 	if addToHistory {
148. 		if text != "" {
149. 			m.history = append(m.history, text)
150. 			m.historyIdx = len(m.history)
151. 		}
152. 	}
153. 	debug.Log("tui", "handleCommand: %s", text)
154. 	return m.handleCommand(text)
155. }
156. 
157. // shouldExecuteWhileBusy returns true for commands that should run immediately
158. // even when the agent is loading (instead of being queued as pending submissions).
159. // Built-in slash commands that only open panels or change settings are safe;
160. // custom commands (which may start a new agent run) and /harness subcommands
161. // (which may start runs) are excluded.
162. func shouldExecuteWhileBusy(text string) bool {
163. 	t := strings.TrimSpace(text)
164. 	if !strings.HasPrefix(t, "/") {
165. 		return false
166. 	}
167. 	parts := strings.Fields(t)
168. 	if len(parts) == 0 {
169. 		return false
170. 	}
171. 	cmd := parts[0]
172. 	switch cmd {
173. 	// Panel / UI commands — always safe
174. 	case "/lang", "/model", "/provider", "/impersonate",
175. 		"/qq", "/telegram", "/tg", "/pc", "/discord",
176. 		"/feishu", "/lark", "/slack", "/dingtalk", "/ding", "/wechat", "/wecom", "/mattermost", "/mm", "/matrix", "/signal", "/irc", "/nostr", "/twitch", "/whatsapp", "/wa", "/im",
177. 		"/skills", "/sessions", "/mcp",
178. 		"/checkpoints", "/memory", "/todo", "/plugins", "/config", "/status",
179. 		"/stream", "/restart", "/help", "/?":
180. 		return true
181. 	// Harness: only the bare command (opens panel) is safe
182. 	case "/harness":
183. 		return len(parts) == 1 || (len(parts) == 2 && strings.EqualFold(parts[1], "panel"))
184. 	}
185. 	return false
186. }
187. 
188. func (m *Model) handleCommand(text string) tea.Cmd {
189. 	if m.knight != nil && strings.TrimSpace(text) != "" {
190. 		m.knight.NotifyActivity()
191. 	}
192. 	if shellCommand, ok := parseShellCommand(text); ok {
193. 		m.setShellMode(true)
194. 		return m.submitShellCommand(shellCommand, true)
195. 	}
196. 
197. 	// Slash commands
198. 	if strings.HasPrefix(text, "/") {
199. 		m.refreshCommands()
200. 		parts := strings.Fields(text)
201. 		cmd := strings.ToLower(parts[0])
202. 		switch cmd {
203. 		case "/exit", "/quit":
204. 			m.quitting = true
205. 			return tea.Quit
206. 		case "/clear":
207. 			m.resetConversationView()
208. 			return nil
209. 		case "/help", "/?":
210. 			m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), m.helpText())
211. 			return nil
212. 		case "/model":
213. 			if len(parts) > 1 {
214. 				if err := m.config.SetActiveSelection(m.config.Vendor, m.config.Endpoint, parts[1]); err == nil {
215. 					if err := m.reloadActiveProvider(); err == nil {
216. 						m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), m.t("command.model_switched", parts[1], m.config.Vendor))
217. 					} else {
218. 						m.chatWriteSystem(nextSystemID(), m.t("command.model_failed", err))
219. 					}
220. 				} else {
```
