```
1. package tui
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"fmt"
5. 	"os"
6. 	"path/filepath"
7. 	"slices"
8. 	"strings"
9. 
10. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/commands"
11. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/debug"
12. )
13. 
14. const (
15. 	mentionPrefix      = "@"
16. 	maxMentionFileSize = 100 * 1024 // 100KB
17. 	maxMentions        = 5
18. )
19. 
20. // Mention represents a parsed @mention reference.
21. type Mention struct {
22. 	Path  string // resolved file/directory path
23. 	IsDir bool
24. }
25. 
26. // ParseMentions extracts @path references from user input.
27. // Returns the cleaned message (with @path removed) and list of mentions.
28. func ParseMentions(input string, workDir string) (string, []Mention, error) {
29. 	var mentions []Mention
30. 	cleaned := input
31. 
32. 	// Find all @path tokens
33. 	for {
34. 		idx := strings.Index(cleaned, mentionPrefix)
35. 		if idx < 0 {
36. 			break
37. 		}
38. 
39. 		// Extract the path token (until whitespace or end)
40. 		rest := cleaned[idx+1:]
41. 		end := strings.IndexAny(rest, " \t\n\r")
42. 		if end < 0 {
43. 			end = len(rest)
44. 		}
45. 		token := rest[:end]
46. 		if token == "" {
47. 			break
48. 		}
49. 
50. 		// Remove from cleaned input
51. 		removeLen := 1 + len(token)
52. 		if end < len(rest) {
53. 			removeLen++ // trailing whitespace
54. 		}
55. 		cleaned = cleaned[:idx] + cleaned[idx+removeLen:]
56. 
57. 		if len(mentions) >= maxMentions {
58. 			// Keep the remaining @mention text in the message
59. 			continue
60. 		}
61. 
62. 		// Resolve path
63. 		fullPath := filepath.Join(workDir, token)
64. 		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(fullPath)
65. 		if err != nil {
66. 			continue
67. 		}
68. 
69. 		// Prevent path traversal: resolved path must stay within workDir
70. 		rel, err := filepath.Rel(workDir, absPath)
71. 		if err != nil || strings.HasPrefix(rel, "..") {
72. 			debug.Log("completion", "@mention path traversal blocked: %s -> %s", token, absPath)
73. 			continue
74. 		}
75. 
76. 		info, err := os.Stat(absPath)
77. 		if err != nil {
78. 			continue
79. 		}
80. 
81. 		mentions = append(mentions, Mention{
82. 			Path:  absPath,
83. 			IsDir: info.IsDir(),
84. 		})
85. 	}
86. 
87. 	cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
88. 	return cleaned, mentions, nil
89. }
90. 
91. // ExpandMentions reads file contents and directory listings for mentions,
92. // returning the expanded message text.
93. func ExpandMentions(input string, workDir string) (string, error) {
94. 	cleaned, mentions, err := ParseMentions(input, workDir)
95. 	if err != nil {
96. 		return input, err
97. 	}
98. 
99. 	if len(mentions) == 0 {
100. 		return input, nil
101. 	}
102. 
103. 	var expanded strings.Builder
104. 	expanded.WriteString(cleaned)
105. 	expanded.WriteString("\n\n[Referenced files]\n")
106. 
107. 	for _, m := range mentions {
108. 		if m.IsDir {
109. 			// List directory contents
110. 			entries, err := os.ReadDir(m.Path)
111. 			if err != nil {
112. 				expanded.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\n@%s (error reading directory: %v)\n", m.Path, err))
113. 				continue
114. 			}
115. 			relPath, _ := filepath.Rel(workDir, m.Path)
116. 			expanded.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\n@%s/ (directory):\n", relPath))
117. 			for _, e := range entries {
118. 				expanded.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  %s\n", e.Name()))
119. 			}
120. 		} else {
121. 			// Read file contents
122. 			info, err := os.Stat(m.Path)
123. 			if err != nil {
124. 				expanded.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\n@%s (error: %v)\n", m.Path, err))
125. 				continue
126. 			}
127. 			if info.Size() > maxMentionFileSize {
128. 				expanded.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\n@%s (skipped: file exceeds %d bytes)\n", m.Path, maxMentionFileSize))
129. 				continue
130. 			}
131. 			data, err := os.ReadFile(m.Path)
132. 			if err != nil {
133. 				expanded.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\n@%s (error reading: %v)\n", m.Path, err))
134. 				continue
135. 			}
136. 			relPath, _ := filepath.Rel(workDir, m.Path)
137. 			expanded.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\n@%s:\n%s\n", relPath, string(data)))
138. 		}
139. 	}
140. 
141. 	return expanded.String(), nil
142. }
143. 
144. // CompleteMention returns file/directory completions for an @mention prefix.
145. // prefix is the text after "@" (e.g., "internal/t" from "@internal/t").
146. func CompleteMention(prefix string, workDir string) []string {
147. 	var dir string
148. 	var partial string
149. 
150. 	if prefix == "" {
151. 		// No prefix: list contents of workDir itself
152. 		dir = workDir
153. 		partial = ""
154. 	} else if strings.HasSuffix(prefix, "/") {
155. 		// Trailing slash: user wants to browse INTO this directory
156. 		dir = filepath.Join(workDir, prefix)
157. 		partial = ""
158. 	} else {
159. 		fullPath := filepath.Join(workDir, prefix)
160. 		dir = filepath.Dir(fullPath)
161. 		partial = filepath.Base(fullPath)
162. 	}
163. 
164. 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
165. 	if err != nil {
166. 		return nil
167. 	}
168. 
169. 	var completions []string
170. 	for _, e := range entries {
171. 		name := e.Name()
172. 		if partial != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(name, partial) {
173. 			continue
174. 		}
175. 		// Skip hidden files (except . files explicitly referenced)
176. 		if strings.HasPrefix(name, ".") && !strings.HasPrefix(partial, ".") {
177. 			continue
178. 		}
179. 		var displayPath string
180. 		if prefix == "" {
181. 			displayPath = name
182. 		} else {
183. 			displayPath = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(prefix), name)
184. 		}
185. 		if e.IsDir() {
186. 			displayPath += "/"
187. 		}
188. 		completions = append(completions, displayPath)
189. 	}
190. 	return completions
191. }
192. 
193. // SlashCommands is the list of all available slash commands.
194. var SlashCommands = []string{
195. 	"/help", "/?", "/sessions", "/resume", "/model", "/provider", "/impersonate",
196. 	"/clear", "/im", "/qq", "/telegram", "/tg", "/pc", "/discord", "/feishu", "/lark", "/slack", "/dingtalk", "/ding", "/wechat", "/wecom", "/mattermost", "/mm", "/matrix", "/signal", "/irc", "/nostr", "/twitch", "/whatsapp", "/wa",

199. 	"/compact", "/todo", "/status", "/knight", "/update", "/restart", "/lang", "/skills", "/stream", "/share", "/tunnel",
200. }
```

```
1. package tunnel
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"encoding/json"
5. 	"fmt"
6. 	"sync"
7. 	"sync/atomic"
8. 	"time"
9. 
10. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/debug"
11. )
12. 
13. // Broker bridges agent events and the WebSocket tunnel protocol.
14. //
15. // Delivery guarantees:
16. //   - Text chunks are batched: PushText appends to a per-msgID buffer;
17. //     a 300ms ticker flushes all accumulated text as a single message.
18. //   - Every outbound message carries a monotonically-increasing Seq.
19. //   - A sender goroutine sends one message at a time and waits for the
20. //     mobile client to ACK (by seq) before sending the next.
21. //   - If ACK times out (5s), the next message is sent anyway.
22. //   - ReplayToClient bypasses ACK flow control for fast reconnect.
23. type Broker struct {
24. 	session         *Session
25. 	onCommand       func(cmd GatewayMessage)
26. 	onClientConnect func()
27. 
28. 	// Sequencing
29. 	nextSeq atomic.Int64
30. 
31. 	// Send queue: all outbound messages go here.
32. 	// The sender goroutine drains it continuously (no ACK blocking).
33. 	// TCP/WebSocket guarantees ordered delivery.
34. 	outMu    sync.Mutex
35. 	outCond  *sync.Cond
36. 	outbound []GatewayMessage // unbounded queue: enqueue never blocks
37. 	outDone  chan struct{}
38. 
39. 	// Text batching
40. 	textMu   sync.Mutex
41. 	textBuf  map[string]*textEntry // msgID → accumulated text entry
42. 	textTick *time.Ticker
43. 	textDone chan struct{} // stop text flusher
44. 
45. 	// Replay log
46. 	logMu   sync.Mutex
47. 	sentLog []GatewayMessage
48. }
49. 
50. func NewBroker(sess *Session) *Broker {
51. 	b := &Broker{
52. 		session:  sess,
53. 		outDone:  make(chan struct{}),
54. 		textBuf:  make(map[string]*textEntry),
55. 		textTick: time.NewTicker(300 * time.Millisecond),
56. 		textDone: make(chan struct{}),
57. 	}
58. 	b.outCond = sync.NewCond(&b.outMu)
59. 
60. 	// Start sender goroutine (ACK flow control)
61. 	go b.senderLoop()
62. 
63. 	// Start text flush ticker
64. 	go b.textFlushLoop()
65. 
66. 	// Handle incoming messages from mobile
67. 	sess.OnMessage(func(msg GatewayMessage) {
68. 		if msg.Type == EventAck {
69. 			return // ACK received, no action needed
70. 		}
71. 		if b.onCommand != nil {
72. 			b.onCommand(msg)
73. 		}
74. 	})
75. 
76. 	sess.OnConnect(func() {
77. 		debug.Log("tunnel", "broker: client connected, replaying %d messages", len(b.sentLog))
78. 		if b.onClientConnect != nil {
79. 			b.onClientConnect()
80. 		}
81. 	})
82. 
83. 	return b
84. }
85. 
86. // ─── Goroutines ───
87. 
88. // textEntry tracks accumulated text for a message stream.
89. // agentID == "" means main agent text; otherwise it's a subagent/teammate.
90. type textEntry struct {
91. 	agentID string
92. 	text    string
93. }
94. 
95. // senderLoop drains the outbound queue. Never blocks the producer.
96. func (b *Broker) senderLoop() {
97. 	for {
98. 		b.outMu.Lock()
99. 		for len(b.outbound) == 0 {
100. 			select {
101. 			case <-b.outDone:
102. 				b.outMu.Unlock()
103. 				return
104. 			default:
105. 			}
106. 			b.outCond.Wait()
107. 		}
108. 		batch := b.outbound
109. 		b.outbound = nil
110. 		b.outMu.Unlock()
111. 
112. 		for _, msg := range batch {
113. 			if err := b.session.Send(msg); err != nil {
114. 				debug.Log("tunnel", "broker: send %s seq=%d failed: %v", msg.Type, msg.Seq, err)
115. 			}
116. 		}
117. 	}
118. }
119. 
120. // textFlushLoop periodically flushes accumulated text buffers to the sendQueue.
121. func (b *Broker) textFlushLoop() {
122. 	for {
123. 		select {
124. 		case <-b.textTick.C:
125. 			b.flushAllText()
126. 		case <-b.textDone:
127. 			return
128. 		}
129. 	}
130. }
131. 
132. // flushAllText sends all accumulated text chunks as a single message per msgID.
133. func (b *Broker) flushAllText() {
134. 	b.textMu.Lock()
135. 	if len(b.textBuf) == 0 {
136. 		b.textMu.Unlock()
137. 		return
138. 	}
139. 	bufs := make(map[string]*textEntry)
140. 	for k, v := range b.textBuf {
141. 		if v.text != "" {
142. 			bufs[k] = v
143. 		}
144. 	}
145. 	for k := range b.textBuf {
146. 		delete(b.textBuf, k)
147. 	}
148. 	b.textMu.Unlock()
149. 
150. 	for msgID, entry := range bufs {
151. 		seq := b.nextSeq.Add(1)
152. 		if entry.agentID != "" {
153. 			data, _ := json.Marshal(SubagentTextData{AgentID: entry.agentID, ID: msgID, Chunk: entry.text})
154. 			msg := GatewayMessage{Seq: seq, Type: EventSubagentText, Data: data}
155. 			b.recordLog(msg)
156. 			b.enqueueOut(msg)
157. 		} else {
158. 			data, _ := json.Marshal(TextData{ID: msgID, Chunk: entry.text})
159. 			msg := GatewayMessage{Seq: seq, Type: EventText, Data: data}
160. 			b.recordLog(msg)
161. 			b.enqueueOut(msg)
162. 		}
163. 	}
164. }
165. 
166. // flushText flushes the buffer for a specific msgID immediately.
167. func (b *Broker) flushText(msgID string) {
168. 	b.textMu.Lock()
169. 	entry := b.textBuf[msgID]
170. 	if entry == nil || entry.text == "" {
171. 		b.textMu.Unlock()
172. 		return
173. 	}
174. 	delete(b.textBuf, msgID)
175. 	b.textMu.Unlock()
176. 
177. 	seq := b.nextSeq.Add(1)
178. 	if entry.agentID != "" {
179. 		data, _ := json.Marshal(SubagentTextData{AgentID: entry.agentID, ID: msgID, Chunk: entry.text})
180. 		msg := GatewayMessage{Seq: seq, Type: EventSubagentText, Data: data}
181. 		b.recordLog(msg)
182. 		b.enqueueOut(msg)
183. 	} else {
184. 		data, _ := json.Marshal(TextData{ID: msgID, Chunk: entry.text})
185. 		msg := GatewayMessage{Seq: seq, Type: EventText, Data: data}
186. 		b.recordLog(msg)
187. 		b.enqueueOut(msg)
188. 	}
189. }
190. 
191. // ─── Lifecycle ───
192. 
193. func (b *Broker) OnCommand(fn func(cmd GatewayMessage)) {
194. 	b.onCommand = fn
195. }
196. 
197. func (b *Broker) OnClientConnect(fn func()) {
198. 	b.onClientConnect = fn
199. }
200. 
201. func (b *Broker) Stop() {
202. 	b.textTick.Stop()
203. 	close(b.textDone)
204. 	close(b.outDone)
205. 	b.outCond.Broadcast()
206. }
207. 
208. // ReplayToClient resends all logged messages, bypassing ACK flow control.
209. func (b *Broker) ReplayToClient() {
210. 	b.logMu.Lock()
211. 	msgs := make([]GatewayMessage, len(b.sentLog))
212. 	copy(msgs, b.sentLog)
213. 	b.logMu.Unlock()
214. 
215. 	debug.Log("tunnel", "broker: ReplayToClient: %d messages", len(msgs))
216. 	// Always start with chat_clear
217. 	clearMsg := GatewayMessage{Type: "chat_clear"}
218. 	if err := b.session.Send(clearMsg); err != nil {
219. 		debug.Log("tunnel", "broker: replay chat_clear failed: %v", err)
220. 		return
221. 	}
222. 	for _, msg := range msgs {
223. 		if err := b.session.Send(msg); err != nil {
224. 			debug.Log("tunnel", "broker: replay send %s failed: %v", msg.Type, err)
225. 			return
226. 		}
227. 	}
228. }
229. 
230. // ─── Session lifecycle ───
231. 
232. func (b *Broker) SendSessionInfo(data SessionInfoData) {
233. 	b.enqueue(EventSessionInfo, data)
234. }
235. 
236. func (b *Broker) PushChatClear() {
237. 	b.logMu.Lock()
238. 	b.sentLog = nil
239. 	b.logMu.Unlock()
240. 	// Clear text buffers too
241. 	b.textMu.Lock()
242. 	b.textBuf = make(map[string]*textEntry)
243. 	b.textMu.Unlock()
244. 	b.enqueue("chat_clear", nil)
245. }
246. 
247. func (b *Broker) PushSharingStopped() {
248. 	b.enqueue("sharing_stopped", nil)
249. }
250. 
251. // ─── User message ───
252. 
253. func (b *Broker) PushUserMessage(text string) {
254. 	b.enqueue(EventUserMessage, map[string]string{"text": text})
255. }
256. 
257. // ─── Streaming text (batched) ───
258. 
259. func (b *Broker) PushText(id, chunk string) {
260. 	b.textMu.Lock()
```

```
1. package tunnel
2. 
3. import (
4. 	"encoding/json"
5. 	"fmt"
6. 	"io"
7. 	"net/http"
8. 	"strings"
9. 	"sync"
10. 	"time"
11. 
12. 	"github.com/gorilla/websocket"
13. 
14. 	"github.com/topcheer/ggcode/internal/debug"
15. )
16. 
17. // RelayClient connects to the ggcode-relay server as the "server" role.
18. // It auto-reconnects on disconnect with exponential backoff.
19. type RelayClient struct {
20. 	relayURL string
21. 	token    string
22. 	crypto   *Crypto
23. 
24. 	conn    *websocket.Conn
25. 	connMu  sync.Mutex
26. 	sendCh  chan []byte
27. 	closed  bool
28. 	closeMu sync.Mutex
29. 	stopCh  chan struct{}
30. 
31. 	onMessage func(msg GatewayMessage)
32. 	onConnect func()
33. 	mu        sync.RWMutex
34. }
35. 
36. func NewRelayClient(relayURL, token string) (*RelayClient, error) {
37. 	crypto, err := NewCrypto(token)
38. 	if err != nil {
39. 		return nil, err
40. 	}
41. 	return &RelayClient{
42. 		relayURL: strings.TrimSuffix(relayURL, "/"),
43. 		token:    token,
44. 		crypto:   crypto,
45. 		sendCh:   make(chan []byte, 256),
46. 		stopCh:   make(chan struct{}),
47. 	}, nil
48. }
49. 
50. // Connect starts the connection loop. It connects, runs pumps, and auto-reconnects.
51. func (rc *RelayClient) Connect() error {
52. 	if err := rc.dial(); err != nil {
53. 		return err
54. 	}
55. 	go rc.run()
56. 	return nil
57. }
58. 
59. func (rc *RelayClient) dial() error {
60. 	url := fmt.Sprintf("%s/ws?role=server&token=%s", rc.relayURL, rc.token)
61. 	conn, _, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.Dial(url, http.Header{})
62. 	if err != nil {
63. 		return fmt.Errorf("relay dial: %w", err)
64. 	}
65. 	rc.conn = conn
66. 	return nil
67. }
68. 
69. func (rc *RelayClient) run() {
70. 	for {
71. 		done := make(chan struct{})
72. 		var once sync.Once
73. 		closeDone := func() { once.Do(func() { close(done) }) }
74. 
75. 		go rc.writePump(closeDone)
76. 		go rc.readPump(closeDone)
77. 
78. 		<-done // Wait for either pump to exit
79. 		rc.conn.Close()
80. 
81. 		rc.closeMu.Lock()
82. 		if rc.closed {
83. 			rc.closeMu.Unlock()
84. 			return
85. 		}
86. 		rc.closeMu.Unlock()
87. 
88. 		// Reconnect with backoff
89. 			debug.Log("tunnel", "relay-client: disconnected, reconnecting...")
90. 		for attempt := 0; ; attempt++ {
91. 			rc.closeMu.Lock()
92. 			if rc.closed {
93. 				rc.closeMu.Unlock()
94. 				return
95. 			}
96. 			rc.closeMu.Unlock()
97. 
98. 			if err := rc.dial(); err != nil {
99. 				backoff := time.Duration(attempt+1) * time.Second
100. 				if backoff > 10*time.Second {
101. 					backoff = 10 * time.Second
102. 				}
103. 					debug.Log("tunnel", "relay-client: reconnect failed (attempt %d): %v, retry in %v", attempt+1, err, backoff)
104. 				select {
105. 				case <-time.After(backoff):
106. 					continue
107. 				case <-rc.stopCh:
108. 					return
109. 				}
110. 			}
111. 				debug.Log("tunnel", "relay-client: reconnected")
112. 			break
113. 		}
114. 	}
115. }
116. 
117. func (rc *RelayClient) writePump(done func()) {
118. 	defer done()
119. 	pingMsg, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]string{"type": "ping"})
120. 	ticker := time.NewTicker(30 * time.Second)
121. 	defer ticker.Stop()
122. 
123. 	for {
124. 		select {
125. 		case msg, ok := <-rc.sendCh:
126. 			if !ok {
127. 				return
128. 			}
129. 			rc.connMu.Lock()
130. 			err := rc.conn.WriteMessage(websocket.TextMessage, msg)
131. 			rc.connMu.Unlock()
132. 			if err != nil {
133. 				return
134. 			}
135. 		case <-ticker.C:
136. 			rc.connMu.Lock()
137. 			err := rc.conn.WriteMessage(websocket.TextMessage, pingMsg)
138. 			rc.connMu.Unlock()
139. 			if err != nil {
140. 				return
141. 			}
142. 		case <-rc.stopCh:
143. 			return
144. 		}
145. 	}
146. }
147. 
148. func (rc *RelayClient) readPump(done func()) {
149. 	defer done()
150. 
151. 	rc.conn.SetReadLimit(1 << 20)
152. 	rc.conn.SetPongHandler(func(string) error {
153. 		rc.conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(300 * time.Second))
154. 		return nil
155. 	})
156. 
157. 	for {
158. 		rc.conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(300 * time.Second))
159. 		_, raw, err := rc.conn.ReadMessage()
160. 		if err != nil {
161. 			if err != io.EOF {
162. 					debug.Log("tunnel", "relay-client: read error: %v", err)
163. 			}
164. 			return
165. 		}
166. 
167. 		var relayMsg struct {
168. 			Type       string `json:"type"`
169. 			Nonce      string `json:"nonce,omitempty"`
170. 			Ciphertext string `json:"ciphertext,omitempty"`
171. 			Role       string `json:"role,omitempty"`
172. 		}
173. 		if json.Unmarshal(raw, &relayMsg) != nil {
174. 			continue
175. 		}
176. 
177. 		switch relayMsg.Type {
178. 		case "connected":
179. 				debug.Log("tunnel", "relay-client: confirmed as %s", relayMsg.Role)
180. 
181. 		case "client_joined":
182. 				debug.Log("tunnel", "relay-client: mobile client joined")
183. 			rc.mu.RLock()
184. 			fn := rc.onConnect
185. 			rc.mu.RUnlock()
186. 			if fn != nil {
187. 				fn()
188. 			}
189. 
190. 		case "pong":
191. 			// keepalive
192. 
193. 		case "encrypted":
194. 			plaintext, err := rc.crypto.Decrypt(relayMsg.Nonce, relayMsg.Ciphertext)
195. 			if err != nil {
196. 					debug.Log("tunnel", "relay-client: decrypt error: %v", err)
197. 				continue
198. 			}
199. 			var msg GatewayMessage
200. 			if json.Unmarshal(plaintext, &msg) != nil {
201. 				continue
202. 			}
203. 			rc.mu.RLock()
204. 			fn := rc.onMessage
205. 			rc.mu.RUnlock()
206. 			if fn != nil {
207. 				fn(msg)
208. 			}
209. 		}
210. 	}
211. }
212. 
213. // Send encrypts and sends a GatewayMessage to the relay.
214. // Safe to call after Close — returns error instead of panicking.
215. func (rc *RelayClient) Send(msg GatewayMessage) error {
216. 	rc.closeMu.Lock()
217. 	if rc.closed {
218. 		rc.closeMu.Unlock()
219. 		return fmt.Errorf("relay client closed")
220. 	}
221. 	rc.closeMu.Unlock()
222. 
223. 	plaintext, err := json.Marshal(msg)
224. 	if err != nil {
225. 		return err
226. 	}
227. 	nonce, ciphertext, err := rc.crypto.Encrypt(plaintext)
228. 	if err != nil {
229. 		return err
230. 	}
231. 
232. 	relayMsg := map[string]string{
233. 		"type":       "encrypted",
234. 		"nonce":      nonce,
235. 		"ciphertext": ciphertext,
236. 	}
237. 	data, err := json.Marshal(relayMsg)
238. 	if err != nil {
239. 		return err
240. 	}
241. 
242. 	select {
243. 	case rc.sendCh <- data:
244. 		return nil
245. 	default:
246. 		return fmt.Errorf("send channel full")
247. 	}
248. }
249. 
250. func (rc *RelayClient) OnMessage(fn func(msg GatewayMessage)) {
251. 	rc.mu.Lock()
252. 	defer rc.mu.Unlock()
253. 	rc.onMessage = fn
254. }
255. 
256. func (rc *RelayClient) OnConnect(fn func()) {
257. 	rc.mu.Lock()
258. 	defer rc.mu.Unlock()
259. 	rc.onConnect = fn
260. }
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 15587m 3s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**还剩 2 个点，其他这波 Windows/Linux 兼容性改动看起来已经比较干净了。**

1. **`internal/tui/tunnel.go:27-30`**
   现在是连续做两次 `TrimPrefix`：
   ```go
   text = strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/share")
   text = strings.TrimPrefix(text, "/tunnel")
   ```
   这会把前缀解析写得比较脆，虽然正常 `/share`、`/tunnel` 能用，但这种写法会误处理某些非预期输入，最好改成 `HasPrefix` + 分支，只裁掉实际命中的那个命令前缀。

2. **`internal/tunnel/relay_client.go:89,103,111,162,179,182,196`**
   这里几处 `debug.Log(...)` 的缩进不对，`gofmt` 会改。功能上没问题，但如果 CI 里有 `gofmt` cleanliness 检查，这会直接挂掉。这个属于确定性的遗留。

**除此之外，我没看到新的明显跨平台遗留：**
- `broker.go` 从 `log.Printf` 切到 `debug.Log` 方向是对的，避免直接往终端打日志影响 TUI，Windows/Linux 都更稳。
- `/share` 在 `commands.go`、`completion.go` 的注册和补全也基本补齐了。
- `debug.Log("tunnel", ...)` 即使没有单独 category，也不会造成运行时问题。
